# 3.8 Link Adaptation

## 3.8.1 Common

### 3.8.1.0-4 Common Selection of the 256QAM TBS table (ID: 11124470)

**3.8.1.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `12739503`)

Common The R&D parameter rdSelect256QamTbsTable (see 5G_L2_1127_replaced([11186317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_274uh_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) allows the stable configuration of the parameter mcsTable for test purposes. The value 1 selects permanently the 64QAM MCS index table 1 for PDSCH defined by 38.214 and the value 2 selects permanently the 256QAM index table 2 for PDSCH defined by 3GPP 38.214. The value 0 doesn't affect the parameter mcsTable. The R&D parameter is [Before CB008475-A] only [End CB008475-A] relevant for FR1 bands below 6GHz [CB008475-A] and FR2 bands [End CB008475-A] .

---

### 3.8.1.0-5 Common Supported MCS/PRB combinations (up to 24R2) (ID: 11124480)

**3.8.1.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11124489`)

Common [Before CNI-103796-AA]

The supported MCS/PRB combinations can be seen in 5G_UP_ALG_4624_replaced([11192116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvivWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for DL PDSCH and 5G_UP_ALG_5091_replaced([11203399](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKgfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for UL PUSCH.

TBS of the MCS/PRB combinations differ depending on which TBS table is selected for the allocation. 3GPP specifies two tables, one TBS table for MCSes up to 64QAM and the other TBS table for MCSes up to 256QAM. The TBS table for MCSes up to 256QAM can be only applied to UEs, which are 256QAM capable. Depending on the selected TBS table the size of the transport blocks is different. Details about the TBS tables can be found in 5G_UP_ALG_4635_replaced([11192394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvkkgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The link adaptation doesn’t use the MCS10 and MCS17 for UEs for which the MCS table up to 64QAM is applied.

For test purpose, some parameters of the TBS calculation are able to be set to fixed values, see 5G_L2_1888_replaced([11215143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-2i__vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: In early development, the link adaptation in DL and UL selects a limited set of MCS/PRB combinations. This set of MCS/PRB combinations is noted in below. And this set of MCS/PRB combinations will become outdated if L1 support all MCS/PRB combinations in coming future:

PUSCH

  * MCS0 with 64, 256 PRBs
  * MCS11, MCS18, MCS20 for 64PRB, and 256PRB allocations
  * MCS0 with 16, 32, 128 PRBs
  * MCS1 to MCS28 for 64PRB, and 256PRB allocations
  * In addition, MCS0, MCS11, MCS18 and MCS20 for 66 and 273PRB allocations
  * In addition, MCS1 to MCS28 for 66 and 273PRB allocations
  * In addition, MCS0 to MCS28 for 162 PRB allocations
  * In addition, MCS0 to MCS28 for 106 PRB allocations
  * In addition, MCS0 to MCS28 for 217 PRB allocations
  * In addition, MCS0 with 48 PRB allocations
  * In addition, MCS0 with 14 PRB allocations
  * In addition, MCS0 with 3, 4, 8, 24 PRB allocations



PDSCH

  * MCS0 with 64, 256 PRBs
  * MCS11, MCS18, MCS20 for 64PRB, and 256PRB allocations
  * MCS0-9 for 24 and 48 PRBs.
  * MCS1 to MCS28 for 64PRB, and 256PRB allocations
  * In addition, MCS0, MCS11, MCS18 and MCS20 for 66 and 273PRB allocations
  * In addition, MCS1 to MCS28 for 66 and 273PRB allocations
  * MCS0 to MCS28 for 162 PRB allocations
  * MCS0 to MCS28 for 106 PRB allocations
  * MCS0 to MCS28 for 217 PRB allocations



For FDD, supported configurations can be found in

  * 5G_UP_IF_1906_replaced([11515776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUPmafv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)). No link adaptation based on CQI is supported. Instead, static MCS setting per PDU type according 5GC000425 or BLER based link adaptation according 5GC000517 is used.
  * 5G_UP_IF_1906_replaced([11515776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUPmafv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for DL and 5G_UP_IF_2270_replaced([11518563](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUqdJ_v1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for UL. Link adaptation (inner and outer loop) according 5GC000517 is supported.



[End CNI-103796-AA]

---

### 3.8.1.0-6 Common Supported MCS (ID: 11124495)

**3.8.1.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11124500`)

Common L2PS shall maintain the list of available MCSes based on supported indexes in L1, as described in:

  * 5G_UP_ALG_260_replaced([11096385](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmv_s_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'MCS overview' for DL
  * 5G_UP_ALG_1004_replaced([11103430](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knveNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ‘Decoder configuration’ for UL



with exclusion of MCS10 and MCS17 from 64QAM mapping table.

Note:

No gain has been identified with the use of MCS10 and MCS17, as the corresponding top lower-order MCSes (i.e.: MCS9 and MCS16) guarantee a more robust transmission compared to the bottom higher-order MCSes (i.e.: MCS10 and MCS17) with almost the same spectral efficiency.

---

### 3.8.1.0-7 Common Activation/deactivation of functional entities in link adaptation (ID: 11124506)

**3.8.1.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11124510`)

Common The activation/deactivation of functional entities within the link adaptation shall facilitate the verification of the implementation. For this purpose, R&D or NIDD parameters shall be implemented, which control the conditions of ILLA and OLLA for UL and DL, or enhanced LA functions respectively. The following set of parameters are requested:

  * rdActIllaDl (see 5G_L2_1128_replaced([11185029](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27u9jvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): Set to 0 deactivates the ILLA in DL. With deactivation of the ILLA the average CQI rrmAvgCqi (see 5G_UP_ALG_3005_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is fix set to a certain value, which is specified by the R&D parameter rdAvgCqi (see 5G_L2_1132_replaced([11185052](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27vkkvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The exponential filter for calculating the average CQI is bypassed (see 5G_UP_ALG_3005_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and all captured CQI reports don’t influence the average CQI parameter rrmAvgCqi. Set to 1 (default setting) activates the DL ILLA.  


  * rdActOllaDl (see 5G_L2_1129_replaced([11185041](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27vkkPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): Set to 0 deactivates the OLLA in DL. With deactivation of the OLLA delta CQI (rrmDeltaCqi) is set to 0 (see 5G_UP_ALG_3060_replaced([11129299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Set to 1 (default setting) activates the OLLA and the OLLA shall be initialized during UE context setup with the carrier specific delta CQI value.

  * rdActIllaUl (see 5G_L2_1130_replaced([11185035](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27u9j_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): Set to 0 deactivates the ILLA in UL. With deactivation of the ILLA the average SINR rrmAvgSinr (see 5G_UP_ALG_3078_replaced([11130719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krANl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is fix set to a certain value, which is specified by the R&D parameter rdAvgSinr (see 5G_L2_1133_replaced([11185056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27vkk_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The exponential filter for calculating the average SINR is bypassed (see 5G_UP_ALG_3078_replaced([11130719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krANl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and all captured SINR measurements don’t influence the average SINR parameter rrmAvgSinr. Set to 1 (default setting) activates the UL ILLA.  


  * rdActOllaUl (see 5G_L2_1131_replaced([11185047](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27vkkfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): Set to 0 deactivates the OLLA in UL. With deactivation of the OLLA delta SINR (rrmDeltaSinr) is set to 0 (see 5G_UP_ALG_3153_replaced([11137866](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcScfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Set to 1 (default setting) activates the OLLA and the OLLA shall be initialized during UE context setup with the carrier specific delta SINR value.




rdActNackFloodDefense (see 5F_L2_1173): Set to 1 activates the HARQ NACK Flood defence mechanism for DL traffic (see 5G_UP_ALG_3068_replaced([11129535](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq8jN_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as well as for UL traffic (see 5G_UP_ALG_3158_replaced([11138143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krdglfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

NIDD NRBTS.actEnhancedLinkAdaptation:

  * Early DLLA mode:



The Early DLLA mode isn’t any longer controlled by the NIDD parameter NRBTS.actEnhancedLinkAdaptation.

  * Update of iniDeltaCqi and iniDeltaSinr:



Set to 1 allows the LA to not feed the initialization parameters of the OLLA in exceptional cases (see 5G_UP_ALG_3060_replaced([11129299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_3153_replaced([11137866](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcScfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

  * Update of the HARQ NACK flood algorithm:



Set to 1 changes the algorithm to detect the pause state for the NACK Flood defence mechanism (DL/UL, see 5G_UP_ALG_3068_replaced([11129535](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq8jN_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/ 5G_UP_ALG_3158_replaced([11138143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krdglfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

  * Fallback of SINR parameter for Rank1 transmissions:



Set to 1 adds a fallback mechanism from SRS- to PUSCH-SINR measurements if SRS returns DTX (see 5G_UP_ALG_3078_replaced([11130719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krANl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: A change of the NIDD parameter setting shall be applied only for newly instantiated UEs.

Editor’s note: rdAvgCqi and rdAvgSinr configurations, which lead to either MCS10 or MCS17 shall be prevented for test related to the 64QAM MCS table.

Editor’s note: The initial DL MCS defined by the NIDD parameter NRCELL.dllaIniMcs is set if rdActIllaDl = 0 and rdActOllaDl = 0. The initial UL MCS defined by the NIDD parameter NRCELL.ullaIniMcs is set if rdActIllaUl = 0 and rdActOllaUl = 0.

The initial DL MSC 9 shall be used in common config when only the SRB1 is re-established instead of value defined by the NIDD parameter NRCELL.dllaIniMcs.

---

### 3.8.1.0-8 Common Calculation of effective code rate (ID: 11124515)

**3.8.1.0-8.0-7**  (ID: `12705059`)

Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common 12705059: Common The effective code rate is defined as the number of information bits (including CRC bits) divided by the number of physical channel bits.

The number of the information bits = The determined TBS + size of CRC. The size of CRC bits = the size of TB CRC bits (see 5G_UP_IF_1279_replaced([11510984](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tTdjSfv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) + the size of CRC bits for each code block * number of code blocks (see 5G_UP_IF_1289_replaced([11511104](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tTexZ_v1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The number of physical channel bits [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $Q_m \times (N_{PRB} \times N_{RE} - N_{RE}^{PTRS} - N_{RE}^{TRS} - N_{RE}^{CRS} - N_{RE}^{CSI} - IM - N_{RE}^{RateMatched} - N_{RE}^{PDCCH-RM}) \times V$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7045.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

where,

\- [image-1][image_desc]The image contains the text "NPRB" with a subscript "N". The letters are slightly blurred and have a chromatic aberration effect, with blue and orange fringes around the edges. The text is set against a black background.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7045.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] is the allocated PRBs;

-[image-2][image_desc]The image contains the text "Qm" in a stylized font. The "Q" is large and appears to be a light blue color with orange and yellow highlights, giving it a somewhat metallic or glowing effect. The "m" is smaller and positioned to the right of and slightly below the "Q". It is also rendered in a similar color scheme, with orange and yellow hues. The text is set against a white background and is enclosed within a black border.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7045.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] is the modulation order;

The text is positioned in the center of the image.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7045.014.png[/image_path][/image-14]is the number of LTE CRS which needs to withdrawn on the flexible area and is equal to:

\- 0 for slot #5

\- 4 for slot #0 and 2 LTE CRS ports (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2201_replaced([11325202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tsrfu_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- 8 for slot #0 and 4 LTE CRS ports (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2201_replaced([11325202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tsrfu_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- 2 for slot #0 and 1 LTE CRS port (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2201_replaced([11325202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tsrfu_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_7622_replaced([11325223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TsrfvvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[CB007372-M]   
For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true), in certain configuration of top/bottom LTE alignment and LTE/NR BWs, NR PRB overlapping LTE DC may contain less LTE CRS to withdrawn. This will be described at EFS level.  
For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) the PRBs with Flexible Rate Matching are in the range [lowestPrbOfLtePbchPss..highestPrbOfLtePbchPss]. The parameters lowestPrbOfLtePbchPss and highestPrbOfLtePbchPss are received from the C-Plane ([12702622: Calculation of RateMatchPattern PRBs for LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YkJIMl4kEfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))   
[End CB007372-M]

\- [image-15][image_desc]The image contains the text "N^P_RE PDCCH-RM". The text is in a dark color against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7045.015.png[/image_path][/image-15]**is the number of Rate-Matched REs in PDCCH symbol(s). If NRCELL.actPdcchSharingWithPdsch is set to False, [image-16][image_desc]The image contains the text "N^P_RE PDCCH-RM". The text is in a dark color against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7045.016.png[/image_path][/image-16]** is equal to 0. If NRCELL.actPdcchSharingWithPdsch is set to True, [image-17][image_desc]The image contains the text "N^P_RE PDCCH-RM". The text is in a dark color against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7045.016.png[/image_path][/image-17] shall be calculated from the REs in PDCCH symbol that are rate matched in PDSCH allocation.****

\- [image-18][image_desc]The image is a close-up of a stylized letter "V" with a gradient of colors. The left side of the "V" is a deep blue, transitioning to a lighter blue and then to a warm orange-brown at the bottom. The right side of the "V" is primarily orange-brown, with hints of blue and yellow mixed in. The overall effect is a smooth, blended gradient that gives the letter a sense of depth and dimension. The edges of the "V" are sharp and well-defined, with a subtle white outline that makes it stand out against a white background. The image is well-lit and in focus, with no visible artifacts or distortions.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7045.017.png[/image_path][/image-18] is the number of layers.

The effective code rate shall be smaller than or equal to maximal effective code rate. If the effective code rate with proposed MCS exceeds the maximum code rate limit, a MCS downgrade is performed so that the effective code rate is smaller than or equal to the maximum effective code rate.**Where maximal effective code rate equals to 0.95 for PDSCH and equals to rdMaxPuschEffectiveCodeRate (see 5G_L2_1431_replaced([11184925](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27uWfPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for PUSCH.**

The code rate condition has to be evaluated again for the UL if UCI is transferred via PUSCH. The supplementary CR evaluation is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_5111_replaced([11203602](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note:

  * while calculation the number of physical channel bits, 5G-L2-PS shall consider the overlap of:

  * PTRS REs

  * TRS REs
  * ZP CSI-RS (CSI-IM) REs
  * ZP CSI-RS (TRS) configured on TRS symbol to puncture all of subcarriers
  * PDCCH rate matched REs
  * [PR875696] LTE CRS REs in case of NRCELL.actAsymmetricDss='true' [End PR875696]  
which means 5G-L2-PS only need to reduce once for the number of REs overlapped with other type from the list above within the allocated PRBs.



Note 2: for detailed information about PDSCH grants see [11364174: MCS re-determination](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udIJyPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

### 3.8.1.0-9 Common Consider ReTx for OLLA (ID: 11124858)

**3.8.1.0-9.0-4**  (ID: `13470297`)

Common [Before CB013712-A] If rdActConsiderReTxForUlOlla set to 1 (true) and the cell is in TDD mode and L2 RT is on ABIL or ASOD and more than 1 cell is configured with UL in the L2 PS instance

CRC feedback for PUSCH retransmissions shall be considered in the UL OLLA, see 5G_UP_ALG_3153_replaced([11137866](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcScfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

rrmActConsiderRetxForUlOlla = 1

ElseIf rdActConsiderReTxForUlOlla set to 2 (testmode) and the cell is in TDD mode and L2 RT is on ABIL or ASOD

rrmActConsiderRetxForUlOlla = 1

[End CB013712-A] 

ElseIf **[Before 03895468-25R1]NRCELL.tmpActFeat2.bit30**[03895468-25R1]NRCELL.actUlLaOllaForRetxInDft**[End 03895468-25R1] is set to True (1) and the cell is in TDD mode,** ****

If UE is configured to be scheduled by DFT-s-OFDM

rrmActConsiderReTxForUlOlla = 1 (UL LA OLLA shall evaluate also the CRC feedback of a PUSCH retransmission)

Else

rrmActConsiderReTxForUlOlla = 0 (UL LA OLLA shall not evaluate the CRC feedback of a PUSCH retransmission because the UE is scheduled by CP)

EndIf

Else

rrmActConsiderRetxForUlOlla = 0

Endif

Note that setup of 2. cell with UL configuration in the L2 PS instance will change the UL OLLA behaviour at run time for the first established cell.

rdACtConsiderReTxForUlOlla is defined in 5G_L2_1313_replaced([11185169](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27wLpvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that the check if there is more than 1 cell configured with UL in the L2 PS instance can be done by checking for each cell in all configured cell groups if there is a non-empty list of uplink subcells, i.e., while ReTX were not considered for UL OLLA when only single cell with UL is in service, consideration will start after having second cell in service.

Such configurations are supported based on the baseband block features 5GC001958 (FR1), 5GC001955 / 5GC001956 / 5GC001968 (FR2).

configured (5G_UP_ALG_4722_replaced([11172097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJoPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS needs to calculate the interpolated spectrum efficiency.

The interpolated spectrum efficiency [image-0][image_desc]The image contains the text "Qinterpolate" in a blurry, low-resolution format. The text is white with a slight blue tint and is set against a white background. The letters are somewhat indistinct due to the blurriness.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7052.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] shall be calculated by using linear extrapolation/interpolation algorithm described below:

If the MCS is the MCS28 in 38.214 table 5.1.3.1-1/5.1.3.1-3 or MCS27 in 38.214 table 5.1.3.1-2 [CB008949-A]or MCS26 in 38.214 table MCS5.1.3.1-4[End CB008949-A], then

[image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in white text against a black background. The formula is: $Q_{interpolate} = Q_{mcs-1} + \frac{(Q_{mcs} - Q_{mcs-1})}{(CQI_{mcs} - CQI_{mcs-1})} \times (rrmCorrAvgCqi - CQI_{mcs-1})$ The formula appears to be related to some form of interpolation, possibly in the context of signal processing or communication systems, given the variables like $Q$ (likely representing a quality metric) and $CQI$ (Channel Quality Indicator). The term $rrmCorrAvgCqi$ might represent an average correlation value.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7052.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

otherwise

[image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a sans-serif font. The formula is: $Q_{interpolate} = Q_{mcs} + (Q_{mcs+1} - Q_{mcs}) / (CQI_{mcs+1} - CQI_{mcs}) * (rrmCorrAvgCqi - CQI_{mcs})$ The formula appears to be related to interpolation, possibly in the context of signal processing or data analysis, given the notation $Q$ and $CQI$ which might represent quality or channel information, and $mcs$ which could refer to modulation and coding scheme. The term $rrmCorrAvgCqi$ suggests a correlation or average of CQI values.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7052.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]

Where

MCS is the MCS obtained according to rrmCorrAvgCqiInTrs, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[image-3][image_desc]The image contains the text "Q.m.c.s." in a blurry, colorful font. ### 3.8.1.1 Number of Downlink non zero power &amp; zero power CSI-RS REs to provide 5G-L1-DL

#### 3.8.1.1.0-1 Number of Downlink non zero power &amp; zero power CSI-RS REs to provide 5G-L1-DL Calculation of numCsirsMappingRes (up to 24R2) / numRateMatchingRes (from 24R3) (ID: 11124985)

**3.8.1.1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11125006`)

Number of Downlink non zero power & zero power CSI-RS REs to provide 5G-L1-DL For FR1 FDD and TDD, when 5G-L2-PS schedule the PDSCH in TRS slot or SSB slot with TRS configuration, 5G-L2-PS shall calculate the number of downlink CSI-RS for TRS REs in PDSCH PRBs according to TRS configuration saved (provided by Control Plane during Cell Setup procedure) and shall provide its value to 5G-L1-DL for each PDSCH grant.

**[Before CB008409-A, CB012269-A]**

For FR1 FDD DSS, 5G-L2-PS shall calculate the number of downlink ZP CSI-RS (CSI-IM) REs in PDSCH PRBs according the ZP CSI-RS configuration provided by the Control Plane in the cell setup procedure (for CSI-IM pattern 0, 4 REs per RB where CSI-IM is scheduled) and shall provide its value to 5G-L1-DL for each PDSCH grant.

**[CB008409-A, CB012269-A]**

gNB DU shall calculate the number of downlink ZP CSI-RS (CSI-IM, **[CB012269-A] TRS**[End CB012269-A]) REs in PDSCH PRBs according to the ZP CSI-RS configuration provided by the higher layer.****

**[End CB008409-A, CB012269-A]**

If NRCELL.actPdcchSharingWithPdsch is set to TRUE, gNB-DU shall calculate the number of PDSCH REs that overlap the DCI occupied REs in PDCCH symbol in the slot and shall provide the value to 5G-L1-DL.

Notes:

  * The rate matching for non zero power (nzp) CSI-RS is not applicable for PDSCH scrambled with a DCI 1_0 or a DCI 1_1 for a UE without nzp CSI-RS configured.

  * The rate matching for zero power (zp) CSI-RS is not applicable for PDSCH scrambled with a DCI 1_0 or DCI 1_1 for a UE without zp CSI-RS configured.

  * To schedule a C-RNTI with rate matching for the [nzp,zp]CSI-RS, the U-Plane must have PDCCH and PDSCH resources and the UE must be added in the CS1 list (see [11174627: UEs selection for CS1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). 




**[Before CNI-103796]- CSI-IM is not planned to be scheduled in TRS slots.**[CB008409-A]This rule is not applied for the TRS slots in which CSI-IM is scheduled in symbols other than CSI-RS for channel acquisition, in a FDD non-DSS cell.**[End CB008409-A] [End CNI-103796]** ****

  * DL PTRS is disabled for FR1 TDD and FDD

---

#### 3.8.1.1.0-2 Number of Downlink non zero power &amp; zero power CSI-RS REs to provide 5G-L1-DL Saving REs for CSI-RS for Tracking (ID: 11125012)

**3.8.1.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11125023`)

Number of Downlink non zero power & zero power CSI-RS REs to provide 5G-L1-DL L2-PS will receive from CP-RT in Cell Setup Request message a flag _csirsTrackingEnable set to True (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_3594_replaced([11302779](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqhjnPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if the CSI-RS rate matching (CSI-RS for Tracking) for PDSCH is activated._

Please refer for details how to populate the table (numCsirsTrRes) to 5G_L2_57_replaced([11141810](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23q85PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Notes:

  * The rate matching for non zero power (nzp) CSI-RS is not applicable for PDSCH scrambled with a DCI 1_0 or a DCI 1_1 for a UE without nzp CSI-RS configured.

  * To schedule a C-RNTI with rate matching for the [nzp,zp]CSI-RS, the U-Plane must have PDCCH and PDSCH resources and the UE must be added in the CS1 list (see [11174627: UEs selection for CS1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). 




**[Before CB009784-A]**

  * PTRS is disabled for CSI-RS rate matching for PDSCH DSS.



**[CB009784-A]**

  * PTRS is disabled for CSI-RS rate matching for FR1 TDD and FDD.



**[End CB009784-A]**

This requirement shall also **applicable to TDD FR1.**

---

#### 3.8.1.1.0-3 Number of Downlink non zero power &amp; zero power CSI-RS REs to provide 5G-L1-DL Counting PDSCH REs which overlap CSI-RS for Tracking REs when CSI-RS Rate Matching is activated (ID: 11125029)

**3.8.1.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11125041`)

Number of Downlink non zero power & zero power CSI-RS REs to provide 5G-L1-DL If needed (see csirsTrackingEnable received in the Cell Setup Request message) L2-PS shall calculate the number of REs which cannot be used due to CSI-RS mapping ([Before CB008370-AA]numCsirsMappingRes [CB008370-AA] numRateMatchingRes [End CB008370-AA] IE) and provide this number to 5G-L1-DL inside the PDSCH Send procedure scrambled by a C-RNTI.

A) For CSI-RS for Tracking:

5G-L2-PS shall count all PDSCH REs which overlap CSI-RS for tracking REs in the current frame and slot. CSI-RS for tracking REs are only available in frames with sfn MOD( csirsTrackingPeriod / 10 ) = 0. In such frames, only the slots given in table numCsirsTrRes (see 5G_L2_57_replaced([11141810](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23q85PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) contain CSI-RS for tracking REs. In those slots, a PDSCH RE is overlapping a CSI-RS for tracking RE if it is located on the same PRB and on the same OFDM symbol as the CSI-RS for tracking RE. [Before CB008370-AA]numCsirsMappingRes [CB008370-AA] numRateMatchingRes [End CB008370-AA] shall be set to the number of overlapping REs. See 5G_L2_885_replaced([11178264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26_WrfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the details.

B) For CSI-RS for Acquisition: (only in FDD mode)

C) 3) for FDD mode only:

Note: the number of DMRS REs (according to the numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData IE in DlData_PdschSendReq 5G_UP_ALG_238_replaced([11096279](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmuKivvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_241_replaced([11096295](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmuxkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_245_replaced([11096320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmuxmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not included in numCsirsMappingRes IE computation.

Applicable to TDD FR1.

---

##### 3.8.1.2 Number of Downlink LTE CRS REs and LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH REs to provide 5G-L1-DL

###### 3.8.1.2-1 Calculation of number of ratematched REs

###### 3.8.1.2-1.0-1 Calculation of number of ratematched REs The DRB which is associated with drbPdschLaProfile profile(NRBTS/NRDRB.nrPdschHRLaDN) shall be regarded as DL high reliability DRB. The DRB which is associated with drbPuschLaProfile profile(NRBTS/NRDRB.nrPuschHRLaDN) shall be regarded as UL high reliability DRB. For either DL or UL UE is regarded as working in BR (Basic Reliability) mode as default when L2-PS doesn't know whether UE is in HR (High Reliability) mode or BR (Basic Reliability) mode during the initial duration. [CB008246-D]For either DL or UL with the support of switching between HR(high reliability) mode which UE is 64qamLowSE capable, UE can work in HR(high reliability) BLER and thus can be in HR(high reliability) mode,  while UE can also work in BR(basic reliability) BLER and thus can be in BR mode, thus the configured HR DRB can work as either HR(high reliability) mode or BR(basic reliability) mode dynamically depending on UE is working on HR(high reliability) mode or BR(basic reliability) mode. [End CB008246-D] [CB008246-G]For either DL or UL with the support of switching between HR(high reliability) mode which UE is not 64qamLowSE capable, UE can work in HR BLER and thus can be in HR(high reliability) mode,  while UE can also work in BR(basic reliability) BLER and thus can be in BR(basic reliability) mode, thus the configured HR DRB can work as either HR(high reliability) mode or BR(basic reliability) mode dynamically depending on UE is working on HR(high reliability) mode or BR(basic reliability) mode. [End CB008246-G] (ID: 11125157)

###### 3.8.1.2-2 Calculation of number of ratematched REs

###### 3.8.1.2-2.0-3 Calculation of number of ratematched REs If at least one DRB of the UE is configured as High Reliable (DRB links to NRBTS/NRPDSCHHRLA.nrPdschHRLaDN or NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaDN), L2-PS shall apply High Reliable (HR) Link Adaptation in the corresponding direction for all the SRBs and DRBs of the UE. The monitoring of the DL/UL radio channel can trigger a switch (in the corresponding direction) from HR Link Adaptation to Basic Reliable (BR) Link Adaptation or vice versa for all the SRBs and DRBs, according to the algorithm described in 5G_UP_ALG_16320_replaced( 11129978 ), 5G_UP_ALG_16423_replaced( 11130006 ), 5G_UP_ALG_16426_replaced( 11138318 ), 5G_UP_ALG_16077_replaced( 11138358 ). Upon receiving the user setup message for a UE, if it is first DRB(s) associated to NRBTS/NRPDSCHHRLA.nrPdschHRLaDN or NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaDN configured for the UE, L2PS shall enable the high reliability DL/UL Link adaptation respectively for this UE.[CB008148-B-UPD0005] Exception: keep Basic reliability UL Link adaptation if UE starts with TP On state per ( 11140543 ) [End CB008148-B-UPD0005] While one or more DRB(s) with NRBTS/NRPDSCHHRLA.nrPdschHRLaDN or NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaDN is added to a UE which at least has one DRB linked to NRBTS/NRPDSCHHRLA.nrPdschHRLaDN or NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaDN, L2PS shall follow the UE current LA work mode for DL/UL respectively. When one or more DRB(s) including at least one DRB without NRBTS/NRPDSCHHRLA.nrPdschHRLaDN or NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaDN are added to a UE which has a DRB linked to NRBTS/NRPDSCHHRLA.nrPdschHRLaDN or NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaDN, L2PS shall follow the UE current LA work mode for DL/UL respectively. When one or more DRB(s) including at least one DRB with NRBTS/NRPDSCHHRLA.nrPdschHRLaDN or NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaDN are added to a UE which does not has a DRB linked to NRBTS/NRPDSCHHRLA.nrPdschHRLaDN or NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaDN, L2PS shall enable the high reliability Link adaptation. While high reliability Link adapation is enabled, all the DRB(s) without NRBTS/NRPDSCHHRLA.nrPdschHRLaDN or NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaDN shall be scheduled with same LA work mode as HR DRBs. If UE are configured with multiple HR DRBs which links to more than 1 NRBTS/PDSCHHRLA, the profile with smallest dllaBlerTargetHR shall be selected to use. If the smallest dllaBlerTargetHR is same mapped to different profiles, L2PS shall select the profile based on the lowest NRBTS/NRPDSCHHRLA.nrPdschHRLaId. If UE are configured with multiple HR DRBs which links to more than 1 NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA, the profile with smallest ullaBlerTargetHR shall be selected to use. If the smallest ullaBlerTargetHR is same mapped to different profiles, L2PS shall select the profile based on the lowest NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaId. If at least one 5QI-1/65/66 DRB is configured or UE is configured with configured grant and mcs-Table in configuredGrantConfig is set to 'qam256', this UE can only work in basic reliability mode.** When UE is in [Before CB008148-B-UPD0005]Transform Precoder Enabled state (5G_UP_ALG_3270_replaced( 11140467 ) and 5G_UP_ALG_3274_replaced( 11140586 )) in Uplink[CB008148-B-UPD0005] TP On state (Transform Precoder enabled per 11140467 ) for PUSCH with DCI 0_1, [End CB008148-B-UPD0005] it shall be in BR(basic reliability) mode in Uplink. it shall be in BR(basic reliability) mode in Uplink. If at least one DRB of the UE is associated HR NRPUSCHHRLA (NRBTS/NRDRB.nrPuschHRLaDN configured) and CG Type2 (NRULCG_PROFILE) is configured and mcs-Table in configuredGrantConfig is not set to 'qam256' and if CG Type 2 is activated for this UE, UE shall be in CG-HR (Configured Grant-High Reliability) mode for CG service.** If none of DRBs of the UE is associated HR NRPUSCHHRLA (NRBTS/NRDRB.nrPuschHRLaDN configured) and CG Type2 (NRULCG_PROFILE) is configured and if CG Type 2 is activated for this UE, UE shall be in CG-BR (Configured Grant-Basic Reliability) mode for CG service. (ID: 13440305)

###### 3.8.1.2-3 Calculation of number of ratematched REs

###### 3.8.1.2-3.0-1 Calculation of number of ratematched REs When UE operates in basic reliability mode in LA, UEs DRB can be configured with PDSCH BR profile and/or PUSCH BR profile which specifies BLER target and CQI/SINR stepdown. The DRB which is associated with drbPdschLaProfile profile(NRBTS/NRDRB.nrPdschBRLaDN) shall be regarded as DL DRB with service aware BLER profile for basic reliability. The DRB which is associated with drbPuschLaProfile profile(NRBTS/NRDRB.nrPuschBRLaDN) shall be regarded as UL DRB with service aware BLER profile for basic reliability. Along with the existing conditions, DRB specific BLER target and CQI/SINR stepdown profile shall also impact on BLER target and CQI/SINR selection. Refer 5G_UP_ALG_26677_replaced( 11130068 ) for DL and 5G_UP_ALG_16024_replaced( 11138277 ) for more details. (ID: 11125187)

###### 3.8.1.2-4 Calculation of number of ratematched REs

Calculation of number of ratematched REs 12679152: Calculation of number of ratematched REs The coefficient applied for the corrected average CQI in slots where the PDSCH are rate matched is computed for the same RB mapping (assumption that the PDSCH PRBs are rate matched in the same way for all the PRBs) and defined as the number of REs for a PDSCH rate matched in a RB divided by the number of REs for a PDSCH without rate matching in a RB avgCqiRatio = (  [image-0][image_desc]The image contains the text "N PDSCH RF". ###### 3.8.1.2.0-1 Number of Downlink LTE CRS REs and LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH REs to provide 5G-L1-DL Number of Downlink LTE CRS REs and LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH REs to provide 5G-L1-DL- For FDD-DSS (ID: 11125053)

**3.8.1.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12677059`)

Number of Downlink LTE CRS REs and LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH REs to provide 5G-L1-DL For FDD DSS:

L2-PS will receive from CP-RT in Cell Setup Request message the _lteCrsMappingStruct IE with the flag_ isLteCrsMappingEnable set to True when DSS is activated with LTE CRS mapping.**

For LTE CRS mapping, see also 5G-L1-DL requirements: 5G_UP_ALG_2989_replaced([11125047](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsEjvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) LTE CRS.

If the flag _isLteCrsMappingEnable is set to true in Cell Setup Request message, 5G-L2-PS shall compute and store in a table the number of LTE CRS REs per RB and per symbol._

In Cell Setup Request message, several IEs are needed to compute the number of LTE CRS REs (base on the RRC IE _lte-CRS-ToMatchAround in 3GPP 38.214 §5.1.4.2):_

  * the number of LTE CRS ports provided by _numLteCrsPorts IE (see 36.211 §6.10.1.2)._

  * the parameter _reLteNuShift which is equal to the shift IE (36.211 §6.10.1.2 )._




[CB007372-AA] - for asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) the lteDlCenterFrequencyScOffset (the offset between LTE DC frequency and NR ARFCN) [End CB007372-AA]

Note: the values {1,2,4} for _numLteCrsPorts IE are supported in higher layers and at LTE side._

  * the LTE Carrier Bandwidth: the number of RBs is provided by _lteDlBandwidth IE._

|   
---|---  
LTE Carrier Bandwidth (MHz) | lteDlBandwidth (RBs)  
20 MHz (FDD) | 100  
15 MHz (FDD) | 75  
10 MHz (FDD) | 50  
5 MHz (FDD) | 25  
  
  * the Nr DL Carrier Bandwidth: the number of RBs is provided by dlBandwidth IE:



[CB007372-AA] For symmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false) [End CB007372-AA] 

|   
---|---  
Carrier Bandwidth (MHz) | dlBandwidth (RBs)  
20 MHz (FDD) | 106  
15 MHz (FDD) | 79  
10 MHz (FDD) | 52  
5 MHz (FDD) | 25  
  
[CB007372-AA] For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) the NR DL Carrier Bandwidth shall be larger than LTE Carrier Bandwidth. Supported NR DL Carrier Bandwidths:

|   
---|---  
Carrier Bandwidth (MHz) | dlBandwidth (RBs)  
50 MHz (FDD) | 270  
40 MHz (FDD) | 216  
35 MHz (FDD) | 188  
30 MHz (FDD) | 160  
25 MHz (FDD) | 133  
20 MHz (FDD) | 106  
15 MHz (FDD) | 79  
  
**[End CB007372-AA]**

For the actual computation defined in 5G_L2_60_replaced([11141837](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23q86PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), it is assumed that [CB007372-AA] for symmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false) [End CB007372-AA] the LTE Dc frequency and Nr ARFCN frequency are identical.

---

###### 3.8.1.2.0-2 Number of Downlink LTE CRS REs and LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH REs to provide 5G-L1-DL Filling CRS rate matching information in DlData_PdschSendReq messages (ID: 11125063)

**3.8.1.2.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `12705062`)

Number of Downlink LTE CRS REs and LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH REs to provide 5G-L1-DL For FDD DSS:

If NRCELL.lteNrDssMode >= "DL_CRS_TDM_UL_FDM", L2 PS shall provide information about CRS rate matching to L1. Thus, for symmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false), L2 PS shall set the parameter _lteCrsOption to 1 (LTECRSMAPPING) in all DlData_PdschSendReq messages sent to 5G-L1-DL with exception of_

If slowPrbPoolingFddFr1EnhAllowed = FALSE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_7785_replaced([11278788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNePvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) AND if NSA DSS 10Mhz boths side blanking configuration is NOT being used (see 5G_UP_ALG_4716_replaced([11171969](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJBIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) 

  * Msg2, where the parameter _lteCrsOption is set to 2 (LTECRSPUNC);_



Msg2 is scheduled using DCI format 1_0 with coreset(0) therefore _lteCrsOption 4,5,6 shall not be applied for such transmissions._

Else

  * Msg2, where the parameter _lteCrsOption is set to 0 (NOLTECRS);_



End If

  * Msg4, where the parameter lteCrsOption is set to 0 (NOLTECRS);
  * SIB1, OSI, paging, where the parameter lteCrsOption is set to 0 (NOLTECRS);
  * PDSCH to UEs in DSS transient phase (5G_L2_142_replaced([11143994](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_233KLvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), where the parameter lteCrsOption is set to 2 (LTECRSPUNC)



SIB1/OSI/Paging/Random Access and PDSCH to UEs in DSS transient phase (5G_L2_142_replaced([11143994](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_233KLvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are scheduled using DCI format 1_0 with coreset(0) therefore _lteCrsOption 4,5,6 shall not be applied for such transmissions._

For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) the L2 PS shall set the parameter _lteCrsOption to 1 (LTECRSMAPPING) [CB007372-M], or other values defined below for Flexible Rate Matching, [End CB007372-M] in all DlData_PdschSendReq messages sent to 5G-L1-DL if_ numLteCrsMappingRes is greater than zero. All transmission scheduled using DCI format 1_0 shall be within NR dedicated area and they should have  _lteCrsOption set to 0 (NOLTECRS)._ **

When the DL PRB sharing in subframe 0/5/9 is enabled with the flexible rate matching isLteFlexibleRateMatchingEnabled set to TRUE (5G_L2_IF_122_replaced([10969219](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UbPTPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) dynamic spectrum sharing structure content of Cell Setup Request message) and UE is configured by L3 to support of Flexible Rate matching (UE indicates rateMatchingResrcSetSemi-Static IE as supported 5G_CP_IF_RRC_6204_replaced([11374757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wa-L8fvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB007372-M] For symmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M] L2-PS shall set the parameter lteCrsOption according to rule below if PDSCH grant is assigned for subframe 0 or 5 and any of allocated PRB is within the range specified below:

1) set to 4 (LTEFLEXMATCHPBCH)

· For 20 MHz, for PRBs in the range [50..56]

· For 15 MHz, for PRBs in the range [36..42]

· For 10 MHz, for PRBs in the range [23..29]

2) 5 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSS)

· For 20 MHz, for PRBs in the range [50..56]

· For 15 MHz, for PRBs in the range [36..42]

· For 10 MHz, for PRBs in the range [23..29]

3) 6 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSSALT)

· For 20 MHz, for PRBs in the range [50..55]

· For 15 MHz, for PRBs in the range [36..42]

· For 10 MHz, for PRBs in the range [23..28]

[CB007372-M] For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) L2-PS shall set the parameter lteCrsOption to:  
4 (LTEFLEXMATCHPBCH) in subframe 0 or to 5 (LTEFLEXMATCHPSS) in subframe 5 if PDSCH grant is assigned in subframe 0 or 5 and any of allocated PRB is within the range [lowestPrbOfLtePbchPss..highestPrbOfLtePbchPss].  
Parameters lowestPrbOfLtePbchPss and highestPrbOfLtePbchPss are received from the C-Plane ([12702622: Calculation of RateMatchPattern PRBs for LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YkJIMl4kEfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) .

[End CB007372-M]

Furthermore, L2-PS shall fill the _numLteCrsMappingRes IE in order to provide the number of LTE CRS [CB007372-M], PBCH, PSS, SSS [End CB007372-M] of this PDSCH grant (see 5G_UP_ALG_2996_replaced([11125095](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))._

For each PDSCH grant, L2-PS will need to know

  * location of allocated PRBs if NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true 

  * the number of PDSCH symbols (see _numOfPdschSymbols IE in 5G_UP_ALG_194_replaced([11096024](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmrHM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_198_replaced([11096047](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmrHO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_201_replaced([11096055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmrHPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))_

  * the start symbol of PDSCH (see _startSymbol IE in 5G_UP_ALG_184_replaced([11095973](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmqgIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_188_replaced([11096004](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmqgLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_191_replaced([11096013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmqgL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))_

  * the number of RBs (see 5G_UP_ALG_217_replaced([11096139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmsVWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_220_replaced([11096153](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kms8YfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_223_replaced([11096162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kms8ZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))_, it could be RA type 0 or type 1)_




Note1: lteCrsOption is set 1 (LTECRSMAPPING) in case PDSCH is scheduled for a UE **outside of transient phase in an MBSFN slot. There are no CRS in such slot to avoid, but the UE does not know. For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) the NOLTECRS is used for DCI 1_1 if all PRBs are in NR dedicated area.**

Note2: [Before PR876662] In MBSFN slots, where NOLTECRS is used, then symbols 12 and 13 are used for transmission. [End PR876662] For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) the NOLTECRS is used for DCI 1_0 (transmissions in NR dedicated area) and the NOLTECRS is used for DCI 1_1 if all PRBs are in NR dedicated area [PR876662] (no LTE rate matching is needed) and NRCELL.dlDMRSAdditionalPosition = 'No additional DMRS'.  
If NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true and NRCELL.dlDMRSAdditionalPosition = 'One additional DMRS' the lteCrsOption=3 (ADDDMRSSHIFT) is used for DCI 1_1 if all PRBs are in NR dedicated area and no LTE rate matching is needed. With ADDDMRSSHIFT the additional DMRS is shifted to symbol 12 when there is no LTE CRS rate matching. The additional DMRS is in symbol 12 for UEs configured with LTE CRS rate matching (see 3GPP 38.211 §7.4.1.1.2) so for all UEs in DSS cell.  
[End PR876662]

---

###### 3.8.1.2.1 Calculation of number of ratematched REs

###### 3.8.1.2.1-1 Calculation of number of ratematched REs

###### 3.8.1.2.1-1.0-2 Calculation of number of ratematched REs For FDD DSS: Note: For DSS [CB007372-AA] with NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-AA], the following assumptions are done: the 2 first symbols are reserved for LTE PDCCH and the Nr PDCCH is on the 3rd symbol . =&gt;  startSymbol = 3 and numOfPdschSymbols = 11 (for PDSCH type mode A). [CB007372-AA] Note2: For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true), the following assumptions are done: the 2 first symbols are reserved for LTE PDCCH in LTE shared area and for NR PDCCH in NR dedicated area ( 12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS ). [CB007372-B] - the 3rd symbol may be used for NR PDCCH in both LTE shared and NR dedicated areas [End CB007372-B] for PDSCH type mode A: =&gt;  startSymbol = 2 and numOfPdschSymbols = 12 or [CB007372-B] =&gt;  startSymbol = 3 and numOfPdschSymbols = 11 [End CB007372-B] [End CB007372-AA] assumption: PTRS is disabled for this PDSCH. Note: the number of DMRS REs (according to the numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData IE in DlData_PdschSendReq 5G_UP_ALG_238_replaced( 11096279 ), 5G_UP_ALG_241_replaced( 11096295 ) and 5G_UP_ALG_245_replaced( 11096320 )) is not included in *numLteCrsMappingRes IE computation. * Note: There is no need to have the result per symbols and PRB, the table has one dimension the PRB. (ID: 12677094)

number of ratematched REs 11125115: Calculation of number of ratematched REs For FDD DSS: If the RB allocation is contiguous (rat0Bitmap IE equal to 0 in 5G_UP_ALG_204_replaced([11096067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmruQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS computes numLteCrsMappingRes with the number of RBs from the startPrb to (startPrb + numOfPrb) (see 5G_UP_ALG_204_replaced([11096067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmruQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If the RB allocation is non-contiguous (rat0Bitmap IE different from 0), L2-PS computes numLteCrsMappingRes with the number of RBs from the startRbRbg to (startRbRbg + nbRbRbg) where startRbRbg is the first RB of this RBG and nbRbRbg = rbgSizeFirst if the first RBG is granted otherwise rbgSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4459_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for each RBG granted (bit =1 in the bitmap rat0Bitmap) and sums all the numLteCrsMappingRes for all the RBG granted. The number of LTE CRS of this PDSCH grant (numLteCrsMappingRes ) is equal to [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. It shows a summation symbol with "rb,i" below it, indicating the variables over which the summation is performed. To the right of the summation symbol is the term "numLteCrRsRes [rb][i]". This represents the number of LTE CRS resources for a given resource block (rb) and index (i).[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7077.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] where the table numLteCrsRes is stored and computed in 5G_UP_ALG_2990_replaced([11125053](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsEj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the rb is the rbIndex for all the RBs in the PDSCH grant and i is the ofdmIndex included in the range [startSymbol..(startSymbol + numOfPdschSymbols )]. Note: for LTE CRS REs mapping for 1 port (numLteCrsPorts = 1 in 5G_UP_ALG_2990_replaced([11125053](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsEj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the ofdmIndex used for LTE CRS mapping are the same than 2 LTE CRS ports, but the LTE CRS REs are asymmetric (see 5G_UP_ALG_453_replaced([11098260](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km9bEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 36.211 §6.12). If the RB allocation is contiguous, the rbindex rb is such as startrb <= rb < (startrb+numPrb). If the RB allocation is non-contiguous, for each RBG granted, the rbindex rb is such as startRbRbg <= rb < (startRbRbg + nbRbRbg). [CB008927-Q] Note: when NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True, gNB-DU shall apply the LTE CRS rate matching for contiguous RB allocation when the allocation is RAT1 and for non contiguous RB allocation when the allocation is dynamicswitch RAT0 [End CB008927-Q]

number of ratematched REs 12683255: Calculation of number of ratematched REs For FDD DSS: If flag LTE/PSS/SSS ratematching flag (isLteFlexibleRateMatchingEnabled=TRUE - see 5G_L2_IF_122_replaced([10969219](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UbPTPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) dynamic spectrum sharing structure content of Cell Setup Request message) and UE is configured by L3 to support of Flexible Rate matching UE indicates rateMatchingResrcSetSemi-Static IE as supported 5G_CP_IF_RRC_6204_replaced([11374757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wa-L8fvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) all the REs of the RBs located on Nr slot 5 and on the same symbols than the LTE PSS/SSS (that is symbols 5 and 6) and on Nr slot 0 on the same symbols than the LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH (that is from symbol 5 to 10) are LTE CRS ratematched and are added to _numLteCrsMappingRes iE and it is computed with the following formula:_ numLtePssSssPbchMappingRes = [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is: $\Sigma_{rb} 12 * nbOfdmSymbol$.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7080.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] Where the nbOfdmSymbols = 6 if the Nr slot = 0 and nbOfdmSymbols = 2 if the Nr slot = 5. [CB007372-M] For symmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M] If the RB allocation is contiguous, the rbindex rb is such as: If ((NRslot = 5) and ((dlBandwidth = 52) or (dlBandwidth = 106)) and (isLteFlexibleRateMatchingAltModeEnabled=TRUE))  if startrb <= ((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3 ) and startrb+numPrb >= ((dlBandwidth>>1) + 3), ((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3 ) <= rb < ((dlBandwidth>>1) + 3)  if startrb > ((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3 ) and startrb+numPrb >= ((dlBandwidth>>1) + 3), startrb <= rb < ((dlBandwidth>>1) + 3)  if startrb <= ((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3 ) and startrb+numPrb < ((dlBandwidth>>1) + 3), ((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3 ) <= rb < (startrb+numPrb). else  if startrb <= ((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3 ) and startrb+numPrb >= ((dlBandwidth>>1) + 4), ((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3 ) <= rb < ((dlBandwidth>>1) + 4)  if startrb > ((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3 ) and startrb+numPrb >= ((dlBandwidth>>1) + 4), startrb <= rb < ((dlBandwidth>>1) + 4)  if startrb <= ((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3 ) and startrb+numPrb < ((dlBandwidth>>1) + 4), ((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3 ) <= rb < (startrb+numPrb). If the RB allocation is non contiguous, for each RBG granted, the rbindex rb is such as: If ((NRslot = 5) and ((dlBandwidth = 52) or (dlBandwidth = 106)) and (isLteFlexibleRateMatchingAltModeEnabled=TRUE)) startRbRbg <= rb < (startRbRbg + nbRbRbg) and ((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3 )<=rb< ((dlBandwidth>>1) + 3). else startRbRbg <= rb < (startRbRbg + nbRbRbg) and ((dlBandwidth>>1) – 3 )<=rb< ((dlBandwidth>>1) + 4). [CB007372-M] For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) the rate matched PRBs are in the range [lowestPrbOfLtePbchPss..highestPrbOfLtePbchPss]. The parameters lowestPrbOfLtePbchPss and highestPrbOfLtePbchPss are received from the C-Plane ([12702622: Calculation of RateMatchPattern PRBs for LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YkJIMl4kEfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB007372-M] The parameter startrb and nbRbRbg are defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2996_replaced([11125095](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The IE dlBandwidth of the Cell Setup Request message corresponds to the NIDD parameter NRCELL/NRCELL_FDD.chBwDl. The _numLteCrsMappingRes IE (5G_UP_ALG_2996_replaced([11125095](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of the DlData_PdschSendReq message is updated in this way:_ 1) Sum the number of LTE CRS rate matched REs (computed in numLteCrsMappingRes , see 5G_UP_ALG_2996_replaced([11125095](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the number of the LTE PSS/SSS or LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH rate matched REs (numLtePssSssPbchMappingRes previously computed). 2) then put this sum result into _numLteCrsMappingRes IE_ Note: This sum is applied too for 1 LTE CRS port rate matching (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_7622_replaced([11325223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TsrfvvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as it is done for 2 and 4 LTE CR ports **Note: when NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True, gNB-DU shall apply the LTE CRS rate matching for contiguous RB allocation when the allocation is RAT1 and for non contiguous RB allocation when the allocation is dynamicswitch RAT0**

---

## 3.8.2 DL Link Adaptation

### 3.8.2.0-4 DL Link Adaptation Overall concept of the DL Link Adaptation (ID: 11125264)

**3.8.2.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `13458461`)

DL Link Adaptation 13458461: DL Link Adaptation 13458461: DL Link Adaptation 13458461: DL Link Adaptation 13458461: DL Link Adaptation 13458461: DL Link Adaptation 13458461: DL Link Adaptation 13458461: DL Link Adaptation 13458461: DL Link Adaptation The DL Link Adaptation consists of two functional entities - the Inner Loop Link Adaptation (ILLA) and the Outer Loop Link Adaptation (OLLA). The feature set of the ILLA covers two basic evaluation functions. On the one hand, the ILLA feeds an exponential filter with the captured CQI reports and calculates the average CQI (see 5G_UP_ALG_3005_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). On the other hand, the average CQI evaluated by the ILLA is corrected by using the delta CQI (see 5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), which is provided by the OLLA. The OLLA determines the offset factor, the delta CQI, in such a way that systematic errors, which are stuck to the CQI reports, are eliminated. For this purpose, the OLLA evaluates the HARQ feedback of initial PDSCH transmissions and adjusts the delta CQI parameter with each received ACK/NACK HARQ feedback correspondingly (see 5G_UP_ALG_3060_replaced([11129299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The corrected average CQI is calculated with the reception of a CQI report or a HARQ feedback as shown in the following flow chart. Calculation of corrected average CQI: [vsd_desc]=== Diagram Content === Start End Recalculate CQI offset (rrmDeltaCqi) Execute recalculation of average CQI (rrmAvgCqi) CQI Report HARQ feedback Calculate corrected average CQI (rrmCorrAvgCqi = rrmAvgCqi + rrmDeltaCqi) === Diagram Metadata === dc:creator: dems1196 Content-Type: application/vnd.visio[/vsd_desc][vsd_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/attachments/m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file7097_0.vsd[/vsd_path] If [Before CB013712-A]NRCELL.actDlMuMimo is true or [End CB013712-A] NRCELL.actDlMuMimoEnh is true, for each co-scheduler users, the rrmCorrAvgCqi shall be updated to include the factor of **rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo with the below equation:** rrmCorrAvgCqi = rrmCorrAvgCqi + rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo where rrmCorrAvgCqi and **rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo are defined in 5G_UP_ALG_7555_replaced([11126154](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).** Finally, the ILLA selects based on the corrected average CQI the MCS that fits to the conditions at the air interface and is best suited for the pending DL data transmission (see 5G_UP_ALG_3045_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The default MCS, which is specified by the NIDD parameter NRCELL.dllaIniMcs for basic reliability LA and NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaIniMcsHR for high reliability LA is applied if the CQI reports are not available or outdated. The MCS shall be evaluated independently per code word, which means per carrier, by a separate DL Link Adaptation. A change of the NIDD parameter NRCELL.dllaIniMcs shall be applied only for newly instantiated UEs. For existing UE contexts, the initially configured default MCS is kept. File: Drawings for 5GC000517_5GC001796_ DLLA_and_ULLA.vsdx Folder:

---

### 3.8.2.1 Inner Loop DL Link Adaptation

#### 3.8.2.1.1 Initial MCS

##### 3.8.2.1.1.0-1 Initial MCS Determination of the DL MCS index (ID: 11125301)

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11125306`)

Initial MCS Overview

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `13454101`)

Initial MCS Based on the evaluated corrected average WB-CQI (see 5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) the Inner Loop Link Adaptation shall determine the MCS index that can be used for DL scheduling, as following.

\- MCS calculation, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_11042_replaced([11125333](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5ufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

\- MCS re-determination (5G_UP_ALG_17189_replaced([11129019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq6G_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- Max code rate check, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_11045_replaced([11125491](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH0vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

\- Early DL OLLA, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_11046_replaced([11125506](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The Inner Loop Link Adaptation shall determine the MCS with reception of a CQI report, or whenever an initial transmission is scheduled except during an ongoing RACH procedure, if link Adaptation Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency is disabled in DL ([12472235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_Of9dYA1aEfCuhewgqkUVKQ)) [Before CB014045-A-UPD002] or the cell is FR2 [End CB014045-A-UPD002], otherwise the MCS shall be calculated before the PDSCH resource assignment using FDM ([11179166](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_h9kTwPvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The proposed MCS shall be provided to the UE context of the DL scheduler for the calculation of the DL PF term (5G_UP_ALG_4232_replaced([11175151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kug0nPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). To this purpose, the CQI2MCS table corresponding to the configured band (FR1 or FR2) the UE capability (64QAM or 256QAM or 1024QAM or lowSE MCS), the selected rank (Rank1, Rank2, Rank3, or Rank4) and 11 PDSCH symbols shall be used.

With NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True and rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs (see 5G_UP_ALG_24185_replaced([11138819](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kriZEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to True, the proposed MCS shall be based on the least number of PRBs (2 PRBs) in the chosen CQI2MCS table as described above, otherwise if rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs is set to False, then the proposed MCS shall be Wideband i.e based on all the available PRBs with respect to the system bandwidth.

The 2-PRB based proposed MCS (known as rrmDlProposedMcs both before scaling (5G_UP_ALG_24101_replaced([11125402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and after scaling (5G_UP_ALG_24146_replaced([11125436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in the rest of the sections related to DL Link Adaptation) shall be used for a UE in a slot for estimation of required resources during FDM phase and later in FD phase, with scaling as per the actual resources granted for TBS construction) (see rationale in 5G_UP_11025_replaced([11350925](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubpjHPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and related functionalities that would require this MCS derived, as listed below.

\- Determining the maximum number of FD resources which initial transmission UEs can get (5G_UP_ALG_4509_replaced([11181264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), which uses the rrmDlProposedMcs (WB-based or 2-PRB based)

\- Resource Distribution for Initial Transmission UEs as Per Priority Class (5G_UP_ALG_21155_replaced([11179648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- Resource distribution to Msg2/Msg4/SRB0 in initial bandwidth part (indeed CORESET(0) bandwidth) (5G_UP_ALG_16521_replaced([11179343](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- Restriction on UE DL max data rate (5G_UP_ALG_4632_replaced([11192333](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvj9dPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- Correction of the MCS for DL scheduling (5G_UP_ALG_4630_replaced([11192270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvjWafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) 

\- Power Reduction (5G_UP_ALG_2572_replaced([11118517](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDyYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) (using scaled MCS)

\- Rank Adaptation (5G_UP_ALG_23994_replaced([11139686](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krpGxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) (using scaled MCS)

\- Determination of UE category for FD resource allocation (5G_UP_ALG_8432_replaced([11181306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- Radio link quality based Scell Activation/Deactivation (5G_UP_ALG_2742_replaced([11121361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqX7e_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- Calculation of estimated throughput measurement for the flow control - non GBR (5G_UP_ALG_4677_replaced([11192687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvmZt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note: The rrmDlProposedMcs used functionalities is the scaled MCS derived from the 2-PRB based proposed MCS (5G_UP_ALG_24146_replaced([11125436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Else

Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS 12659436: Initial MCS In case of FDM, if NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True and rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs (see 5G_UP_ALG_24185_replaced([11138819](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kriZEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to True, gNB-DU follow the rules defined in 5G_UP_ALG_24101_replaced([11125402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for MCS determination, otherwise follow the rules defined hereafter. Note: this previous statement is also applicable in the case (if **NRCELL.actDlDynSwitchAlloc is set to true whatever the allocation is RAT1, "dynamicswitch" RAT1 or "dynamicswitch" RAT0)** The Inner Loop Link Adaptation compares the evaluated corrected average WB-CQI (5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the required CQI for the considered MCS for a DL allocation with respect to the system bandwidth in PRBs starting with the DL MCS of highest supported index (5G_UP_ALG_26337_replaced([11124495](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqq2b_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ‘Supported MCS’) and selects the MCS if the evaluated corrected average CQI is equal or greater than the required CQI. If the conditions are not met, the evaluation continues with the next smaller MCS index in the list of supported MCSes (see 5G_UP_ALG_26337_replaced([11124495](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqq2b_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ‘Supported MCS’) and is repeated until the MCS index is found, which satisfies the condition. If none of the supported MCS indices satisfied the condition, the smallest MCS index in the list shall be selected. Dependent on the system bandwidth in PRBs, used duplex scheme, enableDlPtrs (NRCELL.dlPtrs.actDlPtrs), supported highest modulation scheme, rank, which FR is used, the respective CQI2MCS table shall be applied which can be found for in 5G_UP_ALG_3015_replaced([11126253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9A_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case the used bandwidth in PRBs is not available in the chosen MCS2QCI table, the needed entry is interpolated accordingly if NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is False. In case of FDM and this requirement is executed a second time for scaling purposes if NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is False, and the decision about the PTRS configuration (dlPtrsFlag – see 5G_UP_ALG_4666_replaced([11192615](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlyqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is already done, then dlPtrsFlag shall be reflected in the choice of the respective CQI2MCS table instead of enableDlPtrs (NRCELL.dlPtrs.actDlPtrs) into account. If NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is True **single PRB number defined by R &D parameter **rdDlPRBMcsSelection (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_29011_replaced([11138974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krjnMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be used according to 5G_UP_ALG_3029_replaced([11126315](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9DPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**_* DL allocations of 64PRB / 66PRB for FR2 deployments and of 256PRB / 273PRB for FR1 deployments are supported for system bandwidths of 100MHz. Whenever a UE gets allocated resources for a new transmission (5G_UP_ALG_4510_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is False, the MCS needs to be reselected to take into account the number of PRBs allocated to the UE. If the buffer contains RLC status PDU, gNB-DU shall try to find whether the DL TB size based on scaled MCS which selected according to allocated PRBs is larger than or equal to length of RLC status PDU + Mac Header. If no, then gNB-DU shall try to find a smallest MCS which is greater than the scaled MCS(which is selected according to allocated PRBs) but less than or equal to proposedMCS(based on full bandwidth), and the mapped DL TB size is larger than or equal to RLC status PDU+ Mac Header. If gNB-DU cannot find such MCS, gNB-DU shall still use the scaledMCS obtained based on allocated PRBs. Undesired TBS reduction is possible due to usage of different Cqi jitter values (5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to calculate the DL proposed MCS and the final reselected MCS by ILLA, depending on the implementation when the DL MCS index is forwarded to the DL scheduler. [Before CNI-137616-C] If rdEnableAvoidTbsSegDl (5G_UP_ALG_17597_replaced([11138723](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krhK-_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to True, then [End CNI-137616-C] The scheduler shall make sure that the same jitter is applied for the user when selecting proposed MCS and final reselected MCS during DL SRB transmissions to avoid segmentation and help to drain the buffered bytes and thereby also avoid delay in signalling that may result otherwise. For further development: In future the scheduler should make sure that the same jitter is applied for the user when selecting proposed MCS and final reselected MCS whenever jitter is used and not restricted to SRB transmissions alone. Rationale: The above is particularly beneficial in cells with smaller bandwidth and small DL transmissions as this would reduce the chances of segmentation caused due the jitter and MCS reselection, and thereby efficient usage of PDCCH and PDSCH resources. Whenever a UE is allocated resources for a new transmission (5G_UP_ALG_4510_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the MCS value derived from the corrected average CQI rrmCorrAvgCqi (5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the CQI2MCS mapping tables (in 5G_L1L2_FLOW_173_replaced([11185587](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Be796_vREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_3033_replaced([11126801](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqyyPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_3039_replaced([11127382](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq1OcfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_3041_replaced([11127446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq1OevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or 5G_UP_ALG_3043_replaced([11128089](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq3DsPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and without considering any downgrades to the MCS due to any other reasons (including code rate limitations etc.) shall be included in the L2 trace as parameter cqiDerivedMcs, see 5G_L2_1637_replaced([11201971](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29yzDPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating the process of MCS (Modulation and Coding Scheme) selection. The flowchart begins with a "Start MCS Selection" oval. From there, a request is made to "Provide DL MCS" using a parallelogram shape. The next step is a diamond shape asking if "rdCqiExpiryTime expired". \- If "No", the process proceeds to "reset earlyDilaMode" in a rectangular box. \- If "Yes", the process moves to another diamond shape asking if "rdActEarlyDila activated". \- If "No", the process moves to "set earlyDilaMode" in a rectangular box. \- If "Yes", the process also moves to "set earlyDilaMode" in a rectangular box. After either resetting or setting early Dila mode, the flowchart continues to "Setup sorted list of available MCSes" in a rectangular box. This is followed by "MCSI = biggest MCS available in list" in a rectangular box. Next, there's a diamond shape checking if "MCSI == smallest MCS in list". \- If "Yes", the process moves to a diamond shape checking if "corrected average WB-CQI >= requested CQI for MCSI index". \- If "Yes", it proceeds to a diamond shape checking if "Code Rate <= 0.95". \- If "Yes", it sets "rmmDIProposedMcs = MCSI" in a rectangular box. \- If "No", it proceeds to "Remove evaluated MCS from list" in a rectangular box. \- If "No", it proceeds to "Remove evaluated MCS from list" in a rectangular box. \- If "No" (from "MCSI == smallest MCS in list"), it sets "MCSI = biggest MCS from list" in a rectangular box. From the "Remove evaluated MCS from list" box, the flowchart leads to setting "rmmDIProposedMcs = dlalniMcs" in a rectangular box. Both the path from "rmmDIProposedMcs = MCSI" and "rmmDIProposedMcs = dlalniMcs" lead to the "End MCS selection" oval.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7113.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] MCS selection processing of a user (carrier basis). For EarlyDllaMode, see the definition in 5G_UP_ALG_11046_replaced([11125506](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR is equal to Zero, the determination of the MCS index is finalized with the forwarding of the selected MCS (rrmDlProposedMcs) to the DL scheduler, else if NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR is greater than Zero and less than or equal to 99 AND rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs is set to FALSE AND a UE (either RAT1 or dynamicswitch RAT1, where NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True) has an initial VoNR transmission (5QI-1 bearer either deprioritizedVoip or voipTraffic) - and GBR or Non GBR bearers in case of multi bearers. Then, gNB-DU shall apply the VoNR Robustness Algorithm based on SE downgrade (see 5G_UP_ALG_25588_replaced([11125547](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH3PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to _*rrmDlProposedMcs to compute the new updated **rrmDlProposedMcs, and gNB-DU shall forward the selected MCS (**rrmDlProposedMcs) to the DL scheduler in order to drain out the buffer of the UE.****_* Hence, the below Figure 2 is an extension to Figure 1. [image-1][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process. The flowchart consists of three rectangular boxes connected by arrows. The first box, at the top, contains the text "Link Adaptation". An arrow points downwards from this box, with the text "rrmDlProposedMcs" written next to it. The second box, in the middle, contains the text "SE Reduction Algorithm". An arrow points downwards from this box, with the text "New rrmDlProposedMcs based on percRedSEDIRat1VoNR" written next to it. The third box, at the bottom, contains the text "DL Scheduler".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7113.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] Figure 2: SE Reduction Algorithm for VoNR Robustness If NIDD NRCELL.actDlRankAdpAndSRB1McsLimDurHO is set to TRUE, upon reception of LoCtrl_DlBufferStatusInd with rrcAction as "RRCReconfigHo" or PsUser_UserSuspendSchedulingReq message with ueSuspendCause set as "_ HO_intra_du" or "_HO_others" or "_ HO_with_ExtScell ", in order to increase the reliability of SRB1 transmission to the UE, the MCS limitation below shall be performed for the initial SRB1 transmission. In case PsUser_UserResumeSchedulingReq is received, the MCS limitation below shall not be performed for the initial SRB1 transmission.**_\- For FDD, in case of the initial transmission of SRB1, if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than [Before CB008949-A] rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO (see 5G_L2_3280_replaced([11186451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2758ofvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB008949-A] rrmMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [End CB008949-A], rrmDlProposedMcs shall be set to [Before CB008949-A] rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [CB008949-A] rrmMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [End CB008949-A]. \- For TDD FR1, in case of the initial transmission of SRB1, if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than [Before CB008949-A] rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [CB008949-A] rrmMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [End CB008949-A], rrmDlProposedMcs shall be set to [Before CB008949-A] rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [CB008949-A] rrmMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [End CB008949-A]. __\- For both FDD and FR1 TDD, [Before CB008949-A] rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [CB008949-A] rrmMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [End CB008949-A] shall be set to rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO64QAM (see 5G_L2_11394_replaced([11186458](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2758ovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if UE is configured with 64QAM/64QAM lowSE table or rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO256QAM (see 5G_L2_11395_replaced([11186470](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2758pPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if UE is configured with 256QAM table [CB008949-A] or rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO1024QAM ([12721918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TcUR4G2-EfCmnYHxzZN44w)) if UE is configured with 1024QAM table [End CB008949-A]._* File: Drawings for 5GC000517_5GC001796_ DLLA_and_ULLA.vsdx Folder:
