**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11125402`)

Initial MCS Proposed MCS calculation

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-3.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `13382869`)

Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS 13382869: Initial MCS When link Adaptation Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency for DL in FR1 is enabled (see [12472220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HoCyEA1UEfCuhewgqkUVKQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), if the latest proposed MCS is still not updated as [11125306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5s_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), ILLA shall perform proposed MCS calculation at the FDM ([1179166](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4A_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) before FD resource allocation. When link Adaptation Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency for DL in FR1 is enabled (see [12472220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HoCyEA1UEfCuhewgqkUVKQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), the following applies in TRS slots, if (NRBTS.actSubTRSRateMatch = True) and (NRCELL.actRat1Alloc == True). At DL proposed-MCS calculation phase, in FDM: proposedMcsCqiBackoff = proposed MCS calculated as in the current requirement and considering the CQI backoff with rdTrsSmallPdschAvgCqiRatio (5G_UP_ALG_31623_replaced([11138899](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krjAIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))); ueHasFewDataInBuffer = TBS(4 PRBs, proposedMcsCqiBackoff) >= 'bytes in DL transmission buffer' If (ueHasFewDataInBuffer == True) { proposedMcs = proposedMcsCqiBackoff; } Else { proposedMcs = proposedMcsWoCqiBackoff; // where proposedMcsWoCqiBackoff is the proposed Mcs calculated as in the current requirement and without considering any CQI backoff [CB014045-F]If NRCELL.act4prbAllocDlTrsSlotsForRat1 is set to True,the WRR/ES scheduler shall assume for the PRB distribution that the UE needs at least 4 PRBs to empty its buffer, as specified in [11180562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the WRR-scheduler and [12487768](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Rj_cYBBKEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the Exhaustive scheduler. Rationale: if the UE is allocated less than 4 PRBs the UE is scheduled with a lower MCS (proposedMcsCqiBackoff) than the one used for PRB estimation (proposedMcsWoCqiBackoff).[End CB014045-F] } proposedMcs is used for the estimation of the required PRBs. After the final distribution: If ( (ueHasFewDataInBuffer = False) and (the number of PRBs granted for the PDSCH scrambled by DCI 1_1 is less than 4 PRBs) ) { proposedMcs = proposedMcsCqiBackoff; } The Inner Loop Link Adaptation compares the evaluated corrected average WB-CQI (5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the required CQI for the considered MCS for a DL allocation. The MCS calculation shall be **using 2 PRBs starting with the DL MCS of highest index if NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is False or** using single PRB number defined by R&D parameter **rdDlPRBMcsSelection (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_29011_replaced([11138974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krjnMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is True according to 5G_UP_ALG_3029_replaced([11126315](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9DPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) **and selects the MCS if the evaluated corrected average CQI is equal or greater than the required CQI. The list of MCSes is based on MCS index tables -see 3GPP 38.214 §5.1.3.1- according to mcs-table value (not reserved): 256qam (table 5.1.3.1-2) , qam64LowSE (table 5.1.3.1-3) or 64qam (table 5.1.3.1-1) or 1024qam (table 5.1.3.1-4). Dependent on the system bandwidth in PRBs, used duplex scheme, (Note PTRS is disabled for FDD and FR1 TDD) supported highest modulation scheme, rank, which FR is used, gNB-Du shall apply the respective CQI2MCS table which can be found for in 5G_UP_ALG_3015_replaced([11126253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9A_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**_*_**___rrmDlProposedMcs: DL MCS index calculated using 2 PRBs (instead of full BW allocation) if NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is False_ _or using_ _single PRB number defined by R &D parameter __rdDlPRBMcsSelection (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_29011_replaced([11138974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krjnMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is True according to 5G_UP_ALG_3029_replaced([11126315](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9DPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in the below algorithm for DL._**_*_** **Rationale: In order to avoid segmentation with DL RAT1 allocation (and reduce latency) if NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is False, gNB-DU shall base the computation of the proposed MCS on the assumption of 2 PRB allocation (instead of wideband allocation). In general, the number of PRBs which has the highest SINR requirement should be used. Since the 2 PRBs allocation seemed to have the highest SINR requirement for any given MCS, then the determined number of PRBs is always sufficient to drain the data and hence avoid segmentation. Note that if NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is True, MCS scaling is not needed any more as single** PRB number defined by R&D parameter **rdDlPRBMcsSelection (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_29011_replaced([11138974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krjnMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is used according to 5G_UP_ALG_3029_replaced([11126315](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9DPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))****_* Rationale: If NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is True, the parameter setting of rdDlPRBMcsSelection takes precedence over 2PRB regardless of rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs. If NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is False and NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = TRUE, then 2PRB shall be used when rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs is set with True. If the conditions are not met (see figure below), the evaluation continues with the next smaller MCS index in the list of supported MCSes and is repeated until the MCS index is found, which satisfies the condition. If none of the supported MCS indices satisfied the condition, gNB-DU shall select the smallest MCS index in the list. [image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating the process of "MCS Selection". Here's a breakdown of the flowchart: 1._ *Start MCSNone[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7116.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] Figure 1: MCS determination with 2PRBs During MCS selection processing of a user (carrier basis), for EarlyDllaMode, see the definition in 5G_UP_ALG_11046_replaced([11125506](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR is equal to Zero gNB-DU shall finalize the determination of the proposed MCS index with the forwarding of the selected MCS (rrmDlProposedMcs) to the DL scheduler , else if NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR is greater than Zero and less than or equal to 99 AND a UE (either RAT1 or dynamicswitch RAT1) has an initial VoNR transmission (5QI-1 bearer either deprioritizedVoip or voipTraffic) - and GBR or Non GBR bearers in case of multi bearers. Then, gNB-DU shall apply the VoNR Robustness Algorithm based on SE downgrade (see 5G_UP_ALG_25588_replaced([11125547](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH3PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to **rrmDlProposedMcs to compute the new updated **rrmDlProposedMcs, and gNB-DU shall forward the selected MCS (** rrmDlProposedMcs) to the DL scheduler in order to drain out the buffer of the UE.***** * Hence, the below Figure 2 is an extension to Figure 1. [image-1][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process. The flowchart consists of three rectangular boxes connected by arrows. The first box, at the top, contains the text "Link Adaptation". An arrow points downwards from this box, with the text "rrmDlProposedMcs" written next to it. The second box, in the middle, contains the text "SE Reduction Algorithm". An arrow points downwards from this box, with the text "New rrmDlProposedMcs based on percRedSEDIRat1VoNR" written next to it. The third box, at the bottom, contains the text "DL Scheduler".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7116.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] Figure 2: SE Reduction Algorithm for VoNR Robustness MCS selection limitations for SRB and 5QI5 initial transmission is as per 5G_UP_ALG_31381_replaced([11125468](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugzvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) MCS calculation limitations for initial transmission with 5QI1 data is as per 5G_UP_ALG_32930_replaced([11125480](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) For any UE with initial BWP transmission for RA-msg2/SRB0/msg4, gNB-DU shall perform the MCS selection as per the rules defined in 5G_UP_ALG_16523_replaced([11179366](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). **3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-3.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `12659438`)

Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS 12659438: Initial MCS In case of FDM and if the FD resource distribution is unable to reserve the PRBs to drain out the buffer for a UE (See 5G_UP_ALG_21155_replaced([11179648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku52LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for resource distribution as per priority classes), gNB-DU shall execute MCS re-determination a second time for scaling purposes (but for a MCS upsize) based on the respective CQI2MCS table with the number of PRBs reserved for a new transmission (5G_UP_ALG_4510_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). In case the number of PRBs is not available in the chosen MCS2QCI table (see 5G_UP_ALG_3015_replaced([11126253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9A_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the needed entry is interpolated accordingly. The same list of MCS index is used (based on the same 3GPP table). The starting DL MCS is the current rrmDlProposedMcs (MCSi = rrmDlProposedMcs and not the smallest, see (_) in figure below). The Inner Loop Link Adaptation compares the evaluated corrected average WB-CQI (5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the required CQI for the considered MCS and if the conditions are still met (see figure 1), the evaluation continues with the next higher MCS index in the list of supported MCSes and is repeated until the conditions are no more satisfied. In this case the MCS found is the previous MCS (rrmDlProposedMcs = MCSi – 1, see figure below). If all the supported MCS indices fulfil the conditions, the highest MCS index in the list shall be selected. Note for the highest MCS of the list, a code rate check is done: if it fails, the MCS found is the previous one (rrmDlProposedMcs = MCSi – 1, see figure below). __Rationale: In order to avoid an impact of cell peak and UE peak throughput, if the MCS computed on the assumption of 2 PRBs allocation, gNB-DU shall add a step which upsizes the MCS (provided the condition on the evaluated corrected average CQI which is equal or greater than the required CQI is fulfilled) at FDM scheduler when the number of PRBs is reserved for the RAT1 allocation (see 5G_UP_ALG_4510_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The UEs and bearer candidate for this MCS upsize are when the FD resource distribution is unable to reserve the PRBs to drain out the buffer with the rrmDlProposedMcs based on 2 PRBs.__[image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process for MCS (Modulation and Coding Scheme) selection. The flowchart begins with a rounded rectangle labeled "Start MCS selection". An arrow points to a parallelogram labeled "Request to provide a DL scaled MCS". Next, a rectangular box contains the text "MCSi = rrmDiproposedMcs (_)". An arrow leads from this box to a diamond shape with the question "MCSi > the highest MCS in the list". If the answer to this question is "Yes", an arrow points to a rectangular box labeled "MCSI = MCSI + 1". An arrow then loops back to the diamond shape. If the answer to "MCSi > the highest MCS in the list" is "No", an arrow points to another diamond shape with the condition "Code Rate <= 0.95 (**)". If the answer to "Code Rate <= 0.95" is "No", an arrow points to the left, bypassing the next decision point, and then loops upwards to the "MCSI = MCSI + 1" box. If the answer to "Code Rate <= 0.95" is "Yes", an arrow points to a diamond shape with the condition "Cerrected average WB-COL >= Requested CQI". If the answer to "Cerrected average WB-COL >= Requested CQI" is "Yes", an arrow points to the left, bypassing the next step, and then loops upwards to the "MCSI = MCSI + 1" box. If the answer to "Cerrected average WB-COL >= Requested CQI" is "No", an arrow points to a rectangular box labeled "rrmDiproposedMcs = MCSi - 1". An arrow then leads from this box to a rounded rectangle labeled "End MCS selection". The flowchart uses standard symbols: rounded rectangles for start/end, parallelograms for input/output, rectangles for processes, and diamonds for decisions. The text within the shapes is clear and legible.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7118.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] In some scenarios, as an extra step after choosing the rrmDlProposedMcs described above, if still the buffer could not be drained out completely, gNB-DU (at DL scheduler level) shall try to further upsize the MCS if possible, until the same conditions are met (evaluated corrected average CQI equal or greater than the required CQI and the code rate check is satisfied). If such a MCS index is found, gNB-DU (at FD scheduler level) shall set the rrmDlProposedMcs to this new value else the one already selected is used. **Rationale: In some cases, there could still be some residual bits left in the buffer for the UE after the scaled MCS is determined, and by upsizing the MCS even further may help in draining the buffer completely out, however not necessarily all the time.**For any UE with initial BWP transmission for RA-msg2/SRB0/msg4, gNB-DU shall perform the MCS selection as per the rules defined in 5G_UP_ALG_16523_replaced([11179366](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Undesired TBS reduction is possible due to usage of different Cqi jitter values (5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to calculate the DL proposed MCS and the final scaled MCS reselected by ILLA (and saved to rrmDlProposedMcs), depending on the implementation when the proposed DL MCS index is forwarded to the DL scheduler and the scaling is performed after resource distribution. [Before CNI-137616-C] If rdEnableAvoidTbsSegDl (5G_UP_ALG_17597_replaced([11138723](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krhK-_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to True, then [End CNI-137616-C] gNB (at scheduler level) shall make sure that the same jitter is applied for the user when selecting proposed MCS and final scaled MCS during DL SRB transmissions to avoid segmentation and help to drain the buffered bytes and thereby also avoid delay in signalling that may result otherwise. For further development: In future the scheduler should make sure that the same jitter is applied for the user when selecting proposed MCS and final scaled MCS whenever jitter is used and not restricted to SRB transmissions alone. **Rationale: The above is particularly beneficial in cells with smaller bandwidth and small DL transmissions as this would reduce the chances of segmentation caused due the jitter and MCS reselection, and thereby efficient usage of PDCCH and PDSCH resources.** For any UE with SRB data, further scaled MCS shall also be restricted as per 5G_UP_ALG_31381_replaced([11125468](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugzvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11125450`)

Initial MCS MCS calculation for High reliability DRBs

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11125462`)

Initial MCS For basic principle of MCS calculation please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_22781_replaced([11125340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5u_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

After proposed MCS obtained in 5G_UP_ALG_22781_replaced([11125340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5u_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), L2PS shall restrict the proposed MCS as below:

[CB008927-S-UPD001] Note that the proposed MCS given by the term rrmDlProposedMcs here means ,

when FDM is disabled, WB-based DL proposed MCS (when NRCELL.actRat1Alloc=False) or DL proposed MCS(with NRCELL.actRat1Alloc=True and if rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs (5G_UP_ALG_24185_replaced([11138819](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kriZEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) = True, based on 2-PRBs, else when rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs = False, WB based).

When FDM is enabled, the proposed MCS means the scaled MCS (also given by the same term rrmDlProposedMcs) by taking into account the number of PRBs allocated to the UE. For MCS scaling when NRCELL.actRat1alloc=False or when NRCELL.actRat1Alloc=True and rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs = False , refer to 5G_UP_ALG_22781_replaced([11125340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5u_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), otherwise when NRCELL.actRat1alloc=True and rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs = True, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_24146_replaced([11125436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB008927-S-UPD001]

In case of initial transmission of PDSCH, if UE is configured with LowSE MCS table and works in high reliability LA mode

  * if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaMcsMax64qamLowSERANK1HR while UE works with RANK1, or

  * if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaMcsMax64qamLowSERANK2HR while UE works with RANK2, or

  * if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaMcsMax64qamLowSERANK3HR while UE works with RANK3, or

  * if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaMcsMax64qamLowSERANK4HR while UE works with RANK4,




rrmDlProposedMcs shall be set to corresponding NIDD parameter value.

In case of initial transmission of PDSCH, if UE is configured with 64QAM MCS table and works in high reliability LA mode

  * if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaMcsMax64qamRANK1HR while UE works with RANK1, or

  * if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaMcsMax64qamRANK2HR while UE works with RANK2, or

  * if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaMcsMax64qamRANK3HR while UE works with RANK3, or

  * if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaMcsMax64qamRANK4HR while UE works with RANK4,




rrmDlProposedMcs shall be set to corresponding NIDD parameter value.

[Before CB008927-S-UPD001]rrmDlProposedMcs here mean the wideband proposed MCS and the reselected/scaled MCS taken into account the number of PRBs allocated to the UE.[End CB008927-S-UPD001]

[PR771813] Irrespective of deriving the scaled MCS when FDM is enabled based on WB or 2-PRB based proposed MCS, the above principle to limit the MCS is performed. [End PR771813 ]

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `11125468`)

Initial MCS MCS calculation restriction for SRB and 5QI5

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-3.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `12721920`)

Initial MCS MCS selection limitation for SRB1/2 and 5QI5/69

If actDlForceRankDowng is set to dlVoNRSrbVoNRCtrl/dlVoNRCtrlOnly/dlVonrSrbVoNRCtrNonGbrBr (applicable for FDD only , MCS limitation below shall be performed;

  * For a transmission with 5QI5/69 data **,** rrmDlProposedMcs is calculated with the downgraded rank, if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than maxMcsForDlVoNRCtrl, rrmDlProposedMcs shall be set to maxMcsForDlVoNRCtrl.****



If actDlForceRankDowng is set to dlVoNRSrbVoNRCtrl/dlSrbOnly/dlVonrSrbVoNRCtrNonGbrBr (applicable for FDD only), MCS limitation below shall be performed;

  * For a transmission with SRB1/SRB2 data ,**rrmDlProposedMcs is calculated with the downgraded rank, if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than maxMcsForDlSrb, rrmDlProposedMcs shall be set to maxMcsForDlSrb.**



If the above limitation has been applied for transmission with SRB1/SRB2/5QI5/5QI69, the need for MCS scaling is not evaluated and the gNB-DU (at FD scheduler level) shall use the above determined MCS as final used MCS.

If the MCS determined does not get limited at this stage of proposed MCS calculation for SRB1/2 and 5QI5/69 transmission, then if there is a need for MCS scaling later as described in 5G_UP_ALG_24146_replaced([11125436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), gNB-DU shall limit the scaled MCS as described above and shall be used as final MCS.

With NRCELL.actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP set to TRUE, and during transient phase, MCS limitation shall not be applied to 5QI5 data. Rationale: CB009550 has not specified the handling of bearers in transient phase (except SRB) which are scheduled in initial BWP. Due to this gap introduced with CB009550 the MCS limitation can’t be extended to 5QI5 bearer with NRCELL.actDlForceRankDowng set to dlVoNRCtrlOnly or dlVonrSrbVoNRCtrl or dlVonrSrbVoNRCtrNonGbrBr.

**Note:**

SRB along with other Bearer multiplexing is not supported as part of FDM resource distribution, RAT0 case is an exception to this, and can have this multiplexing post TBS (post MCS downgrade) calculation only.

actDlForceRankDowng: it is derive from NRCELL.actDlForceRankDowng

maxMcsForDlSrb: it is derive from NRCELL.dlForceRankDowngCtl.maxMcsForDlSrb

maxMcsForDlVoNRCTRL: it is derive from NRCELL.dlForceRankDowngCtl.maxMcsForDlVoNRCTRL

MCS selection limitations for SRB1 inital transmission:-

If NIDD NRCELL.actDlRankAdpAndSRB1McsLimDurHO is set to TRUE, upon reception of PsUser_UserSuspendSchedulingReq message with ueSuspendCause set as "_HO_intra_du" or "_ HO_others" or "_HO_with_ExtScell ", in order to increase the reliability of SRB1 transmission to the UE, the MCS limitation below shall be performed for the initial SRB1 transmission._ **

  * For FDD, in case of the initial transmission of SRB1, if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than [Before CB008949-A] rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO (see 5G_L2_3280_replaced([11186451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2758ofvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB008949-A] rrmMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [End CB008949-A], rrmDlProposedMcs shall be set to [Before CB008949-A] rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [CB008949-A] rrmMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [End CB008949-A]

  * For TDD FR1, in case of the initial transmission of SRB1, if rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than [Before CB008949-A] rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [CB008949-A] rrmMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [End CB008949-A], rrmDlProposedMcs shall be set to [Before CB008949-A] rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [CB008949-A] rrmMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [End CB008949-A].




**\- For both FDD and FR1 TDD, [Before CB008949-A] rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [CB008949-A] rrmMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO [End CB008949-A] shall be set to rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO64QAM (see 5G_L2_11394_replaced([11186458](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2758ovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if UE is configured with 64QAM/64QAM lowSE table or rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO256QAM (see 5G_L2_11395_replaced([11186470](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2758pPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if UE is configured with 256QAM table [CB008949-A] or rdMaxMcsForDlSrb1DurHO1024QAM ([12721918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TcUR4G2-EfCmnYHxzZN44w)) if UE is configured with 1024QAM table [End CB008949-A].**

After this step, if NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is False and if needed, **gNB-DU shall perform MCS re-determination based on the FD resource reservation at the end of either resource contention in Exhaustive Scheduling (see**[12483324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qeQ48g-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ)) if enabled, otherwise WRR (5G_UP_ALG_4504_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) or before. See MCS scaling rules defined in 5G_UP_ALG_24146_replaced([11125436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note that if NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is True, MCS scaling is not needed any more as single **PRB number defined by R &D parameter **rdDlPRBMcsSelection (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_29011_replaced([11138974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krjnMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is used according to 5G_UP_ALG_3029_replaced([11126315](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9DPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))****

Note that if the above limitation has been applied for initial SRB1 transmission, the need for MCS scaling is not evaluated and the gNB-DU (at FD scheduler level) shall use the above determined MCS as final used MCS.

If the MCS determined does not get limited at this stage of proposed MCs calculation for initial SRB1 transmission, then if there is a need for MCS scaling later as described in 5G_UP_ALG_24146_replaced([11125436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), gNB-DU shall limit the scaled MCS as described above and shall be used as final MCS.

In case PsUser_UserResumeSchedulingReq is received, gNB-DU shall not perform the above MCS limitation for the initial SRB1 transmission.

**NRCELL.dlForceRankDowngCtl.maxMcsForDlSrb**

**NRCELL.dlForceRankDowngCtl.maxMcsForDlVoNRCTRL**

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `11125480`)

Initial MCS MCS calculation limitation for VoNR

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-3.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11125484`)

Initial MCS If actDlForceRankDowng is set to dlVoNROnly,

  * A transmission with data on **[Before CB008118-C] 5QI-1**[CB008118-C] 5QI-1/65/66 **[End CB008118-C] bearer shall be force downgraded to Rank1 , rrmDlProposedMcs for this transmission shall be calculated using corrected CQI as per 5G_UP_ALG_30473_replaced([11126067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and Rank 1. [PR825506] If computed rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than NRCELL.maxMCSVoNRRankAndMCSRestrict,, rrmDlProposedMcs shall be set to NRCELL.maxMCSVoNRRankAndMCSRestrict.[End PR825506]******



If actDlForceRankDowng is set to dlVoNRSrbVoNRCtrl/dlVonrSrbVoNRCtrNonGbrBr(applicable for FDD only),

  * A transmission with data on **[Before CB008118-C] 5QI-1**[CB008118-C] 5QI-1/65/66 **[End CB008118-C] (and without**[Before CB008118-C] SRB1/SRB2/5QI5 **[CB008118-C] SRB1/SRB2/5QI5/5QI69**[End CB008118-C], bearer shall be force downgraded to Rank1 , rrmDlProposedMcs for this transmission shall be calculated using corrected CQI as per 5G_UP_ALG_30473_replaced([11126067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and Rank 1. [**PR825506]If computed rrmDlProposedMcs is higher than NRCELL.maxMCSVoNRRankAndMCSRestrict, rrmDlProposedMcs shall be set to NRCELL.maxMCSVoNRRankAndMCSRestrict. [End PR825506]****_*_** *****



[PR831117]With NRCELL.actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP set to TRUE, and during transient phase, MCS limitation shall not be applied to 5QI1 data.Rationale: CB009550 has not specified the handling of bearers in transient phase (except SRB) which are scheduled in initial BWP. Due to this gap introduced with CB009550 the MCS limitation can’t be extended to 5QI1 bearer with NRCELL.actDlForceRankDowng set to dlVoNROnly or dlVonrSrbVoNRCtrl or dlVonrSrbVoNRCtrNonGbrBr. [End PR831117]

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11125491`)

Initial MCS Max code rate check

* * *

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11125500`)

Initial MCS Due to code rate restrictions defined by 3GPP TS 38.214 chapter 5.1.3, which request a code rate smaller than or equal to 0.95, and the effectively applied code rate in DL transmission (the effectively applied code rate is higher than the target code rate) some of the MCS/PRB combinations can’t be used in scheduling. Hence a code rate check shall be applied and the MCS/PRB combinations with a code rate greater than 0.95 shall be excluded by the DL link adaptation and hence in DL scheduling for configurations for that the PTRS is activated by means of the parameter dlPtrsFlag.

{The effective code rate is defined as in 5G_UP_ALG_2967_replaced([11124515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqrdc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).}

If NRCELL.actDlMuMimo is true, and dlDataDmrsFdmAllowed decided in 5G_UP_ALG_4405_replaced([11142329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr3wSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is FALSE

nb_cdm_no_data (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_2967_replaced([11124515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqrdc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to calculate the code rate of MCSi is 2

else

nb_cdm_no_data (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_2967_replaced([11124515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqrdc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to calculate the code rate of MCSi is determined by MCSi(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_3054_replaced([11129150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq6uCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The effective code rate of the MCS/PRB combinations with deactivated PTRS are always equal or below 0.95. Details with respect to the parameter dlPtrsFlag are specified in 5G_UP_ALG_4666_replaced([11192615](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlyqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB009784-A] Note: DL PTRS is disabled for FR1 TDD and FDD [End CB009784-A]

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11125506`)

Initial MCS Early DL OLLA

* * *

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11125513`)

Initial MCS For the time between the UE is instantiated and until the first CQI report is received as well as for the case that the last CQI report is outdated, the OLLA itself should be able to adapt the MCS in a special earlyDllaMode:

In this mode, the MCS starts with the default MCS, corrected by rrmDeltaCqi. The latter is (re-) initialized by the value of iniDeltaCqi. The parameter rrmDeltaCqi and iniDeltaCqi are defined in 5G_UP_ALG_17528_replaced([11129358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78KfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

To be able to fast adapt the full MCS range by the OLLA (see 5G_UP_ALG_3060_replaced([11129299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the values of NRCELL.dllaDeltaCqiMin, NRCELL.dllaDeltaCqiMax, NRCELL.dllaDeltaCqiStepDown and rrmDeltaCqiStepUp (see 5G_UP_ALG_3064_replaced([11129684](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq9KQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are temporarily changed according to the following:

  * The value of NRCELL.dllaDeltaCqiMin is temporarily replaced by the minimum value of -15.

  * The value of NRCELL.dllaDeltaCqiMax is temporarily replaced by the maximum value of 15.

  * The value of NRCELL.dllaDeltaCqiStepdown is temporarily replaced by the value of rdEarlyDllaStepDown (5G_L2_1202_replaced([11185107](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27vkovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

  * The value of rrmDeltaCqiStepUp is temporarily replaced by a value, derived from rdEarlyDllaStepDown in the same way as defined by 5G_UP_ALG_3064_replaced([11129684](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq9KQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).




The evaluation of the DL MCS by the earlyDllaMode shall be stopped with reception of the next CQI report different from CQI0. If so, the rrmDeltaCqi is reset to the value of iniDeltaCqi, as well as the before mentioned values of NRCELL.deltaCqiMin, NRCELL.deltaCqiMax, deltaCqiStepUp and NRCELL.deltaCqiStepDown are reset to their standard values. The reset of the exponential avgCqi filter is described by 5G_UP_ALG_3005_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: It is left open for implementation if the reinitialisation of rrmDeltaCqi is done by the first fetched value of iniDeltaCqi for this UE or always by the current value of iniDeltaCqi.

The default MCS (NRCELL.dllaIniMcs) shall be sent to the DL scheduler if the gNB doesn’t receive a valid wideband CQI report (CQI > 0) and no HARQ A/N feedback for a certain period (rdCqiExpiryTime – see 5G_L2_1026_replaced([11184999](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27u9ifvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and hence the corresponding timer is expired.

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `11125519`)

Initial MCS Early DL OLLA for High reliability

* * *

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11125523`)

Initial MCS For the time between the UE high reliability DRB is instantiated and until the first CQI report is received as well as for the case that the last CQI report is outdated, the OLLA itself should be able to adapt the MCS in a special earlyDllaMode

In this mode, the MCS starts with the default MCS for high reliability, corrected by rrmDeltaCqi for high reliability. The latter is (re-) initialized by the value of iniDeltaCqi for high reliability. The parameter rrmDeltaCqi and iniDeltaCqi are defined in 5G_UP_ALG_17528_replaced([11129358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78KfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

To be able to fast adapt the full MCS range by the OLLA (see 5G_UP_ALG_3060_replaced([11129299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the values of NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaDeltaCqiMinHR, NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaDeltaCqiMaxHR, NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaDeltaCqiStepDownHR and rrmDeltaCqiStepUp (see 5G_UP_ALG_3064_replaced([11129684](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq9KQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for high reliability are temporarily changed according to the following:

  * The value of NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaDeltaCqiMinHR is temporarily replaced by the minimum value of -15.

  * The value of NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaDeltaCqiMaxHR is temporarily replaced by the maximum value of 15.

  * The value of NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaDeltaCqiStepdownHR is temporarily replaced by the value of rdEarlyDllaStepDownHR (5G_UP_ALG_17240_replaced([11138701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krhK9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

  * The value of rrmDeltaCqiStepUp for high reliability is temporarily replaced by a value, derived from rdEarlyDllaStepDownHR (5G_UP_ALG_17240_replaced([11138701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krhK9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in the same way as defined by 5G_UP_ALG_3064_replaced([11129684](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq9KQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).




The evaluation of the DL MCS by the earlyDllaMode shall be stopped with reception of the next CQI report different from CQI0. If so, the rrmDeltaCqi[k=HR] is reset to the value of iniDeltaCqi[k=HR], as well as the before mentioned values of NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.deltaCqiMinHR, NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.deltaCqiMaxHR, rrmDeltaCqiStepUp for high reliability and NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.deltaCqiStepDownHR are reset to their standard values. The reset of the exponential avgCqi filter is described by 5G_UP_ALG_3005_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: It is left open for implementation if the reinitialisation of rrmDeltaCqi is done by the first fetched value of iniDeltaCqi for this UE or always by the current value of iniDeltaCqi.

Early DL OLLA for basic reliability, please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_11046_replaced([11125506](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `11125529`)

Initial MCS Default MCS for high reliability

* * *

---

**3.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11125535`)

Initial MCS The default MCS for high reliability (NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaIniMcsHR) shall be sent to the DL scheduler if the gNB doesn’t receive a valid wideband CQI report (CQI > 0) and no HARQ A/N feedback for a certain period (rdCqiExpiryTimeHR – see 5G_UP_ALG_15465_replaced([11138627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krgj5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and hence the corresponding timer is expired.

VoNR Robustness for Initial Transmission UEs with 5QI-1 Bearers in DL 12688640: VoNR Robustness for Initial Transmission UEs with 5QI-1 Bearers in DL 12688640: VoNR Robustness for Initial Transmission UEs with 5QI-1 Bearers in DL 12688640: VoNR Robustness for Initial Transmission UEs with 5QI-1 Bearers in DL 12688640: VoNR Robustness for Initial Transmission UEs with 5QI-1 Bearers in DL 12688640: VoNR Robustness for Initial Transmission UEs with 5QI-1 Bearers in DL 12688640: VoNR Robustness for Initial Transmission UEs with 5QI-1 Bearers in DL 12688640: VoNR Robustness for Initial Transmission UEs with 5QI-1 Bearers in DL gNB-DU shall enhance the performance of VoNR UE with 5QI1/65/66 bearer (either deprioritizedVoip or voipTraffic) at initial transmission in the following steps, when the resource allocation type 1 (RAT1) for PDSCH is considering allocation of PRBs (NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = TRUE) and enhnaced VoNR is activated i.e. NRCELL.actEnhancedVoNR = TRUE. Hence, gNB-DU shall apply VoNR initial transmission robustness algorithm in downlink (DL) based on spectral efficiency downgrade, when algorithm is activated by setting NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR greater than Zero and less than or equal to 99. As a result, gNB-DU shall apply robustness algorithm through the reduction of SE for initial transmission VoNR UEs (with 5QI1/65/66 bearers either deprioritizedVoip or voipTraffic) in DL. gNB-DU shall improve the performance of VoNR UEs with 5QI1/65/66 traffic at initial transmission by reducing the spectral efficiency (SE) corresponding to the **rrmDlProposedMcs, which represents the proposed MCS in DL by Link adaptation based on single PRB number defined by R &D parameter rdDlPRBMcsSelection (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_29011_replaced([11138974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krjnMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is True according to 5G_UP_ALG_3029_replaced([11126315](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9DPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), **else based on 2 PRBs as per the computation in 5G_UP_ALG_24101_replaced([11125402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs (5G_UP_ALG_24185_replaced([11138819](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kriZEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to TRUE, else based on wideband MCS **considering WB-CQI as per the computation in 5G_UP_ALG_22781_replaced([11125340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5u_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs is set to FALSE . As a result of the SE downgrade, gNB-DU shall increase the number of PRBs based on the new rrmDlProposedMcs value of the UE with 5QI1/65/66 bearers - in combination with other traffic bearers - in order to drain out the UE's buffer as demonstrated in Figure 1. Allocation of more PRBs and lower MCS will lead to reducing the BLER and improve the performance of 5QI1/65/66.****_* This algorithm considers UEs with RAT1 allocations (i.e. PRBs) and dynamicswitch RAT1 UEs. However; the algorithm does not consider dynamicswitch RAT0 UEs. [image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process. The flowchart consists of three rectangular boxes connected by arrows. The first box, at the top, contains the text "Link Adaptation". An arrow points downwards from this box, with the text "rrmDlProposedMcs" written next to it. The second box, in the middle, contains the text "SE Reduction Algorithm". An arrow points downwards from this box, with the text "New rrmDlProposedMcs based on percRedSEDIRat1VoNR" written next to it. The third box, at the bottom, contains the text "DL Scheduler".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7138.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] Figure 1: SE Reduction Algorithm for VoNR Robustness Notes: * SE for each_ *rrmDlProposedMcs is as per 3GPP TS38.214 tables: 64QAM (Table 5.1.3.1-1), 256QAM (Table 5.1.3.1-2) [CB008949-C], 1024QAM (Table 5.1.3.1-4) [End CB008949-C] and low SE64QAM (Table 5.1.3.1-3)** * Spectral Efficiency (SE) value in MCS tables = Modulation order (QM) * Target Code e.g. MCS6 in 256QAM table, SE = 4 * 434/1024 = 1.6953 bit/s/Hz The reduction in SE is implemented by configuration of NIDD parameter NRCELL.**percRedSEDlRat1VoNR, which is multiplied with the corresponding SE of rrmDlProposedMcs in order to achieve a lower SE with lower coding rate as explained in the below equation:** _reducedDlSERat1VoNR = max(proposedDlSE * (1-percRedSEDlRat1VoNR/100), SEofMCS0) ---- (i)_ where: _reducedDlSERat1VoNR is the reduced SE based on SE Downgrade Algorithm_ _proposedDlSE is the corresponding SE value of_ _rrmDlProposedMcs_**_SEofMCS0 is the SE of MCS0 _The_ reducedDlSERat1VoNR shall be rounded down to nearest lowest valid SE value from MCS tables for the reason that there are discrete values of SE for MCS indices. After updating the value of _reducedDlSERat1VoNR, the corresponding_ _rrmDlProposedMcs value will replace the older value of_ *rrmDlProposedMcs in the link adaptation algorithm in 5G_UP_ALG_24101_replaced([11125402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs is set to TRUE, else in 5G_UP_ALG_22781_replaced([11125340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5u_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs is set to FALSE._****_If NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR is set to Zero, then the value of_ reducedDlSERat1VoNR in above equation will not change and the corresponding **rrmDlProposedMcs will not change. This implies that the SE reduction algorithm for VoNR robustness is deactivated.**_The SE downgrade algorithm is lower bounded to MCS0 and its corresponding SE, where if a UE has an_ *rrmDlProposedMcs with Zero value, then gNB-DU can not apply the SE downgrade algorithm in this case.** [CB014474-B] The above define algorithm is helpful when the user is in a good channel condition where it can be granted higher MCS. Due to higher MCS usage, likely that few PRBs would be enough to drain the buffered bytes, which could potentially degrade the robustness for the user. However, with this approach there is a chance that excess PRBs could be used if the value of the NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR is significantly large followed by further buffer based MCS downgrade which can negatively impact the spectral efficiency. If there are more VoNR users in the slot this can cause starvation due to lack of enough PRBs and can impact e2e performance.The impact can be more exaggerated if the user is at cell-edge for which the initial rrmDlProposedMcs could already be quite lower in practice. To avoid this issue, the above described SE downgrade algorithm shall be enhanced as described below. At step(1), gNB-DU shall calculate the number of PRBs required to drain the buffered bytes for the user with the initial rrmDLProposedMcs and rank using RAT0 granularity (RBG size) as shown below: upperLimitPrbsNoSERedVoNRRat1 = ceil(NumPrbs/RBG_size)_RBG_size, and shall choose the minimum between this value and the one derived using the rrmDlProposedMcs derived from the downgraded SE_ reducedDlSERat1VoNR _As step(2), using equation (i) i.e SE downgrade algorithm to determine the downgraded SE_ reducedDlSERat1VoNR, gNB-DU shall redetermine the downgraded rrmDlProposedMcs. gNB-DU shall then determine the required number of PRBs to drain the buffered bytes using this downgraded rrmDlProposedMcs and initial rank. Let’s call it as numPrbsSERedVoNRRat1._gNB-DU shall take the minimum of the values calculated using the in step(1) and step(2) and allocate that to the VoNR user requiredPrbs = min (upperLimitPrbsNoSERedVoNRRat1, numPrbsSERedVoNRRat1) The above SE downgrade algorithm shall be applied to users with VoNR bearer alone or multiple bearers along with one VoNR bearer. [End CB014474-B]__Examples are demonstrated in 5G_UP_ALG_25586_replaced([11125577](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH3_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))____Simulation results are available in: ** Rationale: * Input: * Activation of NRCELL.actEnhancedVoNR =TRUE and RAT1 for PDSCH (NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = TRUE) * Configuration of NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR greater than Zero and less than or equal to 99 * [CB014474-B] Configuration of NRCELL.actPrbLimForVoNrRat1 = True [End CB014474-B] * Full buffer UE with initial transmission VoNR traffic (5QI1/65/66 bearer either deprioritizedVoip or voipTraffic) in addition to any other bearers (GBR/non-GBR, ..), if they are available (Note: If VoNR UE has HR bearer, then gNB-DU shall treat the bearer as BR bearer). *__rrmDlProposedMcs from 5G_UP_ALG_24101_replaced([11125402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs is set to TRUE, else from 5G_UP_ALG_22781_replaced([11125340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5u_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs is set to FALSE .__* Algorithm: * Consider_ _rrmDlProposedMcs value from 5G_UP_ALG_24101_replaced([11125402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs is set to TRUE, else from 5G_UP_ALG_22781_replaced([11125340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5u_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs is set to FALSE before __rrmDlProposedMcs is sent to the DL scheduler_** _* Apply the SE downgrade algorithm * [CB014474-B] Apply the maximum limit check on granted PRBs. [End CB014474-B] * Conditions * SE value after downgrade is the nearest lowest value from MCS table * Lower bound: new reduced SE is greater than or equal to SE of MCS0 * Output: * New_ *rrmDlProposedMcs and return the new value to the Link adaptation in 5G_UP_ALG_24101_replaced([11125402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqugxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs is set to TRUE, else 5G_UP_ALG_22781_replaced([11125340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5u_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when rdEnablePropMcsCalcBasedOn2Prbs is set to FALSE to be sent to the DL scheduler** * Number of PRBs are selected to drain out the buffer of the UE based on the relation between (TBS, number of PRBs, MCS) The parameter **rrmDlProposedMcs represents the proposed MCS and is defined by 5G_UP_ALG_3045_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

---

##### 3.8.2.1.2.0-2 VoNR Robustness for Initial Transmission UEs with 5QI-1 Bearers in DL Examples (ID: 11125577)

**3.8.2.1.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11125583`)

VoNR Robustness for Initial Transmission UEs with 5QI-1 Bearers in DL Consider the configuration of the following NIDD parameters: NRCELL.actEnhancedVoNR = TRUE AND NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = TRUE AND (NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR is greater than Zero and less than or equal to 99 for activation of SE Downgrade Algorithm in 5G_UP_ALG_25588_replaced([11125547](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH3PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For the following examples, it is assumed that MCS256QAM table from 3GPP TS 38.214 section 5.1.3.1, Table 5.1.3.1-2, SEofMCS0 = 0.2344 bit/s/Hz, UE with full buffer VoNR traffic (5QI-1 bearers):

Example 1

If **rrmDlProposedMCS for a UE is equal to 14, then the corresponding SE is 3.6094 bit/s/Hz. If NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR is set to 25, then:**

reducedDlSERat1VoNR = max(0.2344, 3.6094*(1-25/100)) = 2.70705 bit/s/Hz

Since, SE values of MCS indices are discrete, the nearest lowest valid SE value is 2.5703 bit/s/Hz, the new corresponding value of **rrmDlProposedMcs is 10.**

Example 2

If **rrmDlProposedMCS for a UE is equal to 27, then the corresponding SE is 7.4063 bit/s/Hz. If NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR is set to 25, then:**

reducedDlSERat1VoNR = max(0.2344, 7.4063*(1-25/100)) = 5.55473 bit/s/Hz

Since, the nearest valid SE value in the table is 5.5547 bit/s/Hz, the new corresponding value of **rrmDlProposedMcs is 21.**

**Note: Setting NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR to 25 in examples 1 & 2 has a different impact on MCS values based on the corresponding SE values i.e. MCS is downgraded 4 steps in example 1, while 6 downgrade steps are achieved in example 2.**

Example 3

Number of PRBs for 5QI-1 = ceil (Number of TBS bits in UE buffer/(SE * REsperPRB))

Let's consider for a certain cell configuration for example REsperPRB = 120 REs, where REsperPRB is the number of REs per PRB for PDSCH

If the UE has TBS = 2000 bits and SE = 3.6094 bits/s/Hz for MCS14, then:

Number of PRBs = ceil(2000/(3.6094 * 120)) = 5 PRBs, 

If NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR to 25, reducedDlSERat1VoNR = max(0.2344,3.6094*(1-25/100)) = 2.70705 bit/s/Hz

Since, the nearest lowest valid SE value is 2.5703 bit/s/Hz, the corresponding new value of **rrmDlProposedMcs is 10.**

After SE reduction, number of PRBs = ceil(2000/(2.5703* 120)) = 7 PRBs

Example 4

If **rrmDlProposedMcs for a UE is 0 and _NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR = 25, then there is no further SE downgrade beyond MCS0. Hence, **rrmDlProposedMcs will be 0 and it will not change._** **

Example 5

If _NRCELL.percRedSEDlRat1VoNR = 0, then there is no SE downgrade i.e. SE Downgrade Algorithm is deactivated. Hence,__rrmDlProposedMcs will will not change._ **

The parameter rrmDlProposedMcs represents the proposed MCS and is defined by 5G_UP_ALG_3045_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.8.2.1.3 WB-CQI Averaging

##### 3.8.2.1.3.0-1 WB-CQI Averaging Averaging of the WB-CQI reports (ID: 11125595)

**3.8.2.1.3.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `13471097`)

WB-CQI Averaging 13471097: WB-CQI Averaging 13471097: WB-CQI Averaging 13471097: WB-CQI Averaging 13471097: WB-CQI Averaging 13471097: WB-CQI Averaging 13471097: WB-CQI Averaging This requirement version generalizes the scope for multiple SCells i.e. SCell -> SCells(s) The Inner Loop Link Adaptation shall capture all WB-CQI reports, which are carried via UCI from the UE i for carrier j to the gNB ~~and forwarded to 5G-L2-PS by the UlData_PucchReceiveRespPs message~~ and shall average all these WB-CQI reports. In other words, the WB-CQI reports shall be averaged separately per UE’s code word. For FDD + FR1 TDD CA and for FR1 CA, 5G-L2-PS P(S)Cell will forward the WB-CQI report to Scell(s) 5G-L2-PS through PsPeerCtrl interface according to 5G_UP_ALG_3617_replaced([11160013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktNNC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_3609_replaced([11159986](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktNNBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). An exponential filter compliant to the following equation shall be used for the averaging of the received WB-CQI reports. **[Before CNI-164101-A]** [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $rrmAvgCqi[i,j, t, k] = min \left( 1 - \frac{1}{rrmNumOfCqiReports}, 1 - \frac{1}{rdAvgCqiFilterConst[k]} \right) \times rrmAvgCqi[i,j, t-1, k] + max \left( \frac{1}{rrmNumOfCqiReports}, \frac{1}{rdAvgCqiFilterConst[k]} \right) \times rrmInstCqi[i, j, t]$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7147.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] [CNI-164101-A] [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in LaTeX. The equation is: $$rrmAvgCqi[i, j, t, k] = \min \left(1 - \frac{1}{rrmNumOfCqiReports}, 1 - \frac{1}{rrmAvgCqiFilterConst[k]}\right) \times rrmAvgCqi[i, j, t-1, k] + \max \left(\frac{1}{rrmNumOfCqiReports}, \frac{1}{rrmAvgCqiFilterConst[k]}\right) \times rrmInstCqi[i, j, t]$$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-04151348.png[/image_path][/image-1] [End CNI-164101-A] with: i: Index of the UE j: Index of the carrier t: Time (UL slot) the CQI report is received k: Link adaptation for basic reliability or high reliability rrmNumOfCqiReports: Number of received WB-CQI reports since start of averaging. Initialized to 1 at reception of first WB-CQI report. [Before CNI-164101-A] rdAvgCqiFilterConst: Filter constant of the exponential filter **separately** for basic reliability and high reliability. (R&D parameter rdAvgCqiFilterConst[k=BR] see 5G_L2_1025_replaced([11185006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27u9ivvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), range of 1, 2, …, 10000 with default 1. rdAvgCqiFilterConst[k=HR] please refer to rdAvgCqiFilterConstHR defined in 5G_UP_ALG_17621_replaced([11138735](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krhK_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [CNI-164101-A] rrmAvgCqiFilterConst[k]: Filter constant of the exponential filter separately for basic reliability and high reliability. The NIDD parameter NRCELL.dlLaAvgCqiFilterConst defines the filter constant of the basic reliability bearer and the NIDD parameter NRCELL/pdschHrLaConf.dlLaAvgCqiFilterConstHR defines the filter constant of the high reliability bearer. Both NIDD parameters can be modified online and shall be applied immediately to all existing and new users. [End CNI-164101-A] rrmAvgCQI: Average WB-CQI (see 5G_UP_ALG_3005_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**_* If actDlCqiIncrRestriction is set to "True" and no UE's DRB is associated with drbPdschLaProfile profile(NRBTS/NRDRB.nrPdschHRLaDN) and rrmRestrictedInstCqi[i,j,t'] is not equal to zero, then rrmInstCqi[i,j,t] shall be calculated for PDSCH LA as follows. Restricted CQI shall not be used for PDCCH LA. Case 1: When there is no rank change indicated or rank change is indicated and it is greater than previously reported rank rrmRestrictedInstCqi[i, j, t] = min( rrmReceivedInstCqi[i, j, t], rrmRestrictedInstCqi[i, j, t'] + allowedCqiIncremen ) Case 2: When there is a rank change indicated and it is lesser than previously reported rank rrmRestrictedInstCqi[i, j, t] = min( rrmReceivedInstCqi[i, j, t], rrmRestrictedInstCqi[i, j, t'] + allowedCqiIncrement + additionalCqiIncrRankRedn) else rrmRestrictedInstCqi[i,j,t] = rrmReceivedInstCqi[i,j,t] rrmInstCqi[i,j,t] = rrmRestrictedInstCqi[i,j,t] Where rrmReceivedInstCqi[i,j,t]: Instantaneous WB-CQI from latest CQI report provided by 5G-L1 UL rrmRestrictedInstCqi[i,j,t']: Previous Restricted Instantaneous WB-CQI report For the transient phase that is corresponding to the period between the first schedule slot that power saving beam used when indicator changed to massive MIMO power saving and a new CQI report received, rrmAvgCqi[i,j,t,k] = rrmAvgCqi[i,j,t,k]+rrmDeltaAvgCqimMimoRTXPowerSaving , where for rrmDeltaAvgCqimMimoRTXPowerSaving calculation, see below formula. 1\. With 64QAM or 64QAM low SE MCS table: rrmDeltaAvgCqimMimoRTXPowerSaving = rdDeltaAvgSINRmMimoRTXPowerSaving/1.9275 2\. With 256QAM MCS table: i. When rrmAvgCqi[i,j,t,k=BR] < 4 rrmDeltaAvgCqimMimoRTXPowerSaving = rdDeltaAvgSINRmMimoRTXPowerSaving/4.307 ii. When rrmAvgCqi[i,j,t,k=BR]>= 4 rrmDeltaAvgCqimMimoRTXPowerSaving = rdDeltaAvgSINRmMimoRTXPowerSaving/1.8384 3\. With 1024QAM MCS table: i. When rrmAvgCqi[i,j,t,k=BR]< 6 rrmDeltaAvgCqimMimoRTXPowerSaving = rdDeltaAvgSINRmMimoRTXPowerSaving/3.2541 ii. When rrmAvgCqi[i,j,t,k=BR]>= 6 rrmDeltaAvgCqimMimoRTXPowerSaving = rdDeltaAvgSINRmMimoRTXPowerSaving/2.0637 ,where rdDeltaAvgSINRmMimoRTXPowerSaving refer to 5G_UP_ALG_14637_replaced([11138607](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krgj4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). To shorten the transient phase for the evaluation of the average WB-CQI, the exponential filter shall use for the first iterations the number of received WB-CQI reports (rrmNumOfCqiReports) instead of the filter constant. The calculation of the average WB-CQI shall start with the reception of the first WB-CQI report (rrmReceivedInstCqi) _*and shall consider all WB-CQI reports for the averaging except the WB-CQI report equal to 0. The CQI report equal to 0 is signalled if the UE cannot satisfy the conditions for reporting the CQI. Hence, the parameter rrmNumOfCqiReports shall not be increased if the reported WB-CQI is equal to 0. Details about the channel quality indicator are specified in 3GPP 38.214 chapter 5.2.2.1.** RrmAvgCQI are separated for HR and BR. Both of them shall be initialized as 0 when UE context set up. On receipt of CQI, L2-PS shall update both rrmAvgCqi[HR] and rrmAvgCqi[BR] For the transient phase that is corresponding to the period between receiving RRC Reconfiguration and RRC Reconfiguration Complete, if mcs-table (as in 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2246_replaced([11282471](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpfBz_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in RRC Reconfiguration is different than that stored and the corresponding CQI table is also updated, that is, reconfigure between 64QAM/64QAMLowSE<->256QAM or 256QAM<->1024QAM or 64QAM/64QAMLowSE<->1024QAM, \- ILLA does not feeds the exponential filter with the captured CQI reports in the transient phase. \- ILLA shall not map the last valid CQI received before transient phase to a new CQI value. This is a conservative approach as remapping to a new CQI value can potentially increase the MCS value e.g if the mcs-table that is stored before the RRC Reconfiguration is higher than the one used during the transient phase i.e 64QAM, so it is recommended to avoid the remapping during the transient phase. \- Post the transient phase, the last valid CQI received before transient phase shall be remapped to a new CQI value for new MCS-table according to 5G_UP_ALG_24684_replaced([11129204](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq7VEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The exponential filter shall be reset and the averaging of the WB-CQI reports shall be initialized to the new CQI value. The re-initialization of the exponential filter requests the reset of rrmNumOfCqiReports to 1. Otherwise if the transient phase is related to a mcs-table reconfiguration which doesn’t imply a change of the CQI table (64QAM <-> 64QAMLowSE), \- ILLA continue to feed the exponential filter with the captured CQI reports in the transient phase Note: 1\. After CQI mapping in above, the new mapped CQI value shall not be regarded as outdated, that is, this UE shall not enter into early mode. If there is no valid CQI received before transient, UE shall enter into early mode according to 5G_UP_ALG_11046_replaced([11125506](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G_UP_ALG_14429_replaced([11125519](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). 2\. Both average WB-CQI for high reliability and basic reliability shall be updated when UE is configured with HR DRBs. 3\. current only cqi-table1 will be used for 64QAMLowSE MCS table, above requirement need to be updated when cqi table 3 is supported for 64QAMLowSE MCS table. 4\. AvgCQI used for PDSCH MCS selection shall follow the UE LA mode. The exponential filter shall be reset whenever a rank change occurs in DL and shall resume the averaging of the WB-CQI reports. The reinitialization of the exponential filter requests the reset of rrmNumOfCqiReports to 1 before the exponential filter is fed with the received WB-CQI report. Whenever the gNB layer doesn’t receive any WB-CQI report and no HARQ A/N feedback is received, which corresponds to any DL transmission, the average WB-CQI shall be obsolete and the exponential filter shall be reinitialized by reception of the next instantaneous WB-CQI report. A WB-CQI report carrying a report of 0 is treated as a non-existent WB-CQI report and can’t prolong the time until the exponential filter is reinitialized. The reinitialization of the exponential filter and rrmRestrictedInstCqi[i,j,t']**requests the reset of rrmNumOfCqiReports to 1 before the exponential filter is fed with the received WB-CQI report. The time after that the exponential filter shall be reinitialized is defined by the R &D parameter rdCqiExpiryTime (5G_L2_1026_replaced([11184999](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27u9ifvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for basic reliability LA and R&D parameter rdCqiExpiryTimeHR(5G_UP_ALG_15465_replaced([11138627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krgj5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for high reliability LA.** L2 PS need to parse the wideband CQI report per 5G_UP_ALG_3626_replaced([11160044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktN0EfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for each activated Scell and judge the Scell overheating status for activation according to 5G_UP_ALG_2871_replaced([11123293](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqiTgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or deactivation according to 5G_UP_ALG_2874_replaced([11123313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqiThPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for WB-CQI report carrying a report of 0. [image-2][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process for recalculating the average CQI (Channel Quality Indicator). The flowchart begins with a "Start" node labeled "Recalculation of average CQI". From there, a diamond-shaped decision node asks "tdCqiExpiryTime expired". If the condition is "yes" (expired), the process proceeds to a rectangular node labeled "Initialize exponential filter (rrmAvgCqi)". If the condition is "no" (not expired), the process proceeds to a rectangular node labeled "(re)startrdCqiExpiryTime". After either the initialization or the restart of the expiry time, the process moves to a rectangular node labeled "Calculate and store average CQI (rrmAvgCqi)". Finally, the flowchart ends with an "End" node labeled "Recalculation of average CQI". There is also a separate rectangular node on the right side of the flowchart labeled "Initialize exponential filter (rrmAvgCqi)". This node appears to be connected to the "yes" branch of the decision node, but its exact placement and connection are slightly ambiguous without explicit arrows. However, based on standard flowchart conventions, it's likely intended to be executed when the expiry time has expired.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7147.002.png[/image_path][/image-2] Recalculation of the average WB CQI File: Drawings for 5GC000517_5GC001796_ DLLA_and_ULLA.vsdx Folder: **NRCELL.actDlCqiIncrRestriction** **NRCELL.dlCqiRestriction.allowedCqiIncrement** **NRCELL.dlCqiRestriction.additionalCqiIncrRankRedn**

---

#### 3.8.2.1.4 Averaged WB_CQI correction

##### 3.8.2.1.4.0-1 Averaged WB_CQI correction Correct average WB-CQI (ID: 11125689)

**3.8.2.1.4.0-1.0-11**  (ID: `12817309`)

Averaged WB_CQI correction The average WB-CQI measurement (5G_UP_ALG_3005_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) may be biased by systematic errors. To eliminate the systematic errors from the CQI reports, the average WB-CQI shall be corrected by summation with the delta CQI coming from the OLLA (5G_UP_ALG_3060_replaced([11129299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and MU-MIMO compensation, etc. 

The rule for calculating the corrected average WB-CQI is:

**_rrmCorrAvgCqi[i,j,t,k] = rrmAvgCqi[i,j,t,k] + rrmDeltaCqi[i,j,t,k] +rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo[i,j,t,k] + rrmBlerCorrectionValue_** \+ rrmCqiJitterDl - rrmCqiOffset[i,j,t]**\+ rrmDeltaCqiForMcsDowngrade[i,j,t,k]******* *

_\- rrmRIMReductionCqi[i, j, t] + rrmDeltaCqiDlMuZf[i, j, t, p]_

with

i: Index of the UE

j: Index of the carrier

t: Time when the summands change the value

k: Link adaptation for basic reliability or high reliability  
p: Index of MU pairing group (see)

rrmCorrAvgCqi: Corrected average WB-CQI

rrmAvgCqi: Average WB-CQI (see 5G_UP_ALG_3005_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rrmDeltaCqi: Delta CQI calculated for UE i and carrier j (see 5G_UP_ALG_17349_replaced([11129351](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78KPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo: Multi-user MIMO delta average CQI calculated for UE i and carrier j

rrmBlerCorrectionValue: Slot-specific BLER correction value

rrmCqiJitterDl Dl specific random jitter value

rrmCqiOffset: CQI offset for high reliability LA, this value shall be calculated according to 5G_UP_ALG_14545_replaced([11126081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).  
rrmDeltaCqiDlMuZf: Delta CQI calculated for UE i and carrier j at DL pairing group p ([11142569: DL pairing group](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-Y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

If NRCELL.actDlMuMimo = True **or NRCELL.actDlMuMimoEnh = True, the value rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo[i,j,t] in the formula corrects average CQI in certain slots for DL MU-MIMO according to 5G_UP_ALG_7555_replaced([11126154](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). This value shall be 0 for high reliability.**

rrmDeltaCqiForMcsDowngrade: special delta cqi value for MCS downgrade for high reliability LA, this value shall be calculated according to 5G_UP_ALG_25831_replaced([11129961](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq9xX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). This value shall 0 for basic reliability LA.

The parameter rrmBlerCorrectionValue[i,j,t] increase/decrease the corrected average CQI in certain slots as specified in 5G_UP_ALG_3012_replaced([11126056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

_rrmRIMReductionCqi: Correction to the WB CQI due to the RIM related DL power reduction for the UE in slot. See 5G_UP_ALG_29926_replaced([11126166](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV_vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for method of its calculation._

The parameter rrmCqiJitterDl is added to the corrected average CQI to occasionally probe for higher MCS to check if the probed MCS hits any flood of NACKs and thus tries to prevent switching to this higher MCS as it will cause sudden BLER increase. It is a value given by two parameters, one is a pseudo random number, and the other is defined by rdCqiJitterDl (5G_UP_ALG_17571_replaced([11138437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreuvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for basic reliability or rdCqiJitterDlHR (5G_UP_ALG_22115_replaced([11138790](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krhyCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for high reliability which gives the maximum jitter which is added. If the probed MCS is ACKed, the usual OLLA correction would continue to increase the rrmDeltaCqi by stepping up, and can help in increasing the average throughput, else if it is NACKed, the OLLA would step-down and stop increasing the MCS. **For users with active 5QI1/65/66 bearer for voice service, this value should be set to 0. Rationale is that, the jitter is more relevant for users that are continuously scheduled with always data in buffer as this could cause a positive impact to the average throughput, whereas smaller transmissions like VoNR, where the UE is scheduled infrequently would not require this.**

For user which has PDSCH scheduled in TRS slot or SSB slot with TRS, gNB-DU shall apply a reduction factor to the Corrected average WB-CQI as follows

If link Adaptation Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency for DL in FR1 is enabled (see [12472220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_HqPxgA1UEfCuhewgqkUVKQ) ) is True, the proposedMcs is calculated before the FD resource distribution. Therefore, for the case below where the avgCqiRatio depends on the number of allocated PRBs (NRBTS.actsubTRSRateMatch = True and NRCELL.actRat1Alloc=True), it is handled as described in [11125402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_D_DOYPvKEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 

rrmCorrAvgCqiInTrs = rrmCorrAvgCqi*avgCqiRatio, where

in FDD, if NRCELL.actTrsPdschAvgCqiRatio = TRUE

or

in TDD FR1, if NRBTS.actSubTRSRateMatch = FALSE and csirsTrackingNumOfPrbs = 52 RBs (see [11275518](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpHOUPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpHOUPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9")) and cell BW after PRB blanking is larger than 52 RBs

{

  * avgCqiRatio is equal to rdTrsPdschAvgCqiRatio (5G_L2_6735_replaced([11184908](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27uWefvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));



}

else if NRBTS.actSubTRSRateMatch is TRUE, 

{

\- when NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True and the number of PRBs igranted for the PDSCH scrambled by DCI 1_1 is less than 4 PRBs, 

\- avgCqiRatio is equal to rdTrsSmallPdschAvgCqiRatio (5G_UP_ALG_31623_replaced([11138899](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krjAIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- otherwise 

\- avgCqiRatio is equal to rdTrsPdschAvgCqiRatio1 (5G_UP_ALG_26001_replaced([11138890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krjAIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

} 

otherwise

{ 

When NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True,

  * avgCqiRatio is computed in 5G_UP_ALG_26487_replaced([11125193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqtSo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))



When NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False,

  * avgCqiRatio is equal to 1 (ie no reduction applied)



}

If **[Before CB015668-A] NRBTS.actTddCsiImRm = TRUE**[CB015668-A] actTddCsiImRm = TRUE (note: actTddCsiImRm is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator) **[End CB015668-A]** ***_, for user which has PDSCH scheduled in CSI-IM slot, rrmCorrAvgCqiInCsiIm is used instead of rrmCorrAvgCqi in this slot as Corrected average WB-CQI, where rrmCorrAvgCqiInCsiIm = rrmCorrAvgCqi_ rdCsiImPdschAvgCqiRatio (5G_UP_ALG_26009_replaced([11138909](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krjAJPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

If NRCELL.actEnhancedPDSCHSchSplSlot is TRUE; For FR1 TDD, for users which has PDSCH scheduled in a slot with S+L < 8, rrmCorrAvgCqiInBi is used instead of rrmCorrAvgCqi in this slot as Corrected average WB-CQI;

rrmCorrAvgCqiInBi= rrmCorrAvgCqi*NRCELL.biSlotPdschAvgCqiRatio;

Editor’s hint: The trigger for updating the corrected average CQI (rrmCorrAvgCqi) is the reception of a new CQI report or the reception of a new HARQ feedback and hence the corrected average CQI shall be recalculated whenever one of the input parameter changes. Note that the jitter is not added in those slots when the corrected average CQI (rrmCorrAvgCqi) is updated by ILLA to determine the proposed MCS**.**

---

##### 3.8.2.1.4.0-2 Averaged WB_CQI correction Conversion between CQI and SINR (ID: 11126006)

**3.8.2.1.4.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12726344`)

Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12726344: 12726344: Averaged WB_CQI correction Here is some background information about CQI/SINR conversion. The below simulation results are from 5GMax. 1\. CQI and SINR mapping table [Before CB008949-A] without 256QAM [CB008949-A] with 64QAM [End CB008949-A]

---|---|---|---|---|---  
**CqiIndex** | **Prb4** | **Prb64** | **Prb128** | **Prb256** | **Modulation**  
1 | -4.94 | -6.64 | -6.1 | -6.1 | QPSK  
2 | -3.82 | -4.12 | -4.11 | -4.1 | QPSK  
3 | -1.89 | -2.11 | -2.1 | -2.62 | QPSK

6 | 3.26 | 3.85 | 3.9 | 3.9 | QPSK  
7 | 5.82 | 5.38 | 6.46 | 6.75 | 16QAM  
8 | 7.32 | 7.47 | 7.56 | 7.9 | 16QAM

6 | 9.56 | 9.9 | 9.9 | 9.9 | 16QAM  
7 | 11.61 | 11.49 | 11.54 | 11.62 | 64QAM

[CB008949-A] |  |  |  |  |   
3\. CQI and SINR mapping table with 1024QAM |  |  |  |  |   
|  |  |  |  |   
---|---|---|---|---|---  
CqiIndex | PRB4 | PRB64 | PRB128 | PRB256 | Modulation  
1 | -3.24 | -3.99 | -3.99 | -3.99 | QPSK  
2 | -1.84 | -2.6 | -2.6 | -2.6 | QPSK  
3 | 1.3 | 0.8 | 1.05 | 1.05 | QPSK  
4 | 5.2 | 4.7 | 4.7 | 4.7 | 16QAM  
5 | 8.75 | 8.25 | 8.25 | 8.25 | 16QAM  
6 | 10.9 | 11.95 | 11.95 | 12.2 | 64QAM  
7 | 14.5 | 13.75 | 13.75 | 13.75 | 64QAM  
8 | 16.3 | 15.8 | 15.8 | 15.8 | 64QAM

To simplify the conversion between CQI and SINR, we get below conversion equations with linear fitting of LS method. These equations do NOT consider number of PRB(s) as a factor because conversion result is almost irrelevant with number of PRB(s). |  |  |  |  |   
1\. [Before CB008949-A] without 256QAM [CB008949-A] with 64QAM [End CB008949-A]: |  |  |  |  |   
SINR = 1.9275*CQI – 7.7624 |  |  |  |  |   
CQI = (SINR + 7.7624)/1.9275 |  |  |  |  |   
2\. With 256QAM: |  |  |  |  |   
i. When (CQI < 4) or (SINR < 6.135): |  |  |  |  |   
SINR = 4.307*CQI-10.795 |  |  |  |  |   
CQI = (SINR + 10.795)/4.307 |  |  |  |  |   
ii. When (CQI >= 4) or (SINR >= 6.135): |  |  |  |  |   
SINR = 1.8384*CQI-1.0909 |  |  |  |  |   
CQI = (SINR + 1.0909)/1.8384  |  |  |  |  |   
[CB008949-A] |  |  |  |  |   
3\. With 1024QAM: |  |  |  |  |   
i. |  |  |  |  |   
When (CQI < 6) or (SINR < 11.75): |  |  |  |  |   
SINR = 3.2541*CQI-8.0913 |  |  |  |  |   
CQI = (SINR + 8.0913)/3.2541 |  |  |  |  |   
|  |  |  |  |   
ii. |  |  |  |  |   
When (CQI >=6) or (SINR >= 11.75): |  |  |  |  |   
SINR = 2.0637*CQI-0.74283 |  |  |  |  |   
CQI = (SINR + 0.74283)/2.0637 |  |  |  |  |   
[End CB008949-A] |  |  |  |  |   
The corresponding figures are shown as below [CB008949-A](Note: "w/o 256QAM" means 64QAM)[End CB008949-A]: |  |  |  |  |   
[image-0][image_desc]The image is a line graph titled "w/o 256QAM CQI to SINR". The x-axis is labeled with numbers from 1 to 15, representing CQI (Channel Quality Indicator). The y-axis is labeled with numbers from -10 to 25, representing SINR (Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio). |  |  |  |  |   
There are two lines plotted on the graph: |  |  |  |  |   
1\. A blue line with circular markers, labeled "Prb256 SINR". This line shows the SINR values for different CQI values. |  |  |  |  |   
2\. An orange line with circular markers, representing a linear fit, with the equation "linear (SINR = 1.9275*CQI-7.7624)" displayed in the legend. |  |  |  |  |   
Both lines show a generally increasing trend, indicating that as the CQI increases, the SINR also tends to increase. The two lines are very close to each other, suggesting that the linear model is a good approximation of the relationship between CQI and SINR in this context. |  |  |  |  |   
The data points appear to be: |  |  |  |  |   
\- For "Prb256 SINR": |  |  |  |  |

\- CQI 1: ~-5.83 |  |  |  |  |   
\- CQI 2: ~-3.90 |  |  |  |  |

The graph visually represents the relationship between CQI and SINR, and how a linear model fits this relationship.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7160.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] |  |  |  |  |   
[image-1][image_desc]The image is a line graph titled "with 256QAM CQI to SINR". The x-axis is labeled with numbers from 1 to 15, representing CQI values. The y-axis is labeled with numbers from -10 to 30, representing SINR values. |  |  |  |  |   
There are two lines plotted on the graph: |  |  |  |  |   
1\. A blue line labeled "Prb256" with circular markers. |  |  |  |  |   
2\. An orange line labeled "piece wise" with circular markers. |  |  |  |  |   
Both lines show a generally increasing trend, indicating a positive correlation between CQI and SINR. The two lines are very close to each other, suggesting that the "piece wise" representation is a good approximation of the "Prb256" data. |  |  |  |  |   
Below the graph, there are two equations provided: |  |  |  |  |   
\- SINR = 4.307*CQI - 10.795 |  |  |  |  |   
\- SINR = 1.8384*CQI - 1.0909 |  |  |  |  |   
These equations likely represent the mathematical models or approximations used for the plotted lines.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7160.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] |  |  |  |  |   
[CB008949-A] |  |  |  |  |   
[image-2][image_desc]The image is a scatter plot showing the relationship between CQI (Channel Quality Indicator) and SINR (Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio) for 1024QAM. The x-axis represents CQI, ranging from 0 to 15, and the y-axis represents SINR, ranging from -5 to 35. |  |  |  |  |   
The plot displays blue dots representing the "Raw data" and a red line representing a "piecewise fitting" of this data. The data points generally show an increasing trend of SINR with increasing CQI. The red line follows this trend, indicating a piecewise linear approximation of the relationship. |  |  |  |  |   
The title of the plot is "With 1024QAM CQI to SINR". The legend in the top left corner clarifies the meaning of the blue dots and the red line. |  |  |  |  |   
The data points are distributed as follows: |  |  |  |  |   
\- Around CQI 1, SINR is approximately -3. |  |  |  |  |   
\- Around CQI 2, SINR is approximately 1. |  |  |  |  |   
\- Around CQI 3, SINR is approximately 3. |  |  |  |  |   
\- Around CQI 4, SINR is approximately 6. |  |  |  |  |   
\- Around CQI 5, SINR is approximately 9. |  |  |  |  |   
\- Around CQI 5.5, SINR is approximately 12. |  |  |  |  |   
\- Around CQI 6.5, SINR is approximately 14. |  |  |  |  |   
\- Around CQI 8, SINR is approximately 17. |  |  |  |  |   
\- Around CQI 10, SINR is approximately 21. |  |  |  |  |   
\- Around CQI 12, SINR is approximately 24. |  |  |  |  |   
\- Around CQI 14, SINR is approximately 27. |  |  |  |  |   
\- Around CQI 15, SINR is approximately 30. |  |  |  |  |   
The piecewise fitting line appears to have segments with different slopes, suggesting that the relationship between CQI and SINR is not perfectly linear across the entire range.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-08041637.png[/image_path][/image-2] |  |  |  |  |
