correction 11126051: Averaged WB_CQI correction 11126051: Averaged WB_CQI correction 11126051: Averaged WB_CQI correction 11126051: Averaged WB_CQI correction 11126051: Averaged WB_CQI correction 11126051: Averaged WB_CQI correction 11126051: Averaged WB_CQI correction 11126051: Averaged WB_CQI correction 11126051: Averaged WB_CQI correction 11126051: Averaged WB_CQI correction 11126051: Averaged WB_CQI correction If NRCELL.actDlMuMimo = True:

The MU-SINR shall be calculated from SU-SINR with the below equation:

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $MU_SINR_k = (\frac{SU_SINR_k}{N}) / (1 + \frac{SU_SINR_k}{N} * dl_pairing_factor_k)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7162.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

where

[image-1][image_desc]The image contains the text "MU_SINRk". The text is in a dark color and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are slightly blurred, and there is a subtle gradient effect on the text, with lighter shades towards the top and darker shades towards the bottom. The background is a light, almost white color with some faint blue and yellow hues, suggesting it might be a digital rendering or a screenshot from a display. The text is positioned centrally within the image.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7162.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] and [image-2][image_desc]SU_SINRk[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7162.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] are linear values of SINR of user k. If SU-SINR is dB value, it needs to be converted to linear value before applying above equation.

[image-3][image_desc]The image contains the text "dl_pairing_factor". The text is in a light color and appears to be slightly blurred. The background is white.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7162.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] is the DL pairing factor of user k which is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4387_replaced([11142153](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr3JM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

N is the number of users in the PairSet in current TTI (see 5G_UP_ALG_4366_replaced([11141973](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr2iIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Please note: To simplify the calculation of MU-SINR, all users in the same user group can use the same value of dl_pairing_factor for the primary user which is calculated during DL pairing algorithm.

If NRCELL.actDlMuMimoEnh = True:

The MU-SINR of paired UE shall be calculated from SU-SINR with equations defined in 5G_UP_ALG_7438_replaced([11143643](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_7440_replaced([11143425](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6MjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 3.8.2.1.4.0-4 Averaged WB_CQI correction CQI correction during slots indicated by increased/decreased BLER (ID: 11126056)

**3.8.2.1.4.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `12721056`)

Averaged WB_CQI correction In certain DL slots, the measured BLER is systematically increased or decreased and hence affects negatively the average corrected CQI and as well the MCS selection. This distortion affects only the DL transmission if the rank proposed by the DL SU-MIMO is Rank3 or Rank4 and [Before CB008949-A] the proposed MCS relates to the 256QAM MCS index table and is greater than MCS19 [CB008949-A] Qm>=8 (which is corresponding to proposed MCS > 19 for 256QAM MCS index table or proposed MCS > 14 for 1024QAM MCS index table ) [End CB008949-A]. Basically, for TDD FR1 with a DL:UL ratio of 4:1 the BLER is increased systematically during the first DL slot following an UL slot and decreased during the special slots (S slots) with a reduced number of DL symbols.

To mitigate this effect the average corrected CQI shall be adapted during slots with increased or rather decreased BLER and if the proposed rank is Rank3 or Rank4 and the proposed MCS [Before CB008949-A] relates to an MCS of the 256QAM MCS index table and is greater than MCS19 [CB008949-A] is greater than 19 for 256QAM MCS index table, or greater than 14 for 1024QAM MCS index table [End CB008949-A]. For this purpose, NIDD parameters provide the CQI correction values, which shall be applied in calculating the average corrected CQI. The NIDD parameter NRBTS.slotLAHighBlerCorrectionFactor defines the CQI correction value for the slots with increased BLER and the NIDD parameter NRBTS.slotLALowBlerCorrectionFactor the CQI correction value for the slots with decreased BLER. Furthermore, the NIDD parameters NRBTS.slotLAHighBlerSlots and NRBTS.slotLALowBlerSlots define the slots, for which the average corrected CQI shall be adapted. Bit 0 of the NIDD parameters NRBTS.slotLAHighBlerSlots and NRBTS.slotLALowBlerSlots shall be aligned with SFN=0, subframe=0 of the frame structure and shall be repeated continuously after Bit 19. The CQI adaptation as such can be activated/deactivated by the NIDD parameters NRBTS.slotLAHighBlerCorrectionFactor and NRBTS.slotLALowBlerCorrectionFactor. If both parameters are set to 0 the CQI adaptation is deactivated. The CQI adaptation is activated independently for slots with high BLER and slots with low BLER if the corresponding NIDD parameter NRBTS.slotLAHighBlerCorrectionFactor or NRBTS.slotLALowBlerCorrectionFactor is set greater than 0.

The average corrected CQI shall be adapted by the parameter rrmBlerCorrectionValue, which is calculated by the following rule:

  * If NRBTS.slotLAHighBlerCorrectionFactor >0 and NRBTS.slotLAHighBlerSlots[i] = 1 for i=0,..19 and (proposed MCS would be > MCS19 of the 256QAM MCS index table [CB008949-A] or > MCS14 of the 1024QAM MCS index table [End CB008949-A]) and proposed Rank = Rank3 or Rank4, then
  * rrmBlerCorrectionValue = - NRBTS.slotLAHighBlerCorrectionFactor;
  * If NRBTS.slotLALowBlerCorrectionFactor >0 and NRBTS.slotLALowBlerSlots[i] = 1 for i=0,..19 and (proposed MCS would be > MCS19 of the 256QAM MCS index table [CB008949-A] or > MCS14 of the 1024QAM MCS index table [End CB008949-A]) and proposed Rank = Rank3 or Rank4, then
  * rrmBlerCorrectionValue = NRBTS.slotLALowBlerCorrectionFactor;
  * Otherwise, rrmBlerCorrectionValue = 0;



with

i: Index of (subframe mod 20) operation

rrmBlerCorrectionValue: Slot-specific BLER correction value

---

##### 3.8.2.1.4.0-5 Averaged WB_CQI correction CQI correction for VoNR UE during Rank change (ID: 11126067)

**3.8.2.1.4.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `12764788`)

Averaged WB_CQI correction If actDlForceRankDowng is set to dlVoNROnly/dlVoNRSrbVoNRCtrl/dlVonrSrbVoNRCtrNonGbrBr (applicable for FDD only) for a transmission with 5QI1 (without SRB1/SRB2/5QI5), a correction shall be applied to the rrmCorrAvgCQI as following:

rrmCorrAvgCqi = Fun(rrmCorrAvgCqi),

Where,

Fun(x) = min (p1 * x + p0, 15)

rrmCorrAvgCqi is as calculated in 5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Through simulation (refer FSY0862022) p0 and p1 is identified corresponding to existing 64QAM, 256 QAM [CB008949-C], 1024 QAM [End CB008949-C] and 64 QAM SE CQI-MCS tables, and the values are as below:

64QAM (Applies to 10% BLER & 1% BLER Tables)

Rank 2 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 1.287 

p0 = -1.946 

Rank 3 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 1.304 

p0 = -2.193 

Rank 4 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 2.053 

p0 = -4.053 

256QAM (10% BLER Tables)

Rank 2 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 1.373

p0 = **-1.272**

Rank 3 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 1.628 

p0 = -1.967 

Rank 4 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 1.943 

p0 = -2.726

64QAM (3GPP based)

Rank 2 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 1.253 

p0 = -0.5378 

Rank 3 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 1.546 

p0 = -0.2992

Rank 4 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 1.758 

p0 = -0.01359 

256QAM (3GPP based)

Rank 2 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 1.588 

p0 = -1.246

Rank 3 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 2.166 

p0 = -1.771 

Rank 4 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 1.997 

p0 = -4.599 

[CB008949-C]

1024QAM (3GPP based)

Rank 2 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 1.405 

p0 = -0.991 

Rank 3 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 1.715 

p0 = -1.288 

Rank 4 to Rank 1 mapping:

p1 = 2.024 

p0 = -1.621 

[End CB008949-C]

12764797: Averaged WB_CQI correction rrmCqiOffset(i,j,t) is the cqi shift value used in rrmCorrAvgCqi calculation for high reliability. Please refer 5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The rule to calculate rrmCqiOffset(i,j,t) is: **rrmCqiOffset(i, j, t) = max{ scalefactorCqiOffset* stdCqi(i,j,t), dllaOffset},** with • i: Index of the UE; • j: Index of the carrier; • t: Time (UL slot) the WB-CQI is reported • [image-0][image_desc]The image contains the text "StdCqi(i, j, t)". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be mathematical notation. The letters are in a dark color, possibly black or dark blue, against a light background. The characters are clear and legible.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7168.002.png[/image_path][/image-0] is the long-term standard deviation of DL CQI based on CSI reports. see below formula to calculate. • dllaOffset: offset value obtain from NRCELL.pdschHrLaConfig.dllaCqiOffsetHR. • scalefactorCqiOffset **: scale factor for stdCqi, if rrmNumOfCqiReport is less than rdAvgCqiExpFilterConstHR (see 5G_UP_ALG_15595_replaced([11138639](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krgj6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), this value shall be set as 0. if rrmNumOfCqiReport is greater than or equal to rdAvgCqiExpFilterConstHR, this value shall be set as rdSCaleFactorCqiOffset(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_25237_replaced([11138858](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kriZG_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).** The rule to calculate [image-1][image_desc]The image contains the text "StdCqi(i, j, t)". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be mathematical notation. The letters are in a dark color, possibly black or dark blue, against a light background. The characters are clear and legible.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7168.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] is: [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $StdCqi(i,j,t) = \sqrt{SqrCqi(i,j,t) - (AvgCqi(i,j,t))^2}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7168.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] [image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: SqrCqi(i, j, t) = SqrCqi(i, j, t - 1) * min[(1 - 1/rrmNumOfCqiReports), (1 - 1/rdAvgCqiExpFilterConstHR)] + rrmInstCqi² * max(1/rrmNumOfCqiReports, 1/rdAvgCqiExpFilterConstHR)[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7168.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] [image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in English. The formula is: AvgCqi(i, j,t) = AvgCqi (i, j, t-1) * min (1 - 1/rrmNumOfCqiReports) * (1 - 1/rdAvgCqiExpFilterConstHR) + rrmInstCqi * max (1/rrmNumOfCqiReports, 1/rdAvgCqiExpFilterConstHR)[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7168.005.png[/image_path][/image-4] with • i:Index of the UE; • j:Index of the carrier; • t:Time (UL slot) the WB-CQI is reported • [image-5][image_desc]The image contains the text "AvgCQlinit =". The text is in a dark color against a white background. The letters are clear and legible.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7168.006.png[/image_path][/image-5] [image-6][image_desc]The image contains the text "WB_CQI_init". The text is in a sans-serif font and is black. The background is white.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7168.007.png[/image_path][/image-6]_,_ • [image-7][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written in a light gray font on a white background. The equation is: $SqrCqi_{init} = WB_CQI_{init}^2$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7168.008.png[/image_path][/image-7] • rdAvgCqiExpFilterConstHR (see 5G_UP_ALG_15595_replaced([11138639](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krgj6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): Filter constant of the exponential filter • rrmInstCqi: Instantaneous WB-CQI from latest CQI report [image-8][image_desc]The image contains the text "WB_CQIinit". As part of **Rank-Switching reset the exponential filters [image-9][image_desc]The image contains the text "SqrCqi" in a blurry, light-colored font against a white background. Update [image-11][image_desc]The image contains the text "WB_CQIinit". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be in a mathematical or scientific context. The text is framed by a black border.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7168.010.png[/image_path][/image-13] and [image-14][image_desc]The image contains the text "AvgCqi" in a blurry, light blue font.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7168.011.png[/image_path][/image-14]. In RRC reconfigure transient phase, if the mcs-table in DL is updated together with corresponding cqi-table updated, that is, reconfigure between 256QAM<->64QAM/64QAMLowSE [CB008949-C] or 1024QAM<->64QAM/64QAMLowSE [End CB008949-C], and a new mapped CQI is obtained after remapping, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_3005_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), then:  set [image-15][image_desc]The image contains the text "WB_CQIinit". Please note that rrmCqiOffset value calculation shall not be interrupted when UE is configured with HR DRB.

##### 3.8.2.1.4.0-7 Averaged WB_CQI correction CQI compensation for DL MU-MIMO (ID: 11126154)

**3.8.2.1.4.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `13469983`)

Averaged WB_CQI correction The **rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo represents CQI adjustment value which is introduced by DL MU-MIMO.**

**[Before CB013712-A]**

**If NRCELL.actDlMuMimo = True:**

So far the CQI adjustment only considers the power reduction due to MU pairing. If the transmitting power of PDSCH is different with the power that UE expects (see the RRC configuration of powerControlOffset in CSI-RS for CSI feedback), the WB-CQI will be compensated because UEs measure and report CQI based on signal of CSI-RS for CSI feedback and the extra power offset between PDSCH and CSI-RS is not considered.

Based on MoRSE simulation result, the CQI compensation has gain on cell edge tput but has loss on cell average tput.

If (there are users can be paired in current TTI), the rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo is calculated as below:

1\. Convert WB-CQI to WB-SU-SINR(dB) by the method which is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_3009_replaced([11126006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

2\. Calculate WB-MU-SINR(dB) from WB-SU-SINR(dB) which is specified in 5G_UP_ALG_7556_replaced([11126030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

3\. Convert WB-MU-SINR(dB) to WB-CQI-Estimated by the method defined in 5G_UP_ALG_3009_replaced([11126006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

4\. rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo = WB-CQI-Estimated - WB-CQI

About WB-CQI, see 5G_UP_ALG_3005_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if ILLA is enabled, or 5G_UP_ALG_9475_replaced([11124506](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqrdcfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if ILLA is disabled.

**[End CB013712-A]**

**If NRCELL.actDlMuMimoEnh = True:**

For SU UE, the rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo = 0. For MU UE, the rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo is calculated as below:

1\. WB-SU-CQI equals to rrmCorrAvgCqi (see 5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) without OLLA correction (rrmDeltaCqi = 0).

2\. Convert WB-SU-CQI to WB-SU-SINR by the method which is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_3009_replaced([11126006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

2\. Calculate WB-MU-SINR from WB-SU-SINR which is specified in 5G_UP_ALG_7556_replaced([11126030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

3\. Convert WB-MU-SINR to WB-MU-CQI by the method defined in 5G_UP_ALG_3009_replaced([11126006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

4\. rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo = WB-MU-CQI - WB-SU-CQI.

---

12737452: Averaged WB_CQI correction L2-RT shall calculate for the UE i, carrier (cell) j and slot t, CQI correction associated with RIM power reduction according to following rules:

In the slot t for which RIM power reduction is applicable according to 5G_UP_ALG_29878_replaced([11161157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktSslPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Determine RIM DL power reduction applicability' in the cell corresponding to the carrier j for UE i

_rrmRIMReductionCqi[i, j, t]_ is calculated according to algorithm equivalent to following:**

\- Let [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression written in a blurry, low-resolution format. The expression appears to be a sequence of bracketed pairs, separated by commas. Each pair consists of two terms, likely variables or functions, with subscripts. The sequence starts with `[cqio, cqi₁]`, followed by `[cqi, cqi2]`, and continues with an ellipsis `...`, ending with `[cqin-1,Cqin]`. The terms `cqi` are consistently present, with the subscripts changing in a sequential manner (0, 1, 1, 2, n-1, n). The overall structure suggests a list or a series of related elements.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] be intervals where for applicable CQI table [Before CB008949-A](64 or 256) [End CB008949-A]currently used for UE CQI to SNIR conversion formula in the interval [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in square brackets. The expression is "cqt_{i-1}, cqt_i". The characters are in a sans-serif font and appear to be in a light blue color with a slight glow effect. The background is white.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] for each [image-2][image_desc]The image is a close-up, abstract shot of a gradient. The gradient transitions from a light, warm beige on the left side to a cool, metallic silver-blue in the center, and then to a dark, almost black color on the right. There are subtle hints of purple and orange within the central blue section, suggesting a complex interplay of light and color. The overall effect is smooth and blended, with soft edges and a luminous quality. The composition is vertical, with the colors flowing from left to right.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] such tat [image-3][image_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "1 ≤ l ≤ n". The text is rendered in a sans-serif font with a slight shadow effect. The numbers and letters are clear and legible.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] according to 5G_UP_ALG_3009_replaced([11126006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is of the form [image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, light-colored font on a white background. The equation is: NIR = a₁CQI + b₁[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]

\- Let [image-5][image_desc]The image contains the text "cqtr" with a subscript "r" on the "t". The letters are in a sans-serif font and have a gradient effect, with colors ranging from yellow to blue. The background is black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.006.png[/image_path][/image-5] be average cqi before the correction and [image-6][image_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract representation of what appears to be a bar graph. The bars are rendered in various shades of blue, brown, and white, with a gradient effect. The background is white, fading to a dark gray on the right edge. The overall impression is one of data visualization, but the lack of clarity makes it impossible to discern specific values or labels.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.007.png[/image_path][/image-6] be such that cqi_r belongs to [image-7][image_desc][cqi_{k-1}, cqi_k][/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.008.png[/image_path][/image-7]

\- let [image-8][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $d_k = \min(cqi_r - cq_{i_{k-1}} - \frac{1}{a_k} \cdot rrmRimDLPowerReduction[i, j, t])$ The equation defines a variable $d_k$ as the minimum of a term involving $cqi_r$ and another term involving $cq_{i_{k-1}}$ and a function $rrmRimDLPowerReduction[i, j, t]$ divided by $a_k$.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.009.png[/image_path][/image-8]

\- for l such that [image-9][image_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "k > l > 0". The characters are black and appear to be handwritten or in a font that mimics handwriting. The background is white with some subtle yellow and blue hues, suggesting it might be a screenshot or a digital image with a light source.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.010.png[/image_path][/image-9],

[image-10][image_desc]d_{k-1} =[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.011.png[/image_path][/image-10]

[image-11][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: min((cqi_{k-1} - cqi_{k-1+1}), \frac{1}{a_{k-l+1}} \max(0, rrmRimDLPowerReduction[i,j,t] - \sum_{m=k-l+1}^{k} a_m d_m))[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.012.png[/image_path][/image-11]

as a result reduction is:

[image-12][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: rrmRIMReductionCqi[i,j,t] = $\sum_{m=1}^{k} d_m$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.013.png[/image_path][/image-12]

where

  * _rrmRimDLPowerReduction[i, j, t] is a power reduction as specified by the 5G_UP_ALG_29909_replaced([11161178](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktSsmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))_



Note:

1\. When the CQI to SNIR function is approximated by the single function of the form [image-13][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is: SNIR = A * CQI + B. The letters and symbols are in black and white, with some slight blurring.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.014.png[/image_path][/image-13], what means , what means that approximation is valid in the whole interval [1,15], aforementioned algoritm reduces to [image-14][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is: rrmRIM ReductionCqi[i,j,t] = ArrmRimDLPowerReduction[i, j, t][/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7172.015.png[/image_path][/image-14]

2\. [Before CB008949-A] As for the 25R2 release CQI to SNIR in 5G_UP_ALG_3009_replaced([11126006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is approximated by single function in the single interval in the case of any non-256 CQI table or by two affine functions on two intervals ([1,4] and [4,15]) in the case of 256 QAM tables

[CB008949-A]

CQI to SNIR in [11126006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) is approximated by single function in the single interval in case of 64QAM CQI table or by two affine functions on two intervals in case of 256QAM/1024QAM CQI table

[End CB008949-A]

Examples:

Let's consider UE using 64 QAM CQI table and assume that power reduction for the transmission is 10 dB and averaged CQI before reduction was 12 we have SNIR as a function of CQI is approximated most recent approximations as in the 5G_UP_AL_19802 by function

SINR = 1.9275*CQI – 7.7624

As approximation is valid in whole interval [1,15] we can calculate reduction being the

d1 = 1/1.9275 * 10 = 5.188

Let's consider UE using 256 QAM CQI table and assume that power reduction for the transmission is 10 dB and averaged CQI before reduction was 12 we have SNIR as a function of CQI is approximated most recent approximations as in the 5G_UP_AL_19802 by function

SNIR=4.307*CQI-10.795 in the interval [1, 4], so the linear coefficient there is 4.307

SNIR=1.8384*CQI-1.0909 in the interval [4, 15], so the linear coefficient there is 1.8384

Current CQI is in the second interval (i.e. [4,15]. Following the algorithm we have:

d2 = min(12-4, (1/1.8384)*10) ~ min(8, 5.4395) = 5.4395

d1 = min(4-1, (1/4.307)_max(0, 10-5.4395_ 1.8384)) ~ min(3, 0) = 0.

So reduction is d1+d2 = 5.4395

For the same 256 QAM case but assuming the value of CQI before reduction is 8 we will have:

d2 = min(8-4, (1/1.8384)*10) ~ min(4, 5.4395) = 4

d1 = min(4-1, (1/4.307)_max(0, 10-4_ 1.8384)) ~ min(3, (1/4.307)*2.6464) ~ min(3, 0.614)=0.614.

So, reduction is d2+d1 = 4+0.614 = 4.614

---

##### 3.8.2.1.4.0-9 Averaged WB_CQI correction CQI correction for non-Gbr with basic reliability only UE in FDD for during rank downgrade (ID: 11126236)

**3.8.2.1.4.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11126243`)

Averaged WB_CQI correction When actDlForceRankDowng is set to dlNonGbrBrOnly (applicable for FDD only), and a rank downgrade is performed according to 5G_UP_ALG_9661_replaced([11139510](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krn4ovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), a CQI adjustment shall be added to rrmCorrAvgCQI to step up the low rank CQI, by rdCqiAdjustRankXToY, where:

 X represents the original rank (can be 2, 3, or 4).

 Y represents the downgraded rank (can be 1, 2).

For example:

 When rank 2 is downgraded to rank 1, rdCqiAdjustRank2To1 will be used to adjust the CQI accordingly.

R&D parameter definitions:

rdCqiAdjustRank2To1 [5G_UP_ALG_31537_replaced([11139130](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krk1U_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))]

rdCqiAdjustRank3To2 [5G_UP_ALG_31539_replaced([11139139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krk1W_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))]

rdCqiAdjustRank4To2 [5G_UP_ALG_31541_replaced([11139148](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krk1XfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))]

---

12684977: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12684977: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12684977: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12684977: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12684977: Averaged WB_CQI correction 12684977: Averaged WB_CQI correction gNB-DU shall calculate averaged corrected CQI for the purpose of PS BWP switching of non-VoNR UEs in bad radio conditions.

Calculation shall be executed as follows:   
[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in an italicized font. The equation is: $rrmCorr AvgBwpSwitchingCqi [t] = (1 - \frac{1}{rd AvgPsBwpSwitchingCqi}) \times rrmCorr AvgBwpSwitchingCqi [t - 1] + \frac{1}{rdAvgPsBwpSwitchingCqi} \times rrmCorr AvgCqi$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/604b0851-4bd5-4c5e-9847-d19cc2fc5859.png[/image_path][/image-0]

Where:

_rdAvgPsBwpSwitchingCqi is filter constant of the exponential filter (see[12684983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_n6qSEFKmEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))_

_rrmCorrAvgCqi calculated in[11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)_

If rrmCorrAvgBwpSwitchingCqi[t-1] does not exist (initial calculation or reset), rrmCorrAvgBwpSwitchingCqi[t] = rrmCorrAvgCqi

As rrmCorrAvgBwpSwitchingCqi is used only for non-VoNR UEs, the calculation is not performed if a VoNR is ongoing and the value is reset when the VoNR bearer is released.

---

#### 3.8.2.1.5 Selection of CQI to MCS mapping tables for MCS index determination

##### 3.8.2.1.5.0-1 Selection of CQI to MCS mapping tables for MCS index determination Corrected average WB-CQI to MCS mapping tables (ID: 11126253)

**3.8.2.1.5.0-1.0-11**  (ID: `12738432`)

Selection of CQI to MCS mapping tables for MCS index determination Basis for the evaluation of the link condition in DL and thus the determination of the DL MCS index is the corrected average WB-CQI (5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The mapping from the corrected average WB-CQI to the corresponding DL MCS index shall be achieved by means of mapping tables.

Separate CQI to MCS mapping tables are specified for rank1, rank2, rank3 and rank4 transmissions in DL, for MCS index tables of 64QAM and 256QAM and 1024QAM and lowSE, and for the system bandwidth of FR1 and FR2.

All the mapping tables represent the required CQI for selecting the most suited MCS (5G_UP_ALG_3045_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The appropriate table for the CQI to MCS mapping is selected depending on the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actDlLaEnhCqi2McsTables, the rank proposed by the DL SU-MIMO feature, the applied MCS index table, and the supported 5G band. In this context it must be considered that the rank may change on slot basis. If the 64QAM MCS index table or the 256QAM/1024QAM MCS index table is applied for the UE depends on the support of the 256QAM/1024QAM scheme in the cell and **the UE capability. Which of the MCS index table shall be applied for the UE is defined by the parameter mcs-Table of the pdschConfig structure send via the PsUser_UserSetupReq message. The MCS index table to be applied for a UE can also be switched by gNB-DU based on the dynamic MCS index table switching algorithm defined in 5G_UP_ALG_28616_replaced([11129283](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq7VHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).** The support of the 256QAM MCS index table is for 5G FR1 and FR2 bands. 

Furthermore, the selection of the mapping tables for FR1 or FR2 shall be decided by evaluating the dlBandwidth used for the link adaptation. The mapping of system bandwidth in DL to the corresponding maximum number of PRBs available for the data transmission on PDSCH can be found in 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2029_replaced([11277080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpLfx_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Following configurations shall be supported:

  * TDD FR1: 24, 38, 51, 78, 106, 133, 162, 189, 217, 245 and 273 PRBs
  * **[Before CB013712-B]TDD FR2: 32 and 66 PRBs**[End CB013712-B]****
  * FDD: 25, 52, 79, 106, 133, 160, 188, 216 and 270 PRBs



Note: The R&D parameter rd425abActivated (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2029_replaced([11277080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpLfx_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_L3_4658) shall be configured in such a way that the 64/256PRB configurations are excluded. 

The selection of the mapping tables for the 64QAM- or 256QAM or 1024QAM or lowSE **MCS index table in use for the individual UE is indicated by C-Plane and is the result of evaluating the UE capabilities.**

Editor's note: Both maximum PRB allocations, the maximum PRB allocation of 64/256PRB and the maximum PRB of 66/273PRB, are supported by the gNB. Which one of the implemented maximum PRB allocations shall be applied is selected by the R&D parameter rd425abActivated. Further details with respect to the R&D parameter rd425abActivated can be found in 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2029_replaced([11277080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpLfx_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_L3_4658.

L2PS supports MCS indexes as described in 5G_UP_ALG_26337_replaced([11124495](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqq2b_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Supported MCS'.

The CQI to MCS mapping tables are defined in a simplified way independently of the number of allocated symbols, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_3029_replaced([11126315](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9DPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 3.8.2.1.5.0-2 Selection of CQI to MCS mapping tables for MCS index determination Simplified average WB-CQI to MCS mapping tables (ID: 11126310)

**3.8.2.1.5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11126315`)

Selection of CQI to MCS mapping tables for MCS index determination General description of DL Mapping tables

---

**3.8.2.1.5.0-2.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `12730007`)

Selection of CQI to MCS mapping tables for MCS index determination For the required CQI to MCS Mapping, tables are defined per PTRS setting Antenna and Rank combinations. Each table includes the required CQI values, used for Link Adaptation of the full channel Bandwidth as well as used for FDM. These tables are regardless of the number of PDSCH OFDM symbols and the number of DMRS symbols.

In detail, for

  * LA: Only the PRB allocations of Full channel Bandwidth (example for TDD FR1: 51/106/133/162/217/273PRB for 20/40/50/60/80/100MHz) are valid for all MCS indexes.
  * Hint for TDD FR1: Tables are already prepared for 133 PRB which is not used by LA in 5G19B.
  * FDM: The complete tables are valid for FDM including LA part. For details see below in this (corresponding) requirement.
  * For any other full channel bandwidths not listed above, if the number of PRBs allocated to the UE is not in the LA table, the required CQI values shall be obtained by linear interpolation using the available values.



The required CQI values based on 5Gmax simulations for 10% BLER or 1% BLER and real channel estimations, using the Simulator SINR (the SINR at RX Antenna input). The latter was converted into the required CQI according to 5G_UP_ALG_3009_replaced([11126006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The input data from 5Gmax simulations can be found at this 5GMax sharepoint link:

[https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?csf=1&e=i6r6vI&cid=ee597ba7%2D7118%2D42c5%2Da51f%2D8153406e31bd&RootFolder=%2Fsites%2F5gmax%2FShared%20Documents%2FSimulationReports%2FNR%2FInternalCustomer%2FLUT%20for%20LA&FolderCTID=0x0120005835DA4A693F4F459973F25EDCB28D07](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?csf=1&e=i6r6vI&cid=ee597ba7%2D7118%2D42c5%2Da51f%2D8153406e31bd&RootFolder=%2Fsites%2F5gmax%2FShared%20Documents%2FSimulationReports%2FNR%2FInternalCustomer%2FLUT%20for%20LA&FolderCTID=0x0120005835DA4A693F4F459973F25EDCB28D07)

The evaluation of the 5Gmax simulation results and its conversion to the mapping table format used in this requirement can be found at this sharepoint link:

[https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&e=t7mP7d&cid=149f8753%2Df981%2D4a23%2Da3ef%2D6cce47860b9a&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2F5G%20Architecture%20documents%2F5G%20NR%20CQI%20Tables%2FCFAM1796%2DE%20McsMappingTables&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&e=t7mP7d&cid=149f8753%2Df981%2D4a23%2Da3ef%2D6cce47860b9a&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2F5G%20Architecture%20documents%2F5G%20NR%20CQI%20Tables%2FCFAM1796%2DE%20McsMappingTables&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c)

Note: MCS10 and MCS17 of the 64QAM MCS index tables are excluded in scheduling and the CQI values of these MCSes are not used. 

If NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is False

\- If a column for the number of PRBs allocated to the UE is not in the LA table, the required CQI values shall be obtained by linear interpolation using the available values.

For example, if 10 PRBs are allocated to the UE, the values for 8 and 16 PRBs are used to interpolate the required CQI values for 10 PRBs.

Else **(i.e. NIDD NRCELL.actDlOptMcsSelection is True**)****

\- A single PRB number defined by R&D parameter rdDlPRBMcsSelection (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_29011_replaced([11138974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krjnMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in each CQI-to-MCS mapping table for FR1( FR1 FDD and FR1 TDD) shall be used irrespective of the actually assigned number of PRBs. This eliminates the need for interpolation and changing the MCS based on exact allocated PRBs.

Selection of CQI to MCS mapping tables for MCS index determination Before clean-up - Subsection will be removed after update of SCT references

---

**3.8.2.1.5.0-2.0-2.0-13.0-2**  (ID: `11126652`)

Selection of CQI to MCS mapping tables for MCS index determination [Before CB009784-A] NO DL PTRS in FR1 TDD

For a General description of DL Mapping tables, please see requirement 5G_UP_ALG_3029_replaced([11126315](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9DPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For clean-up purpose, the CQI to MCS mapping tables are defined in following requirements:

  * 5G_UP_ALG_14230_replaced([11126881](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqzZRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_14231_replaced([11126898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqzZSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_14232_replaced([11126917](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqzZSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and 5G_UP_ALG_14233_replaced([11126935](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqzZTPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)): CQI to MCS mapping table for 4Tx 4Rx Antennae and PTRS ON:

  * 5G_UP_ALG_14234_replaced([11126991](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq0AUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_14235_replaced([11127010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq0AUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)): CQI to MCS mapping table for 2Tx 2Rx Antennae and PTRS ON:




If a column for the number of PRBs allocated to the UE is not in the LA table, the required CQI values shall be obtained by linear interpolation using the available values.

For example, if 10 PRBs are allocated to the UE, the values for 8 and 16 PRBs are used to interpolate the required CQI values for 10 PRBs.

[End CB009784-A]

**3.8.2.1.5.0-2.0-8.0-13.0-2**  (ID: `11127857`)

Selection of CQI to MCS mapping tables for MCS index determination Simplified average WB-CQI to MCS mapping tables for FDD, 64QAM

For a General description of DL Mapping tables, please see requirement 5G_UP_ALG_3029_replaced([11126315](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9DPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note :

MCS10 and MCS17 of the 64QAM MCS index tables are excluded in scheduling and the CQI values of these MCSes are not used.

column 216 PRBs added and has new simulated values in all the tables 

For clean-up purpose, the CQI to MCS mapping tables are defined in following requirements:

  * 5G_UP_ALG_3041_replaced([11127446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq1OevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) : CQI to MCS mapping table for 4Tx 4Rx Antennae and PTRS OFF

  * 5G_UP_ALG_3041_replaced([11127446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq1OevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) : CQI to MCS mapping table for 2Tx 2Rx Antennae and PTRS OFF

#### 3.8.2.1.6 MCS- re-determination

#### 3.8.2.1.6-1 MCS- re-determination

##### 3.8.2.1.6-1.0-5 MCS- re-determination The number of DMRS group(s) without data shall be determined for each PDSCH scheduling with below algorithm. Input: NRCELL.dlDataDmrsFdm.actDlDataDmrsFdm NRCELL.dlDataDmrsFdm.redCap1RxUesEnabled NRCELL.dlDataDmrsFdm.dlDataDmrsFdmThres64QamMcs; NRCELL.dlDataDmrsFdm.dlDataDmrsFdmThres256QamMcs; NRCELL.dlDataDmrsFdm.dlDataDmrsFdmThres64QamLowSEMcs; [CB008949-A] NRCELL.dlDataDmrsFdm.dlDataDmrsFdmThres1024QamMcs; [End CB008949-A] NRCELL.dlDataDmrsFdm.thres256QamMcsRedCap1Rx; NRCELL.actDlMuMimoEnh; UE type (whether UE is 1 RX RedCap UE); mcs-Table; mcs; the number of layers; DL DMRS configuration type (5G_UP_ALG_24898_replaced( 11192062 )); IsUePairedWithOtherUe (indicating whether the UE is MU paired with other UE in the time of this scheduling, it is output of MU pairing algorithm). Algorithm: Set actLocalDmrsFdm, localThres64Qam, localThres256Qam, localThresLowSE [CB008949-A], localThres1024Qam [End CB008949-A] as below based on UE type. output/UE type 1 RX RedCap UE Other UE actLocalDmrsFdm redCap1RxUesEnabled actDlDataDmrsFdm localThres64Qam dlDataDmrsFdmThres64QamMcs dlDataDmrsFdmThres64QamMcs localThres256Qam thres256QamMcsRedCap1Rx dlDataDmrsFdmThres256QamMcs localThresLowSE dlDataDmrsFdmThres64QamLowSEMcs dlDataDmrsFdmThres64QamLowSEMcs [CB008949-A]  localThres1024Qam  [End CB008949-A] dlDataDmrsFdmThres1024QamMcs (Note 3) dlDataDmrsFdmThres1024QamMcs Set maxMcs, localThres as below based on mcs-table used for PDSCH scheduling. output/mcs-table 64QAM MCS table 256 QAM MCS table 64QAM Low SE MCS table [CB008949-A] 1024 QAM MCS table [End CB008949-A] maxMcs 28 27 28 26 localThres localThres64Qam localThres256Qam localThresLowSE localThres1024Qam Set isMuPairedUe to true if NRCELL.actDlMuMimoEnh is True and UE is paired to other UE in this PDSCH scheduling, otherwise set to false. The number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data shall be determined as below: Input Output isMuPairedUe DL DMRS configuration type DlDmrsFdm &amp; mcs number of layers Number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data false Type 1 (actLocalDmrsFdm is true) and (maxMcs&gt;=mcs&gt;= localThres) and (number of layers &lt;=2) 1 (actLocalDmrsFdm is false) or ( mcs&lt; localThres ) or (number of layers &gt; 2) 2 Type 2 (actLocalDmrsFdm is true) and  (maxMcs&gt;=mcs&gt;= localThres) layers &lt;=2 1 2&lt; layers &lt;=4 2 (actLocalDmrsFdm is false) or ( mcs&lt; localThres ) any 3 True Type 1 any any 2 Type 2 (actLocalDmrsFdm is true) and (maxMcs&gt;=mcs&gt;= localThres) any According to 5G_UP_ALG_25267_replaced( 11145445 ) (actLocalDmrsFdm is false) or ( mcs&lt; localThres ) any 3 Output: Number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data Note: this requirement is used for link adaptation hence only initial transmission is considered. The scenario with mcs &gt; maxMcs is a re-transmission. When 64QAM LowSE table is used, DL DMRS configuration type 2 will not be configured. [CB008949-A] Normally RedCap UE does not support 1024QAM MCS table, still use the common 1024QAM threshold here in case UE still report the capability. [End CB008949-A] For 1RX UE max number of layers is always 1 (ID: 12721263)

#### 3.8.2.1.6-2 MCS- re-determination

##### 3.8.2.1.6-2.0-2 MCS- re-determination When a [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] bearer is added to a UE it is possible that the CSI reporting period will be reconfigured to a shorter period to improve voice performance.  If this happens L2-PS will continue to send CSI decode requests to L1 based on the original configuration until CP-RT informs L2-PS that the Reconfiguration Complete message has been received.  At that time L2-PS will use the new CSI report configuration to send CSI decode requests to L1. (ID: 11129197)

##### 3.8.2.1.6.0-1 MCS- re-determination MCS Selection in DSS transient phase (ID: 11129025)

**3.8.2.1.6.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12678602`)

MCS- re-determination When nrLteDssMode > 1 [CB007372-AA] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-AA] and cell is configured for SA or for Concurrent SA and NSA operation (NRCELL.cellDepType parameter set to SA or both), during the DSS transient phase (see 5G_L2_142_replaced([11143994](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_233KLvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the Inner Loop Link Adaptation, when NRBTS/actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom set to false, shall use the MCS0 (rrmDlProposedMcs = 0 (see 5G_UP_ALG_3045_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) for all scheduled DL transmissions.

[CB007372-AA] Note: For DSS with NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true all DCI 1_0 transmissions are in NR dedicated part of the carrier (see [12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB007372-AA]

**Note: Only NRCELL.cellDepType during cell setup needs to be considered. Subsequent Cell reconfiguration can be ignored, as change of NRCELL.cellDepType requires new reservations and coordination procedure with CRM.**

For determination of the DL MCS index, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_3045_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in detail.

---

re-determination 12543872: MCS- re-determination 12543872: MCS- re-determination 12543872: MCS- re-determination 12543872: MCS- re-determination 12543872: MCS- re-determination While PDSCH data scheduling in slots where CSI-RS for tracking is configured **or in slots where Flexible Rate matching is configured (5G_UP_ALG_4722_replaced([11172097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJoPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or in slots where ZP CSI-RS (CSI-IM or TRS) are located (For DSS, refer 5G_UP_ALG_4716_replaced([11171969](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJBIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actRateMatchedCsiIm is set to 'true')**, 5G-L2-PS shall continue to determine the DL MCS index according to the following steps:****

 Step1: Determine the rrmDlProposedMcs according to 5G_UP_ALG_3045_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), calculate the interpolated spectrum efficiency [image-0][image_desc]The image contains the text "Qinterpolate" in a blurry, stylized font. The letters are a dark color with a slight purple and green chromatic aberration, set against a white background. The text is centered horizontally and vertically within the frame.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7431.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] by using the rrmCorrAvgCqiInTrs **(see 5G_UP_ALG_7557_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) according to 5G_UP_ALG_2975_replaced([11124878](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqrdf_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), then go to Step2;**

 Step2: If the rrmDlProposedMcs equals to supported minimum MCS, skip the following steps and use this rrmDlProposedMcs for scheduling; Otherwise, go to Step3;

 Step3: Determine the TBS according to 5G_UP_ALG_4621_replaced([11192057](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kviITvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) by using the rrmDlProposedMcs, then calculate the effective spectrum efficiency [image-1][image_desc]The image contains the text "Qeffective" in a blurry, stylized font. The letters are a dark color with a rainbow-like chromatic aberration effect. The background is white.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7431.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] according to 5G_UP_ALG_2978_replaced([11124960](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsEgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If [image-2][image_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "Q_interpolate >= Q_effective". The text is in a dark color against a light background.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7431.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] and effective code rate calculated in 5G_UP_ALG_2967_replaced([11124515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqrdc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is smaller than 0.95, skip the following steps, use the current rrmDlProposedMcs and the determined TBS for scheduling if the effective code rate is larger than or equals to Code-Rate-Threshold-for-MCS-reselection, use rrmDlProposedMcs – 1 to determine the TBS and the scheduling if the effective code rate is smaller than Code-Rate-Threshold-for-MCS-reselection; Otherwise, go to Step4;

 Step4: Set rrmDlProposedMcs to the maximum MCS that smaller than the current rrmDlProposedMcs, i.e., rrmDlProposedMcs = rrmDlProposedMcs – 1, then go to Step2.

The re-determined DL MCS is forwarded to the DL scheduler.

The parameter rrmDlProposedMcs represents the proposed MCS and is defined by 5G_UP_ALG_3045_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: Code-Rate-Threshold-for-MCS-reselection shall be 0.6 by default, determined by 5Gmax simulation results.

[PR826449-25R3] Note 2: for detailed information about PDSCH grants see [11364174: MCS re-determination](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udIJyPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) [End PR826449-25R3]

##### 3.8.2.1.6.0-3 MCS- re-determination Scaling of MCS for VoNR UE with rank change (ID: 11129078)

**3.8.2.1.6.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `13425816`)

MCS- re-determination 13425816: MCS- re-determination 13425816: MCS- re-determination 13425816: MCS- re-determination 13425816: MCS- re-determination If (NRCELL.actDlVoNRLimMaxMcsAndLayer is set to TRUE), an initial transmission with a request class priority (as defined in 11174018) which is either >= voipTraffic or == deprioritizedVoip (which refers to 5QI1/65/66) shall use a forced rank 1 transmission with a scaled MCS that is calculated from the original scaled MCS (rrmDlScaledMcsOrig) as following:

rrmDlScaledMCS=min(rrmDlScaledMcsForcedRank1(rrmDlScaledMcsOrig, Reported Rank), NRCELL.maxMCSVoNRRankAndMCSRestrict )

Where, 

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical definition. The definition is for `rrmDlScaledMcsForcedRank1`. It is defined as a piecewise function with four cases, each corresponding to a different rank. The definition is as follows: `rrmDlScaledMcsForcedRank1` = \- `rrmDlScaledMcsOrig` if `rank = 1` \- `rank2ToRank1Map(rrmDlScaledMcsOrig, TBSReductionPct)` if `rank = 2` \- `rank3ToRank1Map(rrmDlScaledMcsOrig, TBSReductionPct)` if `rank = 3` \- `rank4ToRank1Map(rrmDlScaledMcsOrig, TBSReductionPct)` if `rank = 4`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7435.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

rrmDlScaledMcsOrig is the rrmDlScaled (i.e. “non-downgraded” MCS) calculated under the reported rank,

rank**XToRank1Map(rrmDlScaledMcsOrig, TBSReductionPct) is a pre-configured table (link to the table is available at the end of the requirement) that issues rrmDlScaledMcsForcedRank1 such that:**

[image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is presented on two lines. The first line reads: TBS(rrmDlScaledMcsForcedRank1, rank1) The second line reads: = TBS(rrmDlScaledMcsOrig, ReportedRank) * (100 - TBSReductionPct) The equation appears to be defining a relationship between two TBS (Transport Block Size) values, one that is "forced" and one that is "original", with a reduction percentage applied.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7435.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

TBSReductionPct refers to rdVoNRRestrictMaxMCSAndLayerTBSReductionPct (5G_UP_ALG_12851_replaced([11138525](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krf80fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The table can be found under [VONR_MCS_rank1_mapping_table_2.0.xlsx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20UP%20Algorithms/3%20L2RT%20Algorithms/VONR_MCS_rank1_mapping_table_2.0.xlsx?d=wff494851387f4ef09bb7383c344356f8&csf=1&web=1&e=U0fLw7)

Note 1: The forced MCS evaluation bases on TBSes corresponding to a transport block of TDD FR1 with 273 PRB and 12 PDSCH symbols.  
Note 2: The MCSes MCS10 and MCS17 are not supported if the 64QAM MCS index table is selected. The not supported MCS are indicated in the table by grey background color.

rrmDlScaledMcsForcedRank1 shall also be calculated when VoNR quality check is performed, meaning when UE has a VoNR [CB014699-E] and Critical VoNR [End CB014699-E] bearer and NRBTS/powerSavingBwpSwitching/mcsThresholdVoNRToPsBwp is set to a value greater than 0.

Notes:

  * VoNR rank and MCS restriction algorithm activation flag value is derived from NRCELL.actDlVoNRLimMaxMcsAndLayer.

  * If NRCELL.maxMCSVoNRRankAndMCSRestrict is not configued, gNB-DU shall use the default value as specified 5G_UP_ALG_12849_replaced([11138511](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krfVzvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

  * Per L2PS implementation limitation: The MCS/Rank calculation is performed only when the first traffic to be multiplexed into a DL transmission is being considered. MCS/Rank can’t be recalculated when additional traffic needs to be multiplexed into a DL transmission. As traffic is added to a DL transmission in decreasing order of request class priority, the above logic – in addition to (5G_UP_ALG_4356_replaced([11177988](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzvgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) - guarantees that

  * [CB014699-E] criticalVoipTraffic satisfies the MCS/Rank restriction [End CB014699-E].

  * voipTraffic satisfies the MCS/Rank restriction
  * deprioritizedVoip satisfies the MCS/Rank restriction or is postponed until it satisfies the MCS/Rank restriction

re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination 12763349: MCS- re-determination gNB-DU shall calculate averaged proposed MCS for the purpose of PS BWP switching of VoNR UEs in bad radio conditions.

Calculation shall be executed as follows:   
[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is: $avgProposedMCS[t] = (1 - \frac{1}{rdAvgPrMcs}) \times avgProposedMCS[t-1] + (\frac{1}{rdAvgPrMcs}) \times proposedMCS$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/88ee7464-9f32-43e2-a30a-db3b0c2e5ffa.png[/image_path][/image-0]

Where:

_rdAvgPrMcs is filter constant of the exponential filter (see 5G_UP_ALG_32819_replaced([11139175](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krk1Y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))_

_proposedMCS is [Before 04963047-25R3] rrmDlScaledMCS [04963047-25R3] rrmDlScaledMcsForcedRank1 [End 04963047-25R3] calculated in 5G_UP_ALG_12909_replaced([11129078](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq6uA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))_

If avgProposedMCS[t-1] does not exist (initial calculation), avgProposedMCS[t] = proposedMCS

As [Before 04963047-25R3] rrmDlScaledMCS [04963047-25R3] rrmDlScaledMcsForcedRank1 [End 04963047-25R3] is only applied to VoNR UEs, analogously avgProposedMCS shall be calculated only for VoNR UEs and reset every time VoNR bearer is setup.

##### 3.8.2.1.7.0-1 CQI mapping mapping CQI from cqi-table 1 to cqi-table 2 (up to 26R2) (ID: 11129210)

**3.8.2.1.7.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12737561`)

CQI mapping [Before CB008949-A]

This mapping table is used to map a received CQI which is related to CQI Table 1 to the corresponding CQI

value (same/similar spectral efficiency) in CQI Table 2. Its use is specified in 5G_UP_ALG_3005_replaced([11125595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|   
---|---  
64QAM/64QAMLowSE CQI table 1 | 256QAM CQI table 2  
1 | 1  
2 | 1  
3 | 2  
4 | 2  
5 | 3  
6 | 3  
7 | 4  
8 | 5  
9 | 6  
10 | 7  
11 | 8  
12 | 9  
13 | 10  
14 | 11  
15 | 12  
  
Note: current only cqi-table1 will be used for 64QAMLowSE MCS table, above requirement need to be updated while cqi table 3 is supported for 64QAMLowSE MCS table.

[End CB008949-A]

written in black text on a white background. The equation is: $\epsilon_i = R_i * Qm_i * V_i$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_7457.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] -  Ri and Qmi are coding rate and modulation order of UE(i) according to rrmDlProposedMcs (5G_UP_ALG_3045_replaced( 11125301 )) -  Vi is the number of layer for UE(i). (ID: 11129275)

#### 3.8.2.1.8-2 MCS mapping

##### 3.8.2.1.8-2.0-3 MCS mapping For TDD-FR1 and FDD-FR1, if NRCELL.actDlMcsTableAndDmrsSwitching is set to 1 (DL MCS Table) or 3 (DL MCS table + DMRS type), or if NRCELL.actDl1024Qam is equal to value other than 'disabled' and UE is DL 1024QAM capable (see 12766773: Check for 1024QAM support )[Before CB013712-B] or for TDD-FR2 and if NRCELL.actDlMcsTableAndDmrsSwitching is set to 1 (DL MCS Table)[End CB013712-B], gNB-DU shall monitor the initialProposedMcs provided by link adaptation before any MCS downgrade (as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_22781_replaced( 11125340 )) and the reported rank of a UE, over several predefined sampling periods to determine if the MCS index table shall be switched for a UE based on the channel conditions. The monitoring algorithm shall work as following. gNB-DU shall initialize the nbSamples1, nbSamples2 and timeOfLastSample with 0. For each new DL transmission by a UE, gNB-DU shall perform the following. Each time the UE is scheduled in DL with a new transmission, the gNB-DU shall calculate the metric: M = SE(initialProposedMcs) * dlRank Where: o initialProposedMcs refers to the cqiDerivedMcs without considering any impact/downgraded due to special TRS/CSI-IM/Bi slots. (cf. Note1) o SE(initialProposedMcs) represents the Spectral Efficiency (SE) value of the MCS given in 3GPP 38.214 MCS index tables. o dlRank is the latest Rank reported by the UE. gNB-DU shall compare the metric M as following, depending on whether the UE is configured with 64QAM or 256QAM or 1024QAM MCS table in DL: If NRCELL.actDlMcsTableAndDmrsSwitching is set to 0 (disabled) or 2 (DMRS type), and NRCELL.actDl1024Qam is equal to any value other than 'disabled' and UE is DL 1024QAM capable (see 12766773: Check for 1024QAM support ) o if the UE is configured with 256QAM: • Check condition 1 (target MCS table is 1024QAM): if M &gt;= NRCELL.dlMcsTable256QamTo1024QamThr, o if the UE is configured with 1024qam: • Check condition 1 (target MCS table is 256QAM): if M &lt; NRCELL.dlMcsTable1024QamTo256QamThr, Else if NRCELL.actDlMcsTableAndDmrsSwitching is set to 1 (DL MCS Table) or 3 (DL MCS Table + DMRS Type), and ( NRCELL.actDl1024Qam is set to 'disabled' or UE is not DL 1024QAM capable (see 12766773: Check for 1024QAM support )) o if the UE is configured with 64QAM: • Check condition 1 (target MCS table is 256QAM): if M &gt;= NRCELL.dlMcsTable64QamTo256QamThr, o OR, if the UE is configured with 256qam: • Check condition 1 (target MCS table is 64QAM): if M &lt; NRCELL.dlMcsTable256QamTo64QamThr, Else if NRCELL.actDlMcsTableAndDmrsSwitching is set to 1 (DL MCS Table) or 3 (DL MCS Table + DMRS Type), and NRCELL.actDl1024Qam is equal to any value other than 'disabled' and UE is DL 1024QAM capable (see 12766773: Check for 1024QAM support ) o if the UE is configured with 64QAM: • Check condition 1 (target MCS table is 256QAM): if M &gt;= NRCELL.dlMcsTable64QamTo256QamThr, • Check condition 2 (target MCS table is 1024QAM): if M &gt;= NRCELL.dlMcsTable256QamTo1024QamThr, Note: The threshold for 256QAM to 1024QAM MCS table switch is reused for 64QAM to 1024QAM MCS table switch. o if the UE is configured with 256QAM: • Check condition 1 (target MCS table is 1024QAM): if M &gt;= NRCELL.dlMcsTable256QamTo1024QamThr, • Check condition 2 (target MCS table is 64QAM): if M &lt; NRCELL.dlMcsTable256QamTo64QamThr, o if the UE is configured with 1024qam: • Check condition 1 (target MCS table is 256QAM): if M &lt; NRCELL.dlMcsTable1024QamTo256QamThr, • Check condition 2 (target MCS table is 64QAM): if M &lt; NRCELL.dlMcsTable256QamTo64QamThr, Note: The threshold for 256QAM to 64QAM MCS table switch is reused for 1024QAM to 64QAM MCS table switch. Endif If the condition 1 above is verified the gNB-DU shall keep track of it by incrementing an internal counter (nbSamples1). If M does not satisify the condition 1 above, the algorithm for condition 1 is reset/restarted (nbSamples1 = 0). If the condition 2 above is verified the gNB-DU shall keep track of it by incrementing an internal counter (nbSamples2). If M does not satisify the condition 2 above, the algorithm for condition 2 is reset/restarted (nbSamples2 = 0). If nbSamples2 is 0 and nbSamples1 &gt;= NRCELL.dlNumOfValidSamples, a request to re-configure the MCS table (to the target MCS table mentioned above with condition 1) is sent to CP-RT. If nbSamples1 &gt;= NRCELL.dlNumOfValidSamples and nbSamples2 &gt;= NRCELL.dlNumOfValidSamples, a request to re-configure the MCS table (to the target MCS table mentioned above with condition 2) is sent to CP-RT. If nbSamples1 is 0 and nbSamples2 &gt;= NRCELL.dlNumOfValidSamples， a request to re-configure the MCS table (to the target MCS table mentioned above with condition 2) is sent to CP-RT. Two consecutive DL new transmissions of the UE which are considered for the monitoring have to be at least rdDlMonitoringWindow ms apart from each other, where rdDlMonitoringWindow is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_28618_replaced( 11138963 ). If two consecutive initial transmissions from the UE are more than (NRCELL.dlNumOfValidSamples * rdDlMonitoringWindow) ms apart, gNB-DU shall first reset the algorithm counters (nbSamples1 = 0, nbSamples2 = 0, timeOfLastSample = 0) and then proceed with calculation of metric M as outlined above. The monitoring algorithm shall not be applicable during the early DL LA mode (5G_UP_ALG_11046_replaced( 11125506 )). Once the MCS table switch for a UE is indicated to CP-RT and the UE has entered the transient phase, gNB-DU shall flush all the pending retransmissions by the UE and free the HARQ process for fresh transmission (see 5G_UP_ALG_8181_replaced( 11153814 )). gNB-DU shall compute rrmDeltaCqiMcsTableSwitch in 13467458 only for the first initial transmission after the switch from 256QAM MCS table [PR877993-26R3] or 64QAM MCS table [End PR877993-26R3] to 1024QAM MCS table after transient phase and recompute rrmDeltaCqi in 11129358 , when the sum of last rrmDeltaCqi and rrmAvgCqi before switching is greater than 15 (highest CQI index) before switching, so that rrmDeltaCqiMcsTableSwitch shall avoid any bias that can potentially lead to an increase in spectral efficiency value of the corresponding MCS index from 1024QAM MCS table more than the spectral efficiency value of the corresponding MCS index from 256QAM MCS table [PR877993-26R3] or 64QAM MCS table [End PR877993-26R3] before switching (see: 13467458: OLLA correction – Compensation due to dynamic switching of MCS index tables and 12737524: mapping CQI from one cqi-table to another cqi-table ) In case NRCELL/actDl1024Qam contains an element equal to 'Dl1024QamVoNR' and UE is DL 1024QAM capable (see 12766773: Check for 1024QAM support ), If UE is configured with 256QAM or 64QAM and VoNR call is active, gNB-DU shall not trigger the MCS table switch, and shall reset the monitoring algorithm (nbSamples1=0, nbSamples2=0) and stop monitoring.  The algorithm shall restart after VoNR call is released. Else if UE is configured with 1024QAM and VoNR call is active, gNB-DU shall perform above monitoring algorithm and trigger the MCS table switch to 256QAM or 64QAM once the criteria meet. If switch to 256QAM MCS table or 64QAM is successfully finished while VoNR call is still active, gNB-DU shall not anymore trigger the MCS table switch, and shall reset the monitoring algorithm (nbSamples1=0, nbSamples2=0) and stop monitoring. The algorithm shall restart after VoNR call is released. Endif In any of the following cases, the gNB-DU shall reset and stop the monitoring ( 13374150 ) as long as: • The UE is not 256QAM capable; • The UE has an active VoNR call and (NRCELL/actDl1024Qam is set to 'disabled' or NRCELL/actDl1024Qam contains no element equal to 'Dl1024QamVoNR') • The UE has an active HR DRB and has no active VoNR call. • The trigger to switch the MCS table is sent to CP-RT. The monitoring shall restart after the reception of RRC reconfiguration complete or cancellation from CP-RT. • The UE is in the transient phase or in the Enhanced RRC Reconfiguration State. • The UE is in SCell in case of inter-gNB CA. • The UE is in SCell and NRBTS/dlMcsTabAndDmrsSwitchImpCells is not set to "servingCell" in case of intra-gNB CA. Note1: The parameter initialProposedMCS is introduced in this algorithm because cqiDerivedMcs contains the impact of TRS/CSI-IM/Bi slots in SW and ttiTrace. Such impact results in MCS downgrade which is undesired for the above algorithm. (ID: 13480436)

#### 3.8.2.1.8-3 MCS mapping

##### 3.8.2.1.8-3.0-2 MCS mapping For TDD-FR1, if NRCELL.actDlMcsTableAndDmrsSwitching is set to 2 (DMRS type) or 3 (DL MCS table + DMRS type), gNB-DU shall monitor the initialProposedMcs provided by link adaptation before any MCS downgrade (as defined in 11125333: MCS calculation ) and the reported rank of a UE, over several predefined sampling periods to determine if the DL DMRS type shall be switched for a UE based on the channel conditions. The monitoring algorithm is defined as follows. gNB-DU shall initialize the nbSample with 0. For each new DL transmission by a UE, gNB-DU shall perform the following. Each time the UE is scheduled in DL with a new transmission, the gNB-DU shall calculate the metric: M = SE(initialProposedMcs) * dlRank Where: o initialProposedMcs refers to the cqiDerivedMcs without considering any impact/downgraded due to special TRS/CSI-IM/Bi slots. (cf. Note1) o SE(initialProposedMcs) represents the Spectral Efficiency (SE) value of the MCS given in 3GPP 38.214 MCS index tables. o dlRank is the latest Rank reported by the UE. gNB-DU shall compare the metric M as following, depending on whether the UE is configured with DL DMRS type 1 or DL DMRS type 2: o if the UE is configured with DL DMRS type 1: • Check if M &gt;= NRCELL.dlDmrsType1ToType2Thr, o OR, if the UE is configured with DL DMRS type 2: • Check if M &lt; NRCELL.dlDmrsType2ToType1Thr, If the condition above is verified the gNB-DU shall keep track of it by incrementing an internal counter (nbSamples). If nbSamples &gt;= NRCELL.dlNumOfValidSamples, a request to switch the DL DMRS type is sent to CP-RT. Two consecutive DL new transmissions of the UE which are considered for the monitoring, must be at least rdDlMonitoringWindow ms apart from each other, where rdDlMonitoringWindow is defined in 11138963: rdDlMonitoringWindow . If M does not satisfy the condition above, the algorithm is reset/restarted (nbSamples = 0). If two consecutive initial transmissions from the UE are more than (NRCELL.dlNumOfValidSamples * rdDlMonitoringWindow) ms apart, gNB-DU shall first reset the algorithm counter (nbSamples = 0) and then proceed with calculation of metric M as outlined above. The monitoring algorithm shall not be applicable during the early DL LA mode ( 11125506: Early DL OLLA ). Once the DMRS type switch for a UE is indicated to CP-RT and the UE has entered the transient phase, gNB-DU shall flush all the pending retransmissions by the UE and free the HARQ process for fresh transmission (see 11153814: Transient phase with DCI 1_0 in CSS upon RRC Reconfiguration ). In any of the following cases, the gNB-DU shall reset and stop the monitoring ( 13374150 ), as long as: The UE is not DL DMRS type 2 capable; The UE has an active VoNR call OR an active HR DRB. The trigger to switch the DL DMRS type is sent to CP-RT. The monitoring shall restart after the reception of RRC reconfiguration complete or cancellation from CP-RT. The UE is in the transient phase or in the Enhanced RRC Reconfiguration state. The UE is in SCell in case of inter-gNB CA. The UE is in SCell and NRBTS/dlMcsTabAndDmrsSwitchImpCells is not set to "servingCell" in case of intra-gNB CA. It is subband BF UE and NRBTS.NRCELL.NRDLMUMIMO.actDlMuPairingGroup is set to True. If both the MCS index table switch ( 11129283: Dynamic switching of MCS index table ) and DL DMRS type switch indications are to be sent to CP-RT in the same slot, gNB-DU shall combine both indications in one message. Note1: The parameter initialProposedMCS is introduced in this algorithm because cqiDerivedMcs contains the impact of TRS/CSI-IM/Bi slots in SW and ttiTrace. Such impact results in MCS downgrade which is undesired for the above algorithm. [CB008949-D] If NRCELL/actDl1024Qam is set to value other than 'disabled' and NRCELL/actDl1024Qam contains no element equal to 'Dl1024QamType2',  gNB-DU shall not send indication to CP-RT for switching to DMRS type 2 if any of below condition is met: - Both MCS table switch and DL DMRS type switch indications are combined according to above algorithm and target MCS table is 1024QAM. - 1024QAM MCS table is currently used and no MCS switch indication send to CP-RT. [End CB008949-D] (ID: 12815649)

##### 3.8.2.1.8.0-1 MCS mapping mapping MCS from other RANK to RANK1 for 64QAM (ID: 11129241)
