\- Evaluation of UE specific and slot specific delta SINR if NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE.actRimMitigationExtend is set to TRUE and RIM UL mitigation extension is triggered, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_27592_replaced([11138090](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krc5i_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**3.8.3.2.0-1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11137899`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation Skip delta SINR evaluation

* * *

---

**3.8.3.2.0-1.0-1.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `11137935`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation The Outer Loop Link Adaptation (OLLA) shall evaluate the delta SINR (rrmDeltaSinr[i,j,t]) for correcting the wideband SINR measurements referring to carrier j of UE i. For this purpose, the OLLA shall evaluate the CRC feedback of the initial PUSCH transmissions provided by L1 by means of the message UlData_PuschReceiveRespHarqU.

If rrmActConsiderRetxForUlOlla == 1, see 5G_UP_ALG_2973_replaced([11124858](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqrdevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

In addition to initial PUSCH transmissions, OLLA shall also evaluate the CRC feedback of all PUSCH retransmissions. 

The OLLA algorithm shall skip the delta SINR evaluation if:

1\. The granted MCS is equal to the maximum MCS for basic reliability Link adaption supported in UL and the UL link adaptation allows the reception of a NACK without MCS decrement (see 5G_UP_ALG_3156_replaced([11138107](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krc5jvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and its associated CRC ACK feedback is received for the HARQ process.

2\. The granted MCS is equal to the maximum MCS (the unique maximum MCS supported by the used MCS table, qam64 or qam64LowSE MCS table) for high reliability LA and the UL link adaptation allows the reception of a NACK without MCS decrement (see 5G_UP_ALG_3156_replaced([11138107](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krc5jvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and its associated HARQ ACK feedback is received for the HARQ process.

3\. The proposed number of PRBs is equal to the smallest PRB allocation, and a CRC NACK feedback is received for the HARQ process when UE is not scheduled with 64qamLowSE table. If rrmLastATBStepFlagLowSE==TRUE (5G_UP_ALG_17299_replaced([11132066](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krEfCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and a CRC NACK feedback is received for the HARQ process when UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE table.

[Before CB013204-A] The R&D parameter rdMinNumPrbsPusch (see 5G_L2_1195_replaced([11185095](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27vkoPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) allows the definition of the smallest PRB allocation for MCS0 using Rank 1 for test purposes.[CB013204-A] The smallest PRB allocation is given by min(4, rrmMinPuschPrbATB (5G_UP_ALG_31946_replaced([11132110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krEfEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))). [End CB013204-A]

Note when UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE table the OLLA algorithm shall skip the delta SINR evaluation if a CRC NACK feedback is received for the HARQ process when the rescaled corrected average WB-SINR is still smaller than the required SINR for the MCS/PRB combination of the last ATB step (rrmLastATBStepFlagLowSE==TRUE).

The purpose of this requirement (2) is to ignore NACKs when the lowest MCS/PRB combination is reached so that OLLA will not be driven unnecessarily low at edge-of-cell conditions. For the case of UL Slot Aggregation this "lowest MCS/PRB" combination is determined as follows rather than the conditions (2) above:

If this is a 4x SAg assignment and a Nack is received, then this condition (2) will skip the delta SINR evaluation.

4\. If rrmActConsiderRetxForUlOlla == 0, see 5G_UP_ALG_2973_replaced([11124858](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqrdevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the CRC feedback is belonging to an HARQ retransmission.

5\. The UL scheduler selects for the UL transmission for both Basic Reliability and High Reliability a lower MCS or a lower PRB allocation with MCS0 than recommended by the UL Link Adaptation and a CRC ACK feedback is received for the HARQ process.

Note: Condition # 5 is ignored if a lower MCS is selected and a "limited" UL MCS downgrade is configured for Basic Reliability. See 5G_UP_ALG_6857_replaced([11137777](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krbrYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

6\. DTX is indicated in UlData_PuschReceiveRespHarqU and one of the following:

  * proactive grant is not skippable and rdUseDtxInUlOlla = FALSE (see 5G_L2_1258_replaced([11185268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27wyuPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for UE in BR mode or rdUseDtxInUlHrOlla = FALSE for UE in HR mode

  * If UE is configured for skippable grants (5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), any PUSCH DTX indication from grants listed in 5G_UP_ALG_16415_replaced([11195380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))




7\. If appliedUlBeamFallback == TRUE (5G_UP_ALG_1888_replaced([11111451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the beam probing procedure (see 5G_UP_ALG_2041_replaced([11112416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZr_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or the beam re-probing procedure (see 5G_UP_ALG_2051_replaced([11112493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpWAwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is performed.

8\. If the UL ILLA is in regular operation mode (rdActIllaUl set to 1 – see 5G_UP_ALG_9475_replaced([11124506](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqrdcfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the UL ILLA is paused during startup procedure or when no UL transmission is received for rdSinrExpiryTime (see 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for basic reliability, and rdSinrExpiryTimeHR (see 5G_UP_ALG_16856_replaced([11138651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krgj6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for high reliability.

  1. If appliedUlBeamSweeping == TRUE (5G_UP_ALG_1888_replaced([11111451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the beam sweeping procedure related to PUSCH (see 5G_UP_ALG_2062_replaced([11112532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpWn0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is indicated.



10\. If actInactiveBwpScanning = TRUE and an inactive BWP scanning procedure is ongoing (see 5G_UP_ALG_16104_replaced([11111313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpMPyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

11\. HARQ feedback corresponds to PUSCH transmission during the transient phase of pCSI report reconfiguration (see 5G_UP_ALG_12845_replaced([11159141](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktHtdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

  1. HARQ feedback corresponds to a Configured Grant HARQ process.

  2. HARQ feedback which corresponds to a PUSCH transmission is in different BR/HR mode from the HR/BR mode during HRAQ feedback's initial transmission due to fallback (5G_UP_ALG_16426_replaced([11138318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))/recovery(5G_UP_ALG_16077_replaced([11138358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreusvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

  3. In high reliability LA mode, the granted MCS by RankX is equal to the corresponding rank-specific MCS threshold: NRCELL.puschHrLaConfig.ullaMcsMax64qamLowSERANKxHR (qam64lowSE MCS Table) or NRCELL.puschHrLaConfig.ullaMcsMax64qamRANKxHR (64QAM MCS Table), and the applied threshold (based on used rank and MCS table) is set to a value lower than the maximum MCS (the unique maximum MCS supported by the used MCS table, qam64 or qam64LowSE MCS table, i.e.,MCS28). If HARQ-ACK feedback is received for such transmission, delta SINR is not updated.

  4. If a ACK is received and Recent Bler estimation defined in 5G_UP_ALG_17217_replaced([11138412](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreuuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is greater than NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA.ullaBlerTargetHR, L2PS shall skip the ACK and rrmDeltaSinr(k=HR) shall not be updated. Recent Bler estimated refer to 5G_UP_ALG_17217_replaced([11138412](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreuuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.8.3.2.0-1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11137940`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation Evaluation of Delta SINR

---

**3.8.3.2.0-1.0-1.0-3.0-7**  (ID: `13255771`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation 13255771: Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation The equation below specifies the algorithm of the OLLA for correcting the average SINR. All conditions for skipping the recalculation of delta SINR are specified by the branch, which belongs to an N/A CRC feedback. As defined in 5G_UP_ALG_15105_replaced([11125150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) UE is regarded as working in BR (Basic Reliability) mode as default and thus default BR LA (Link adaptation) is applied when L2-PS doesn't know whether UE is in HR (High Reliability) mode or BR (Basic Reliability) mode during the initial duration. For basic reliability LA: If the R&D parameter rdUseDtxInUlOlla (see 5G_L2_1258_replaced([11185268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27wyuPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to TRUE and rrmAvgSinrUl [i, j, t] (see [11130873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_gw_vwPvKEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) < rdUlOllaDtxSinrThreshold ([12636711](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_1GF6dEBDEfCal_QUJn4xrg)), a DTX indication shall be interpreted as a NACK. For high reliability LA: If the R&D parameter rdUseDtxInUlHrOlla (see 5G_L2_10057_replaced([11185274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27wyufvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to TRUE and rrmAvgSinrUl [i, j, t] (see [11130873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_gw_vwPvKEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) < rdUlOllaDtxSinrThreshold ([12636711](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_1GF6dEBDEfCal_QUJn4xrg)), a DTX indication shall be interpreted as a NACK. [image-0][image_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_8283.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] with i: Index of the UE j: Index of the carrier t: Slot the calculation relates to rrmDeltaSinr: Delta SINR calculated for UE i and carrier j rrmDeltaSinrStepup: Delta SINR step up applied for CRC ACK feedback (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_3157_replaced([11138188](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krdgnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) ullaDeltaSinrStepdown: Delta SINR stepdown applied for CRC NACK feedback **value defined in 5G_UP_ALG_16024_replaced([11138277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** ullaDeltaSinrMax: Maximum delta SINR **(NIDD parameter) for basic reliability, for high reliabity: ullaDeltaSinrMaxHR** ullaDeltaSinrMin: Minimum delta SINR depending on the active bearer in UL: * For VoNR users with active 5QI1/65/66 bearer for voice service ullaDeltaSinrMin is equal to [Before 05213959] NRCELL.tmpParam8/10 - 50 [05213959] NRCELL.ullaDeltaSinrMinHR [End 05213959] * For users with active high reliability bearer ullaDeltaSinrMin is equal to NRCELL.ullaDeltaSinrMinHR * Otherwise ullaDeltaSinrMin is equal to NRCELL.ullaDeltaSinrMin For VoNR users with active 5QI1/65/66 bearer for voice service, if the UE is SAg capable and NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg is set to Enabled_VoNr, **and SAg Mode is ON (see 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then it is possible that a 2x SAg grant will be used.** If a 2x grant is used, then a DTX is treated as a NACK. The determination of a 2x SAg grant is as defined by 5G_UP_ALG_19882_replaced([11205071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). **Initialization/Re-initialization of rrmDeltaSinr:** When an HR DRB is configured at UE context set-up (NSA or handover case) and it is allowed to apply HR LA immediately (for example: no VoNR DRB configured at the same time, see 5G_UP_ALG_15603_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the complete set of cases), then rrmDeltaSinr is initiated to min(NRCELL.puschHrLaConfig.iniDeltaSinrHR, iniDeltaSinr[j,ue, k=HR]), otherwise rrmDeltaSinr is initialized to iniDeltaSinr[j,ue, k=BR]) at UE context setup. After UE context setup: \- When UE switches high reliability UL LA to basic reliability UL LA (due to fallback or UE release the last HR DRB or VoNR 5QI1/65/66 DRB configured), L2PS shall not re-initialize rrmDeltaSinr**.** \- When UE switches basic reliability UL LA to high reliability UL LA (due to recover or UE established with first HR DRB **or VoNR 5QI1/65/66 DRB released), rrmDeltaSinr is adjusted as follows: rrmDeltaSinr = max(rrmDeltaSinr – StdSinr, NRCELL.puschHrLaConfig.ullaDeltaSinrMinHR).** StdSinr is the long-term standard deviation of UL SINR based on SINR reports from PUSCH or SRS, pelease refer to 5G_UP_ALG_15846_replaced([11130560](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krANk_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**_* If UE works with high reliability UL LA and there is a long time no scheduling defined as R &D parameter rdNoSchedulingExpiryTimeHR(5G_UP_ALG_18192_replaced([11138780](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krhyBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for high reliability LA, UE shall reset rrmDeltaSinr. that is, rrmDeltaSinr = min(NRCELL.puschHrLaConfig.iniDeltaSinrHR, iniDeltaSinr[j,ue]) Please note according to 5G_UP_ALG_15603_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if at least one 5QI1/65/66 DRB is configured, this UE can only works in basic reliability mode. [image-1][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to UL OLLA (Uplink Open Loop Link Adaptation). Here's a breakdown of the flowchart: 1\. _*Start UL OLLA**: The process begins with the "Start UL OLLA" oval. 2\. **Initialization** : The first step is `rrmDeltaSinr[t] = iniDeltaSinr[j.ue]`, which likely initializes a variable `rrmDeltaSinr` at time `t` with an initial value from `iniDeltaSinr`. 3\. **CRC feedback of initial UL Tx** : This is a process step indicated by a parallelogram, representing the reception of CRC feedback for the initial Uplink Transmission. 4\. **DTX detected** : This is a decision point (diamond shape) checking if DTX (Discontinuous Transmission) is detected. 5\. **ACK received** : This is another decision point checking if an ACK (Acknowledgement) is received. 6\. **Conditional Logic based on ACK and DTX** : * If DTX is detected, the flow seems to proceed to the "ACK received" decision. * If ACK is received, the flow proceeds to another decision. 7\. **Scheduled MCS < proposed MCS**: This is a decision point checking if the scheduled MCS (Modulation and Coding Scheme) is less than the proposed MCS. 8\. **Further Conditional Logic** : * If the scheduled MCS is not less than the proposed MCS, the flow proceeds to check if `rrmDeltaSinr[t] > ullaDeltaSinMin`. * If `rrmDeltaSinr[t] > ullaDeltaSinMin`, the next step is `rrmDeltaSinr[t] = rrmDeltaSinr[t-1]`. This suggests that if the current `rrmDeltaSinr` is above a minimum threshold, it remains unchanged from the previous time step. * If `rrmDeltaSinr[t]` is not greater than `ullaDeltaSinMin`, the next step is `rrmDeltaSinr[t] = rrmDeltaSinr[t-1] - dllaDeltaSinStepdown`. This indicates a decrease in `rrmDeltaSinr` by a step-down value. * If the scheduled MCS is less than the proposed MCS, the flow proceeds to check if `rrmDeltaSinr[t] < ullaDeltaSiniMax`. * If `rrmDeltaSinr[t] < ullaDeltaSiniMax`, the next step is `rrmDeltaSinr[t] = rrmDeltaSinr[t-1] + rrmDeltaSinStepup`. This indicates an increase in `rrmDeltaSinr` by a step-up value. The flowchart outlines a dynamic adjustment mechanism for a parameter (likely related to signal-to-noise ratio or SINR) based on transmission feedback (CRC, ACK) and the comparison of scheduled and proposed MCS.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_8283.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] rrmDeltaSinr evaluation by UL OLLA File: Drawings for 5GC000517_5GC001796_ DLLA_and_ULLA.vsdx Folder:

---

**3.8.3.2.0-1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11138025`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation Evaluation of the initial delta SINR

---

**3.8.3.2.0-1.0-1.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `11138085`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation 11138085: Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation It may be assumed that the value of rrmDeltaSinr is for most of the UEs in the cell in the same range and the transient phase until the OLLA reaches the usually applied deltaSinr can be shorten by appropriate selection of the initial rrmDeltaSinr. Hence 5G-L2-PS shall provide per carrier j an exponential filter for evaluating the cell-specific initial rrmDeltaSinr that can be assigned to each UE i for that a context is set up. The exponential filter shall be initialized by 0 when the carrier j is set up and shall be feed with one rrmDeltaSinr sample per UE i that is taken from the OLLA on completion of the UE’s transient phase. It is assumed that the transient phase is completed if n CRC feedbacks are received for the UE i and n is determined by the maximum of 50 and the number of rrmDeltaSinrStepUp (see 5G_UP_ALG_3157_replaced([11138188](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krdgnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) until the border ullaDeltaSinrMax (NIDD parameter) for BR or ullaDeltaSinrMaxHR (NIDD parameter) for HR could be reached starting from rrmDeltaSinr = 0.

Note that 'n', the number of CRC feedbacks is a constant value, see the formula below.

If NIDD parameter NRBTS.actEnhancedLinkAdaptation is set to 1:

The rrmDeltaSinr value of the UE should never feed into the exponential filter of iniDeltaSinr at end of the transient phase if

\- the granted MCS was equal to the highest MCS for Basic Reliability Link adaptation or limited maximum MCS (5G_UP_ALG_17281_replaced([11131699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krD38_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) by NIDD parameters with corresponding RANK for high reliability Link adaptation supported in UL,

\- the proposed number of PRBs was equal to the smallest PRB allocation.

For BR:  
[image-0][image_desc]n = max{50, ullaDeltaSinrMax / rrmDeltaSinrStepUp}[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_8288.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] 

with

rrmDeltaSinrStepup = ullaDeltaSinrStepdown*rrmullaBlerTarget/(1-rrmullaBlerTarget)

where ullaDeltaSinrStepdown and ullaBlerTarget are [Before CB013303-A] NIDD parameters.[CB013303-A], value defined in 5G_UP_ALG_16024_replaced([11138277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB013303-A]

For HR:

[image-1][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_8288.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

with

rrmDeltaSinrStepup = ullaDeltaSinrStepdownHR*ullaBlerTargetHR/(1-ullaBlerTargetHR)

where ullaDeltaSinrStepdownHR and NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.ullaBlerTargetHR are NIDD parameters. [CB013303-A] Refer 5G_UP_ALG_16024_replaced([11138277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB013303-A]

The exponential filter for evaluating the initial DeltaSinr (iniDeltaSinr[j,ue,k]) shall be compliant to

[image-2][image_desc]The image is completely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_8288.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]

with:

i: Index of the UE

j: Index of the carrier

ue: Loop index of the formula, the rrmDeltaSinr sample is taken from a particular UE when UEs are either in HR (High Reliability) mode or BR (Basic Relaibility) mode.

k: Link adaptation for basic reliability or high reliability

rdDeltaSinrFilterConst: 

Filter constant of the exponential filter (R&D parameter, range of 1, 2, …, 10000 with default 20 – see 5G_L2_1167_replaced([11185018](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27u9jPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

**3.8.3.2.0-1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11138090`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation Evaluation of UE specific and slot specific delta SINR

---

**3.8.3.2.0-1.0-1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11138096`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation If the NIDD parameter NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE.actRimMitigationExtend is set to TRUE and RIM UL mitigation extension is triggered (see 5G_UP_ALG_27175_replaced([11163444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktZaQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the gNB-DU shall evaluate the slot specific deltaSinr parameters by two slot specific OLLAs. One of the OLLAs calculates the deltaSinr parameter belonging to the 1st UL slot after S slot and the other OLLA the deltaSinr of the 2nd UL slot after S slot. With the separation of the deltaSinr evaluation per UL slot, the gNB-DU shall assign the CRC feedback of the transmissions to the OLLA, which belongs to the corresponding UL slot. If RIM UL mitigation extension is stopped (see 5G_UP_ALG_27175_replaced([11163444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktZaQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the gNB-DU shall evaluate the UE specific deltaSinr parameters by the UE specific OLLAs. For this purpose, the CRC feedback of all UL transmissions is considered by the UE specific OLLA.

During the time RIM UL mitigation extension is triggered the OLLA belonging to all UL slots shall be set to ‘invalid’. In return, the slot specific OLLAs shall be set to ‘invalid’ if RIM UL mitigation extension isn’t any longer triggered and the OLLA, which belongs to all UL slots shall continue the evaluation of the deltaSinr.

The gNB-DU shall initialize the slot specific OLLAs belonging to the 1st UL slot and the 2nd UL slot by the deltaSinr lastly calculated by the OLLA corresponding to all UL slots before RIM UL mitigation extension is triggered. If RIM UL mitigation extension is no longer triggered (see 5G_UP_ALG_27175_replaced([11163444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktZaQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the necessity to support slot specific OLLAs isn’t any longer given, the gNB-DU shall release the slot specific OLLAs and shall reinitialize the UE specific OLLA, which belongs to all UL slots. [PR781033] The gNB-DU shall distinguish the reinitialization procedure of the UE specific OLLA depending on the classification of the UE (5G_UP_ALG_27198_replaced([11195190](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6iwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If the UE is classified as a cell edge UE, the gNB-DU shall reinitialize the UE specific OLLA by the lastly evaluated slot specific delta SINR of the 2nd UL slot before the RIM UL mitigation extension is stopped (see 5G_UP_ALG_27175_replaced([11163444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktZaQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Conversely, if the UE is not classified as a cell edge UE, [End PR781033] the gNB-DU shall reinitialize the UE specific OLLA by the average of the two slot specific delta SINR parameters, which are lastly evaluated by the slot specific OLLAs before the RIM UL mitigation extension is stopped (see 5G_UP_ALG_27175_replaced([11163444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktZaQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [PR781033] The gNB-DU shall average the delta SINR parameter in dB domain. [End PR781033]

---

#### 3.8.3.2.0-2 Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation Special correction for OLLA offset (ID: 11138103)

**3.8.3.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11138107`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation Expansion of OLLA’s control range for maximum MCS in UL

---

**3.8.3.2.0-2.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11138136`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation The requirement below applies in the same way for BR and HR LA. For HR LA the specific HR OLLA parameters and delta SINR are used.

With the upgrade from 19A release to 19B release and the introduction of the simplified MCS mapping tables the peak throughput of a single UE in UL could be lower. To prevent the gNB from negative impacts on its KPIs with introduction of the 19B release, the OLLA shall allow the reception of a single CRC NACK indication without impairment in link adaptation’s MCS proposing if the link adaptation proposes the maximum MCS for several UL transmissions. The link adaptation shall propose the maximum MCS for basic reliability and for high reliability even if a sporadic CRC NACK indication is received occasionally after a series of CRC ACK indications.

For this purpose, the OLLA shall support an expanded control range of the parameter rrmDeltaSinr[i,j,t] for the maximum MCS only, which is called hereinafter the “MaxMode”. For the unambiguous identification the present controlling range of the OLLA is called the “StdMode”.

If the OLLA receives a CRC ACK indication for the maximum MCS (the unique maximum MCS in MCS table depending on 64QAM/64QAM LowSE MCS table or 256QAM MCS table) the first time, the OLLA shall change the operation mode from “StdMode” to “MaxMode”. With entering the “MaxMode” the OLLA shall capture the just calculated value of rrmDeltaSinr[i,j,t] and shall store the just calculated value in the parameter rrmDeltaSinrMaxMode. Within the “MaxMode” the OLLA shall process CRC ACK indications and shall clip the result of rrmDeltaSinr[i,j,t] such that the value of rrmDeltaSinr[i,j,t] does not exceed the sum of rrmDeltaSinrMaxMode and [Before CB013303-A] NRCELL.ullaDeltaSinrStepdown for basic reliability LA and NRCELL.puschHrLaConfig.ullaDeltaSinrStepdownHR for high reliability LA[CB013303-A] ullaDeltaSinrStepdown defined in 5G_UP_ALG_16024_replaced([11138277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB013303-A] Within the “MaxMode” the OLLA shall process the CRC NACK indication in the same way as in “StdMode”. The OLLA shall stop the “MaxMode” if either the calculated rrmDeltaSinr[i,j,t] becomes less than rrmDeltaSinrMaxMode or rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr[i,j,t] doesn’t allow any longer the proposing of the maximum MCS for full allocation. The check if rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr[i,j,t] allows still the maximum MCS shall be done before a possible MCS downgrade is triggered. The calculated rrmDeltaSinr[i,j,t] must always meet the range restrictions defined by the NIDD parameters ullaDeltaSinrMin and ullaDeltaSinrMax for basic reliability and by the NIDD parameters ullaDeltaSinrMinHR and ullaDeltaSinrMaxHR for high reliability. 

When UE works in high reliability LA mode and NRCELL.puschHrLaConfig.ullaMcsMax64qamRANKxHR for 64QAM table(or NRCELL.puschHrLaConfig.ullaMcsMax64qamLowSERANKxHR for qam64LowSE table, x=1/2, see 5G_UP_ALG_17281_replaced([11131699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krD38_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the used RANK x is lower than maximum MCS (the unique maximum MCS in MCS table depending on 64QAM/64QAM LowSE MCS table or 256QAM MCS table) the OLLA shall stop the “MaxMode”. (Note: this applies to the case that the rank switches while the UE in HR LA mode is in MaxMode, or to the case that a recovery from BR to HR LA takes place while the UE is in MaxMode.) . Furthermore, L2PS shall also set rrmDeltaSinr for high reliability to rrmDeltaSinriMaxMode.

---

**3.8.3.2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11138143`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation Handling of CRC NACK flood in UL OLLA

---

**3.8.3.2.0-2.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `11138177`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation If the UL radio link quality between gNB and UE becomes suddenly worse, the HARQ entity in the gNB may provide a CRC NACK or DTX indication for each received transport block. All these CRC NACK/DTX feedbacks request HARQ retransmissions with high priority in UL scheduling and allocate resources, which can’t be assigned to UEs with initial transmissions.

A PUSCH DTX indication , will not count for CRC Nack flood identification if the DTX is associated with any of the following:

  * 2x or 4x Slot Aggregation



If UE is configured for skippable grants (5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), any PUSCH DTX indication shall be managed per 5G_UP_ALG_16415_replaced([11195380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Aditionally, a CRC NACK/DTX corresponding to a configured grant is not in the scope of the NACK flood algorithm

To overcome the excessive consumption of scheduling resources by UEs with seriously deteriorated air link quality and the resulting interference, the UL LA shall indicate these UEs to the UL scheduler. The indication shall be sent by means of the parameter rrmPauseUeInUlScheduling and shall request the pause in scheduling if at least a configurable sum of consecutive CRC NACK- and DTX- feedbacks related to initial transmissions are received. This sum is defined by the R&D parameter rdMinCrcNackIndUlEnhanced (see 5G_L2_1171_replaced([11185068](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27vklfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the PCell and rdMinCrcNackIndUlEnhancedSCell (see 5G_L2_1172_replaced([11185130](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27wLoPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the SCell if UE is in BR mode in the respective Cell(Pcell or Scell). Any CRC ACK feedback resets the sum as well as the pause indication.

The pause in UL scheduling shall be interrupted by a single UL transmission (initial transmission), which is periodically scheduled for evaluation purposes (see 5G_UP_ALG_4892_replaced([11197752](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwH-JvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The pause shall be reset for SCell after its activation.

In case of high reliability LA, the scheduling shall not be paused. The number of received HARQ NACK or DTX feedback shall be reset after the fallback/switch calculation. The sum for consecutive NACK/DTX considered as a NACK flood for high reliability is defined by R&D parameter rdMinCrcNackIndUlEnhancedHR (5G_L2_15202_replaced([11185072](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27vklvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the PCell and rdMinCrcNackIndUlEnhancedSCellHR (5G_L2_15203_replaced([11185136](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27wLofvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the SCell, which shall lead to fallback metric checking and possible switching/fallbacking from HR to BR according to 5G_UP_ALG_16426_replaced([11138318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in the respective Cell(Pcell or Scell).

Please note that L2PS shall schedule all the DRB(s) and SRB(s) with basic reliability uplink LA when UE switches from high reliability UL LA to basic reliability UL LA due to CRC NACK flood. Please also note that such switch/fallback triggers also a reinitialization of rrmDeltaSinr according to requirement 5G_UP_ALG_11060_replaced([11138025](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krc5hvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Please note that switch or fallback from HR mode to BR (Basic Reliability) mode does not mean triggering reconfiguration on the CP side.

**[PR811432] When NRCELL.actAugPriorityBoost** is enabled, for traffic with request priority anticipatedUlGrantSrb, the sum of consecutive NACK/DTX for NACK flood is defined by R&D parameter rdMinCrcNackIndUlAugSrb (see 5G_L2_23682_replaced([11183290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27iJQ_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [PR851547] The pause in UL scheduling for traffic with request priority anticipatedUlGrantSrb shall be applied only for DL RRC Setup, RRC Re-establishment and RRC Resume procedure. For other SRB1 procedure, traffic with request priority anticipatedYlGrantSrb shall be stopped after maximum UL Harq transmission. [End PR851547] Rationale: For traffic with request priority antcipatedUlGrantSrb is applicable only for SRB traffic. And it is used only to receive the RRC complete message, hence the number of HarqProcess used might not go more than 2 or 3. Also, priority for anticipatedUlGrantSrb is high, need to ensure that this shall not block other UEs getting scheduled, as in the worst case, this grant will be stopped only when the guard timer expires at the C-Plane expires.**_*[End PR811432]_*******

---

#### 3.8.3.2.0-3 Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation OLLA offset step (ID: 11138183)

**3.8.3.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11138188`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation Calculation of the delta SINR step up parameter

---

**3.8.3.2.0-3.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11138255`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation 11138255: Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation The requirement below applies in the same way for BR and HR LA. For HR LA the specific HR OLLA parameters and delta SINR are used. The OLLA shall increase and decrease the delta SINR of an UE in a balanced manner. Balanced manner means in this context that the probability of CRC ACK feedbacks and CRC NACK feedbacks is taken into account for the calculation of the complementary step sizes. The probability for which the CRC NACK feedback and hence the delta SINR step down occurs is compliant to the BLER target that is defined by rrmUllaBlerTarget. Correspondingly, the probability with that the CRC ACK feedback and hence the delta SINR step up occurs is 1 – rrmUllaBlerTarget. Accordingly, the size of the delta SINR step up is calculated by [image-0][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_8310.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] When NRCELL.actUl256Qam is enabled and UE is in 256QAM mode (then UE is scheduled with Table 5.1.3.1-2 for 256QAM) and MCS19<= used MCS<MCS27 and for reserved MCSes, which are applied for an adaptive retransmission of these initial transmissions, if the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) [image-1][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_8310.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] with k: Link adaptation for basic reliability or high reliability rrmDeltaSinrStepup: Delta SINR step up applied for CRC ACK feedback ullaDeltaSinrStepdown: Delta SINR stepdown applied for CRC NACK feedback [Before CB013303-A](defined by NIDD parameter) for basic reliability: NRCELL.ullaDeltaSinrStepdown; for high reilability: NRCELL.puschHrLaConfig.ullaDeltaSinrStepdownHR [CB013303-A], value is determined according to 5G_UP_ALG_16024_replaced([11138277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB013303-A] [Before CB013303-A]k: Link adaptation for basic reliability or high reliability[End CB013303-A] stepUpFactor: factor given by the R&D parameter (rdMcs19StepUpFactor, rdMcs20StepUpFactor ....rdMcs26StepUpFactor - see 5G_L2_3348_replaced([11186070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2725WvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) … 5G_L2_3355_replaced([11186109](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_273gYfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) correspondent to used MCS or to initial MCS when the reserved MCS is used, if the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE). Note: stepUpFactor is used to lower the rrmDeltaSinrStepup for specific MCS in the scope of decreasing the target BLER per MCS. rrmUllaBlerTarget: BLER target for PUSCH transmissions, value is determined according to 5G_UP_ALG_16024_replaced([11138277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.8.3.2.0-4 Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation Special OLLA value for UL enhanced Link Adaptation buffer based MCS downgrade for high reliability (ID: 11138261)

**3.8.3.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11138267`)

Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation When buffer based MCS downgrade occurs for UL data transmission, corresponding CRC ACK shall be skipped in OLLA calcuation, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_11054_replaced([11137899](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcSd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). To prevent the MCS value always be kept in a very small value, If NRCELL.ulMcsDowngradeForHr is set with "enhanced", gNB-DU/L2PS shall accumulate the OLLA steps for CRC-ACK feedbacks corresponding to an UL data initial transmission for high reliability with MCS downgrade for insufficent buffer size into a separate variable (rrmDeltaSinrForMcsDowngrade), otherwise rrmDeltaSinrForMcsDowngrade is set with zero.

rrmDeltaSinrForMcsDowngrade shall be initiialized as 0 when an HR DRB is configured at UE context set-up (NSA or handover case) and it is allowed to apply HR LA immediately (for example: no VoNR DRB configured at the same time, see 5G_UP_ALG_15603_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the complete set of cases) or UE switch from basic reliability UL LA to high relaiblity UL LA.

rrmDeltaSinrForMcsDowngrade shall be initiialized as 0 when a NACK received for the UL data initial transmission regardless of MCS downgrade.

rrmDeltaSinrForMcsDowngrade shall not exceed the threshold rdThresholdForDeltaSinrMcsDowngrade, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_25850_replaced([11138880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kriZH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

L2PS shall skip the rrmDeltaSinrForMcsDowngrade evaluation ACK of CRC feedback for an UL data initial transmission with MCS downgrade for insufficient buffer size if:

1\. If appliedUlBeamFallback == TRUE (5G_UP_ALG_1888_replaced([11111451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the beam probing procedure (see 5G_UP_ALG_2041_replaced([11112416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZr_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or the beam re-probing procedure (see 5G_UP_ALG_2051_replaced([11112493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpWAwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is performed

2\. If the UL ILLA is in regular operation mode (rdActIllaUl set to 1 – see 5G_UP_ALG_9475_replaced([11124506](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqrdcfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the UL ILLA is paused during startup procedure or when no UL transmission is received for rdSinrExpiryTimeHR (see 5G_UP_ALG_16856_replaced([11138651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krgj6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for high reliability.

3\. If appliedUlBeamSweeping == TRUE (5G_UP_ALG_1888_replaced([11111451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the beam sweeping procedure related to PUSCH(see 5G_UP_ALG_2062_replaced([11112532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpWn0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is indicated.

4\. If actInactiveBwpScanning = TRUE and an inactive BWP scanning procedure is ongoing (see 5G_UP_ALG_16104_replaced([11111313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpMPyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

5\. CRC feedback which corresponds to a PUSCH transmission is in basic reliability LA mode and UE works in high reliability LA due to recovery(5G_UP_ALG_16077_replaced([11138358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreusvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

6\. HARQ feedback corresponds to PUSCH transmission during the transient phase of pCSI report reconfiguration (see 5G_UP_ALG_12845_replaced([11159141](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktHtdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

7\. If a ACK is received and Recent Bler estimation defined in 5G_UP_ALG_17217_replaced([11138412](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreuuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is greater than NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA.ullaBlerTargetHR, L2PS shall skip the ACK and rrmDeltaSinr for high reliability shall not be updated. Recent Bler estimated refer to 5G_UP_ALG_17217_replaced([11138412](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreuuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

8\. UE works in "MaxMode" with high reliability LA or UE works with basic reliability LA.

---

### 3.8.3.3 BLER Target

#### 3.8.3.3.0-1 BLER Target Determination of UL BLER target and Delta SINR Step Down (ID: 11138277)

**3.8.3.3.0-1.0-9**  (ID: `13441824`)

BLER Target If UE is in BR (Basic Reliability) mode in UL (5G_UP_ALG_15105_replaced([11125150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_15603_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

ullaDeltaSinrStepdown = NRCELL.ullaDeltaSinrStepdown

if “Limited UL MCS Downgrade” is enabled (see 5G_UP_ALG_6857_replaced([11137777](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krbrYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and an Ack is received for an UL MCS downgrade

if (VoNR capable user with active 5QI-1/65/66 bearer for voice service)

rrmUllaBlerTarget = [Before 05544335-26R3] rdUlLimitedMcsStepupSizeBlerVoNR, (see [13441718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_oMw5ECR4EfGxldjOZQLezQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [05544335-26R3] NRCELL.ulLimitedMcsStepupSizeBlerVoNR [End 05544335-26R3]

else

rrmUllaBlerTarget = NRCELL.ulLimitedMcsStepupSizeBler

else if (VoNR capable user with active 5QI-1/65/66 bearer for voice service) and (NRCELL.actTargetBlerVoNR is set to True),

rrmUllaBlerTarget = NRCELL.ullaTargetBlerVoNR as long as the voice session is active,

else if (UE is configured with at least one DRB with DRB specific target BLER),

if (NRCELL.actDrbTargetBlerForBR is set to TRUE),

rrmUllaBlerTarget = NRBTS/NRDRB/NRPUSCHBRLA.ulDrbBlerTarget 

if more than one DRBs of the UE is configured with DRB specific target BLER, rrmUllaBlerTarget shall be set with DRB BLER target of highest qos priority level. If multiple DRBs of the UE is configured with highest qos priority level value, then rrmUllaBlerTarget shall be set with the smallest ulDrbBlerTarget.

ullaDeltaSinrStepdown = NRBTS/NRDRB/NRPUSCHBRLA.ulDrbDeltaSinrStepdown if configured.

if NRCELL.actAdaptiveUlBlerTarget is set to TRUE and rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr (see 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) < NRCELL.adaptiveUlBlerTarget.ullaBlerTargetSinrThreshold,

rrmUllaBlerTarget = min (rrmUllaBlerTarget, NRCELL.adaptiveUlBlerTarget.ullaBlerTargetLowSinr)

endif

endif

else if NRCELL.actAdaptiveUlBlerTarget is set to TRUE and rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr (see 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) < NRCELL.adaptiveUlBlerTarget.ullaBlerTargetSinrThreshold,

rrmUllaBlerTarget = NRCELL.adaptiveUlBlerTarget.ullaBlerTargetLowSinr

else

rrmUllaBlerTarget = NRCELL.ullaBlerTarget

endif

else if UE is in HR (High Reliability) mode in UL (5G_UP_ALG_15105_replaced([11125150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_15603_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

rrmUllaBlerTarget = NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.ullaBlerTargetHR for High Reliability

If HR DRBs of the UE links to more than 1 NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA, rrmUllaBlerTarget shall be set with the smallest ullaBlerTargetHR. If the smallest ullaBlerTargetHR is same mapped to different profiles, L2PS could select the profile e.g. based on the lowest NRBTS/NRPUSCHHRLA.nrPuschHRLaId as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_15603_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

ullaDeltaSinrStepdown = NRCELL.puschHrLaConfig.ullaDeltaCqiStepdownHR 

endif

Note1: NRCELL.ulLimitedMcsStepupSizeBler can be zero which effectively disables Ack “step up” as per “full UL MCS Downgrade” functionality

Note2: NRCELL.ullaTargetBlerVoNR is used for the VoNR users as long as the voice session is active. By using a lower initial BLER target the scheduler will tend to use a lower MCS when allocating resources for voice packets which can help reduce packet loss in quickly changing RF environments. The reduced BLER buys additional margin in the face of unexpected fading. The OLLA chain shall work for this user with respect to this configured VoNR specific BLER target and shall revert back to generic BLER target given by the NIDD parameter ullaBlerTarget, once the voice session is released. Any other bearers active along with the voice bearer also take the advantage for the conservative BLER target set by the NIDD parameter above.

---

#### 3.8.3.3.0-2 BLER Target Switch or Fallback from high reliability to basic reliability in PUSCH (ID: 11138318)

**3.8.3.3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11138353`)

BLER Target 11138353: BLER Target Due to the change of radio conditions the channel quality is not good to meet the BLER target for HR (High Reliability) mode and thus need switch or fallback from HR mode to BR (Basic Reliability) mode. L2PS shall start a timer with period ulHRBlerOutagePeriod (NIDD NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA.ulHRBlerOutagePeriod) after UE starts high reliability link adaption for PUSCH(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_15603_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UE works with high reliability LA. When the timer expired or if CRC NACK Flood event happens according to 5G_UP_ALG_3158_replaced([11138143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krdglfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) no matter the timer expired [Before CB008246-R] if the number of valid sample of wideband proposed MCS (and thus wide-band TBSize) is greater than rdMinValidSampleForHRBR(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_17796_replaced([11138746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krhyAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), [End CB008246-R] L2PS shall calculate the percentage of wide-band TBSize (related to the previous period of ulHRBlerOutagePeriod seconds, or related to the period till the CRC NACK flood event is received instead of the timer expiration) which is less than or equal to the threshold ulHRBlerOutageTbsThresh (defined by NIDD NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA.ulHRBlerOutageTbsThresh) if UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE table, or ulHRBlerOutageTbsThresh64qam (defined by NIDD NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA.ulHRBlerOutageTbsThresh64qam) if UE is scheduled with 64qam table. The wide-band TBSize is derived from rrmUlProposedMcs (i.e., the proposed MCS provided by UL LA from 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rrmMaxNumPrbUl (i.e., proposed max UL PRB number limitation provided by UL LA from 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) (The calculation can be referenced by 5G_UP_ALG_4856_replaced([11195916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) by way of rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot*8). [Before CB008246-R] * For wide-band TBSize samples L2-PS shall take those calculated by the UL FD-scheduler as the criteria for determining UL HR/BR region. [End CB008246-R] * L2Ps shall monitor the radio link quality in uplink periodically for BLER target by calculating the the percentage below. [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in text. The formula calculates the "Percentage of widebandTBSize within the period". The formula is: Percentage of widebandTBSize within the period = 100 * (total number of valid widebandTBSize < ulHRBlerOutageTbsThresh within the period) / (total number of valid widebandTBSize samples within the period)[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_8323.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] if UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE table [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in text. The formula calculates the "Percentage of widebandTBSize within the period". The formula is: Percentage of widebandTBSize within the period = 100 * (total number of valid widebandTBSize < ulHRBlerOutageTbsThresh64qam within the period) / (total number of valid widebandTBSize samples within the period)[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_8323.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] if UE is scheduler with 64qam table [CB008246-R] During the period, L2PS shall check the valid sample of wideband proposed MCS before RANK limitation(5G_UP_ALG_17280_replaced([11131696](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krD38vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at least one sample every N slots. Please refer to 5G_L2_14633_replaced([11163923](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25p54fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_L2_13222_replaced([11163939](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25p55PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details. Note: this wideband proposed MCS is used special for UE switch between HR and BR. Based on the wideband proposed MCS above the corresponding Wide-band TBSize samples are calculated. [End CB008246-R] -Wide-band TBSize samples/Wideband proposed MCS calculated in transient phase shall be excluded. 1\. -Wide-band TBsize samples for CG-HR (5G_UP_ALG_15603_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be excluded for slots when CG PUSCH is transmitted. 2\. - Wide-band TBSize samples/Wideband proposed MCS for re-transmission transmission shall be excluded. * If this percentage is greater than or equal to ulHRBlerOutagePercentage (NIDD NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA.ulHRBlerOutagePercentage) and high reliability of DRBs of this UE works with high reliability LA, L2PS shall switch/fallback the UE from high reliability UL LA to basic reliability UL LA for PUSCH data scheduling and UE shall be in Transform Precoder Disabled state first before transition to Tansform Precoder Enabled State (5G_UP_ALG_3282_replaced([11140672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krvNZ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). After UE is transisted from Transfor Disabled State to Tranform Enabled State (5G_UP_ALG_3282_replaced([11140672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krvNZ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) the timer with period ulHRBlerOutagePeriod for the UE shall be stopped. L2PS shall also switch/fallback to basic reliability OLC for PDCCH aggregation level selection if dynamic alSelection is used(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4069_replaced([11170363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt-pEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or dciDedicatedAggregationLevel if alSelection is "static" . Otherwise, L2PS shall restart the timer with period ulHRBlerOutagePeriod for the UE if the timer is stopped due to timeout. If a fallback to basic reliability LA is triggered by a NACK flood event as per condition above, the timer shall be stopped. Upon fallback from HR to BR for UL LA triggered by UL PDCCH criteria, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_18296_replaced([11170717](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3PvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), L2PS shall reset the timer with period ulHRBlerRecoverPeriod for PUSCH LA recover precedure. When UE switches from high reliability UL LA to basic reliablity UL LA, L2PS shall schedule all the DRB(s) and SRB(s) with basic reliablity uplink LA. Please note that such switch/fallback triggers also a reinitialization of rrmDeltaSinr according to requirement 5G_UP_ALG_11060_replaced([11138025](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krc5hvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When UE switches from high reliability UL LA to basic reliability UL LA, UE's CG (Configured Grant) shall also be switched from CG-HR mode to CG-BR mode (5G_UP_ALG_15603_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). CG OLLA parameters such as cgOlla, cgSinrOffsetDown, cgSinrOffsetUp, cgMonitoringPeriod, cgNackThreshold as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_15651_replaced([11172853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be reset to the corresponding values for CG-BR mode. The behavior afterwards shall follow the description in 5G_UP_ALG_14664_replaced([11172802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_15651_replaced([11172853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Please note that switch or fallback from HR mode to BR (Basic Reliability) mode does not mean to trigger reconfiguration in CP side.

---

#### 3.8.3.3.0-3 BLER Target Switch or Recover from basic reliability to high reliability in PUSCH (ID: 11138358)

**3.8.3.3.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11138388`)

BLER Target 11138388: BLER Target Due to the change of radio conditions the channel quality may become good to meet the BLER target for HR (High Reliability) mode and thus need switch or recovery from BR (Basic Reliability) mode to HR mode.

L2PS shall start a timer with period ulHRBlerRecoverPeriod (NIDD NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA.ulHRBlerRecoverPeriod) after UE starts high reliability link adaption for PUSCH(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_15603_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) but UE works in mode with basic reliability LA due to fall back (please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_16426_replaced([11138318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UE is in Transform Precoder Disabled state (5G_UP_ALG_3270_replaced([11140467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krt_UPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or after UE is triggered from Transform Precoder Enabled to Transform Precoder Disabled (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_3284_replaced([11140751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krvNbvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). When the timer expired [Before CB008246-R]if the number of valid sample of wideband proposed MCS (and thus wide-band TBSize) is greater than rdMinValidSampleForHRBR(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_17796_replaced([11138746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krhyAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End CB008246-R], L2PS shall calculate the percentage of wide-band TBSize (related to the previous period of ulHRBlerRecoverPeriod seconds) which is greater than or equal to the threshold ulHRBlerRecoverTbsThresh (defined by NIDD NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA.ulHRBlerRecoverTbsThresh parameter) if UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE table, or ulHRBlerRecoverTbsThresh64qam (defined by NIDD NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA.ulHRBlerRecoverTbsThresh64qam) if UE is scheduled with 64qam table. The wide-band TBSize is derived from rrmUlProposedMcs (i.e., the proposed MCS provided by UL LA from 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rrmMaxNumPrbUl (i.e., proposed max UL PRB number limitation provided by UL LA from 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) (The calculation can be referenced by 5G_UP_ALG_4856_replaced([11195916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) by way of rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot*8).

  * L2Ps shall monitor the radio link quality in uplink periodically for BLER target by calculating the percentage below.



[image-0][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_8326.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

if UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE table

[image-1][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_8326.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

if UE is scheduler with 64qam table

[CB008246-R]

During the period, L2PS shall check the valid sample of wideband proposed MCS before RANK limitation(5G_UP_ALG_17280_replaced([11131696](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krD38vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at least one sample every N slots. Please refer to 5G_L2_14633_replaced([11163923](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25p54fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_L2_13222_replaced([11163939](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25p55PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

Note: this wideband proposed MCS is used special for UE switch between HR and BR. Based on the wideband proposed MCS above the corresponding Wide-band TBSize samples are calculated.

[End CB008246-R]

-Wide-band TBSize samples/Wideband proposed MCS calculated in transient phase shall be excluded.

-Wide-band TBsize samples for CG-BR (5G_UP_ALG_15603_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be excluded for slots when CG PUSCH is transmitted.

  * If this percentage is greater than or equal to ulHRBlerRecoverPercentage (NIDD NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA.ulHRBlerRecoverPercentage) and the UE scheduling in UL follows the BR LA due to 5G_UP_ALG_16426_replaced([11138318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and (recover condition from basic reliability OLC to high reliability OLC is also meet(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_17138_replaced([11170742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if dynamic alSelection is used) and UE is not in Pause shceduling status due to CRC NACK flood in 5G_UP_ALG_3158_replaced([11138143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krdglfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), L2PS shall switch/recover the UE from basic reliability UL LA to high reliability UL LA for PUSCH data scheduling. L2PS shall also switch to high reliabilty OLC for PDCCH aggregation level selection if dynamic alSelection is used(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4069_replaced([11170363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt-pEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or dciDedicatedAggregationLevelHR if alSelection is "static" . Otherwise, L2PS shall restart the timer with period ulHRBlerRecoverPeriod.



When UE is in "Transform Precoder enable state" as defined by 5G_UP_ALG_3270_replaced([11140467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krt_UPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in L2PS shall not switch/recover the UE from basic reliability UL LA to high reliability UL LA for PUSCH data scheduling.

If scheduling pausing triggered by NACK flood(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_3158_replaced([11138143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krdglfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is end, L2PS shall restart the timer for recover with ulHRBlerRecoverPeriod.

When UE switches from basic reliability UL LA to high reliablity UL LA, L2PS shall schedule all the DRB(s) and SRB(s) with high reliablity uplink LA. Please note that such switch/recover triggers also a reinitialization of rrmDeltaSinr according to requirement 5G_UP_ALG_11060_replaced([11138025](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krc5hvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When UE switches from basic reliability UL LA to high reliability UL LA, UE's CG (Configured Grant) shall also be switched from CG-BR mode to CG-HR mode (5G_UP_ALG_15603_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). CG OLLA parameters such as cgOlla, cgSinrOffsetDown, cgSinrOffsetUp, cgMonitoringPeriod, cgNackThreshold as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_15651_replaced([11172853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be reset to the corresponding values for CG-HR mode. The behavior afterwards shall follow the description in 5G_UP_ALG_14664_replaced([11172802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_15651_replaced([11172853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Please note that switch or recover from BR mode to HR (Basic Reliability) mode does not mean to trigger reconfiguration in CP side.

---

#### 3.8.3.3.0-4 BLER Target Skip condition for switching from basic reliability Link adaptation to high reliability Link adaptation (ID: 11138394)

**3.8.3.3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11138407`)

BLER Target In below condition, L2PS shalll not start timer with period mentioined in 5G_UP_ALG_16077_replaced([11138358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreusvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the UE to switch from basic reliability Link adaptation to high reliability Link adaptation:

1\. UE is configured with configured grant and mcs-table in configuredGrantConfig is set to 'qam256'

2\. UL of UE works with DFT-s-OFDM mode

  1. At least one [Before CB008118-CB] 5QI-1 [CB008118-CB] 5QI-1/65/66[End CB008118-CB] DRB is configured.

---

#### 3.8.3.3.0-5 BLER Target Estimate the Recent BLER for High reliability UL LA (ID: 11138412)

**3.8.3.3.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11138420`)

BLER Target 11138420: BLER Target Recent BLER shall be estimated when UE works with high reliability downlink adaptation.

Conditional UL OLLA based recent BLER estimation, OLLA delta won’t increase until BLER achieving BLER Target.

To estimate the recent BLER:

[image-0][image_desc]The image is completely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_8331.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

• BlerRecent Recent BLER estimation for the UL HR LA. Initial value of recent BLER is the setting of NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA.ullaBlerTargetHR. if HR DRBs of the UE links to more than 1 NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA, rrmUllaBlerTarget shall be set with the smallest ullaBlerTargetHR.

• forgetfactor Forget factor. forgetFactor shall be set as NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA.ullaBlerTargetHR. if HR DRBs of the UE links to more than 1 NRBTS/PUSCHHRLA, rrmUllaBlerTarget shall be set with the smallest ullaBlerTargetHR.

• t t is an index for sample(ACK/NACK) number

• newSample newSample is the ACK/NACK/DTX of PUSCH HARQ which used MCS was from HR PUSCH LA, 0 for ACK and 1 for NACK. [PR724999]newSample shall consider initial transmission only.[End PR724999] DTX shall be interpreted as a NACK in the recent BLER estimation if R&D parameter rdUseDtxInUlHrOlla is set to TRUE (Please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_11058_replaced([11137940](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcSfvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [PR731211, CB008246-J] Recent BLER calculation is skipped for HARQ feedback corresponds to a Configured Grant HARQ process when UE is in HR mode. (Rationale: This is the same with delta SINR evaluation skip for HARQ feedback corresponds to a Configured Grant HARQ process (5G_UP_ALG_11054_replaced([11137899](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcSd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).[End PR731211, CB008246-J]

[PR731211, CB008246-I]

If DTX is indicated in UlData_PuschReceiveRespHarqU (regardless of interpreted as NACK or not) for UE in HR mode and one of the following:

  * proactive grant is not skippable (5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rdUseDtxInUlHrOlla = FALSE (see 5G_L2_10057_replaced([11185274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27wyufvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  * If UE is configured for skippable grants (5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and PUSCH DTX indication is received for any




grant type as per list in 5G_UP_ALG_16415_replaced([11195380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

then the recent BLER calculation is skipped.

Rationale: This rationale about recent BLER calculation skip regarding proactive UL scheduling is that UE might have no data to be sent in the uplink which is the same with proactive UL scheduling specific part within delta SINR evaluation skip defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11054_replaced([11137899](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcSd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End PR731211, CB008246-I]

If UE first enters high reliability work mode, initial value of recent BLER is the setting of ullaBlerTargetHR, otherwise Recent BLER shall be initialled as saved Recent BLER when UE switch from HR to BR.

---

### 3.8.4.0-16 R&amp;D Parameters rdDeltaAvgSINRmMimoRTXPowerSaving (ID: 11138607)

**3.8.4.0-16.0-1**  (ID: `11138612`)

R&D Parameters Defines the delta SINR caused by half of TRXs muted for Massive MIMO power saving

Value range: [PR740267] -10.0, …, 0.0

Default value: -3.0 [End PR740267]

Unit: N/A

### 3.8.4.0-25 R&amp;D Parameters rdEarlyDllaStepDownHR (ID: 11138701)

**3.8.4.0-25.0-1**  (ID: `11138705`)

R&D Parameters It represents the Size of the delta CQI step down in OLLA for a NACK when waiting for the first valid CQI when UE DL LA works in high reliability mode.

It is recommended to use a value greater than the NIDD parameter dllaDeltaCqiStepDownHR to allow a fast MCS adaptation when waiting for the first valid CQI.

A value smaller than the NIDD parameter dllaDeltaCqiStepDown is disregarded and replaced by the NIDD value itself.

Value range: 0.001..3.000 step 0.001

default value: 1.500

unit: NA

online modifiable: Yes

specified in: 5G_UP_ALG_14429_replaced([11125519](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_3063_replaced([11129500](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq8jMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

### 3.8.4.0-50 R&amp;D Parameters rdDlPRBMcsSelection (ID: 11138974)

**3.8.4.0-50.0-2**  (ID: `11138983`)

R&D Parameters rdDlPRBMcsSelection

Short description: Define the number of PRB used as single column PRB for whether to activate or deactivate PRB-independent CQI-to-MCS mapping table for DL MCS Selection

Value range: 2, ..., 273

[Before CNI-131244]

Default value: 8

[CNI-131244]

Default value: 2

[End CNI-131244]

Unit: NA

Online modifiable: No

Step Size: 1

Please note if this R&D parameter is changed then one common value shall be used, i.e, it shall exist in all DL mapping table. Changing value from default value to other value is used for debugging purpose.

---

### 3.8.4.0-51 R&amp;D Parameters rdUlPRBMcsSelection (ID: 11138989)

Value range: [Before CB013204-A]2, ..., 273 [CB013204-A]1, ..., 273 [End CB013204-A]

Default value: 2

Unit: NA

Online modifiable: No

Step Size: 1

Please note if this R&D parameter is changed then one common value shall be used, i.e, it shall exist in all UL mapping table. Changing value from default value to other value is used for debugging purpose.

R&D Parameters It indicate whether optimized PUSCH PRB allocation is used when ATB is triggered. When it set to [Before 05409086] true [05409086] 1 or 2 [End 05409086], PUSCH PRB could be smaller than 4 during ATB (ATB enhancement).

Value range: [Before 05409086] true, false [05409086] 0(false), 1(Enable ATB enhancement for both SA and NSA UEs), 2(Enable ATB enhancement for SA UEs)[End 05409086]

Default value:[Before 05409086] [Before CB013204-A-UPD003] false [CB013204-A-UPD003] true [End CB013204-A-UPD003] [05409086] 2 [End 05409086]
